basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Baqara — The Cow — is the longest sūra of the Qur'an: 286 Medinan verses that form a compendium of the whole Law and the whole path. It opens by distinguishing hearts before the Book — those who are God-conscious, those who deny, those who dissemble — and unfolds the story of Ādam, the Children of Israel, and Ibrāhīm raising the House, through to the great ordinances of prayer, fasting, pilgrimage, and almsgiving. At its heart shines the Throne Verse (āyat al-kursī, v.255), where Qushayrī contemplates the Living, the Self-Subsisting, whose knowledge encompasses all while nothing encompasses Him. It closes on the final two verses, sent down from a treasury beneath the Throne, and on the words in which the believer lays down his whole burden: God charges no soul beyond its capacity — and whoever entrusts himself to Him discovers that the burden was, all along, carried by Another.
themes: the three postures of the heart before the Book
Ādam, the Children of Israel, and the memory of grace
Ibrāhīm and the founding of the House
the ordinances: prayer, fasting, pilgrimage, almsgiving

versets.ar: الم
versets.tr: alif lām mīm
versets.trad: Alif. Lām. Mīm.
versets.commentaire: These disjointed letters that open the sūra belong to the order of ambiguous verses whose full interpretation, it is said, none possesses but God: to every Book its secret, and the secret of God in the Qurʾān is these isolated letters. Qushayrī also reads them as keys to the divine Names: the alif points to "Allāh," the lām to "the Subtle" (al-Laṭīf), the mīm to "the Glorious" (al-Majīd) and "the King" (al-Malik). And he meditates upon the solitude of the alif, the one letter that joins to no other in the script: contemplating this stroke, the servant awakens to the need of all creation for Him, and to His independence from all. As the alif stands upright and alone before the line, so too the soul devotes itself to God alone, never swerving from its upright standing between His hands.

mots.def: three opening letters; according to Qushayrī, keys to the divine Names.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ الْكِتَابُ لَا رَيْبَ ۛ فِيهِ ۛ هُدًى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: dhālika l-kitābu lā rayba fīhi hudan li-l-muttaqīn
versets.trad: This is the Book! In it there is no doubt. It is a guidance for those who are mindful of God,
versets.commentaire: "This Book": Qushayrī also hears in it the prior decree, all that God has inscribed of His mercy and His solicitude for His beloved ones — faith, gnosis, love, and beautiful conduct — without any doubt. And he adds that the book of the beloved is precious in the eyes of the beloved, above all in the absence of the encounter: it is their consolation, their intimacy, their healing, and their very breath. As for the guidance of the God-fearing (al-muttaqūn), it is clarity and proof, light and pathway, for the one whom the True has preserved from the darkness of ignorance and elected through the realities of union; and the truly God-fearing is the one who guards himself even from gazing upon his own piety, who never leans upon it, and sees his deliverance only in the sheer grace of his Master.

mots.def: demonstrative of distance marking majesty.
rayb: doubt, suspicion.

mots.racine: ه د ي / و ق ي
mots.def: muttaqī: one who guards himself through reverential awe.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْغَيْبِ وَيُقِيمُونَ الصَّلَاةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna yuʾminūna bi-l-ghaybi wa-yuqīmūna ṣ-ṣalāta wa-mimmā razaqnāhum yunfiqūn
versets.trad: those who believe in the Unseen, perform the prayer, and spend [in charity] a portion of what We have granted them,
versets.commentaire: The reality of faith, says Qushayrī, is assent, then its verification, and the secret of both is the grace that makes them possible. To believe in the Unseen (al-ghayb) is to hold fast to what reason cannot reach by compulsion: only he believes in the Unseen who bears within him the lamp of the Unseen. Of prayer he distinguishes the outward fulfilment of its pillars from the absence from oneself through the vision of the One for whom one prays: the prayer of the common folk recalls the heart to the awareness of duty, but the elect return to it from the realities of union — their faces turned toward the qibla, their hearts submerged in nearness. And the spending of the people of Truth is not only of their goods but of their states: they spend their souls in the duties of servanthood and their hearts in the unbroken contemplation of Lordship.

mots.racine: أ م ن / غ ي ب
mots.def: al-ghayb: the invisible, the hidden.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و
mots.def: iqāma: the upright fulfilment of prayer.

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ن ف ق
mots.sens: they spend from what We have granted them
mots.def: rizq: bestowed sustenance.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمَا أُنزِلَ مِن قَبْلِكَ وَبِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yuʾminūna bi-mā unzila ilayka wa-mā unzila min qablika wa-bi-l-ākhirati hum yūqinūn
versets.trad: those who believe in what has been revealed to you and in what was revealed before you, and who hold the life to come in certainty.
versets.commentaire: Their faith in the Unseen, says Qushayrī, necessarily entails their faith in the Qurʾān and in the Books revealed before it; for to attest the truthfulness of the Intermediary — upon him be peace — in one part of what he proclaims obliges one to attest it in all. And if they hold the life to come in certainty (yaqīn), it is because they have already borne witness to it in the invisible. He recalls the word of ʿĀmir ibn ʿAbd al-Qays: "Were the veil lifted, I would not have one grain more of certainty." For the truth of certainty is release from the waverings of conjecture and the dismissal of all that suspicion holds possible.

mots.sens: in what has been revealed to you
mots.def: unzila: was sent down.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ق ب ل
mots.sens: and in what was revealed before you
mots.def: the earlier Scriptures.

mots.racine: أ خ ر / ي ق ن
mots.sens: and of the hereafter they are certain
mots.def: yaqīn: certainty free of doubt.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ عَلَىٰ هُدًى مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ ۖ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika ʿalā hudan min rabbihim wa-ulāʾika humu l-mufliḥūn
versets.trad: Such are they who follow a Guidance from their Lord, and such are they who are the truly blessed.
versets.commentaire: "Upon a guidance from their Lord": this, says Qushayrī, is clarity, certainty, unveiling, and verification, for He disclosed Himself to their hearts first through His signs, then through His attributes, and at last through His reality and His essence. As for felicity (al-falāḥ), it is triumph in the object of desire, victory in the quest: these have obtained abiding in the place of the encounter and have overcome their enemies — the promptings of the soul and the troubled thoughts of the heart — standing by the True for the True, without any intermediary of reason or return to memory and reflection.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ر ب ب
mots.sens: upon a guidance from their Lord
mots.def: hudā: right direction.
mufliḥ: one who succeeds, prospers, triumphs.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا سَوَاءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ أَأَنذَرْتَهُمْ أَمْ لَمْ تُنذِرْهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū sawāʾun ʿalayhim a-andhartahum am lam tundhirhum lā yuʾminūn
versets.trad: As for those who disbelieve, it is all one to them whether you warn them or do not warn them; they will not believe.
versets.commentaire: He whose very description is a veil, cut off from the contemplation of his True, holds equal within himself the word that turns him toward God and the word that helps him gather his portion; indeed, he leans toward the calls of heedlessness and lends them an avid ear. Why should he do otherwise? He is sealed with the mark of separation, imprisoned in the dungeon of absence, barred from the place of nearness. It is a decree sealed beforehand by God, a word already cut: power does not contradict itself, and whoever disputes the True in His ruling is struck down by the assaults of Majesty. And whoever has strayed from the vision of the grace laid upon him in the eternal portion imagines that all proceeds from his own movements: he leans upon his works and is blind to His bounties.

mots.def: kafara: to deny, to veil the truth.
sawāʾ: indifferent, equivalent.

mots.sens: whether you warn them or not
mots.def: andhara: to warn of a peril.

versets.ar: خَتَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ وَعَلَىٰ سَمْعِهِمْ ۖ وَعَلَىٰ أَبْصَارِهِمْ غِشَاوَةٌ ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: khatama llāhu ʿalā qulūbihim wa-ʿalā samʿihim wa-ʿalā abṣārihim ghishāwatun wa-lahum ʿadhābun ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: God has set a seal upon their hearts and upon their hearing, and over their eyes a veil. A terrible chastisement awaits them.
themes: the veil and the great chastisement
versets.commentaire: The seal, says Qushayrī, keeps what is outside from entering and what is within from going forth: thus the True has decreed that there shall not depart from the hearts of His enemies the ignorance and error that dwell in them, and that no insight nor guidance shall enter therein. Upon the hearing of their hearts has settled the veil of abandonment, closing those ears to the perception of the call of the True. And he distinguishes with subtlety: among the elect too, the preoccupations of the sciences and the agitation of questions sometimes occupy the heart and prevent it from receiving without intermediary the secrets of the True — but this belongs to the elect of the elect. The great chastisement, lastly, is their illusion of holding something, when they are in trial: in this world separation, in the next the burning.

mots.def: khatama: to set a seal, to close.

mots.racine: ق ل ب / س م ع
mots.sens: upon their hearts and their hearing
mots.def: qalb: heart; samʿ: hearing.

mots.racine: ب ص ر / غ ش و
mots.def: ghishāwa: veil, covering.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ع ظ م
mots.def: ʿaẓīm: immense, terrible.

versets.ar: وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَن يَقُولُ آمَنَّا بِاللَّهِ وَبِالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَمَا هُم بِمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mina n-nāsi man yaqūlu āmannā bi-llāhi wa-bi-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-mā hum bi-muʾminīn
versets.trad: Among the people are some who say, "We believe in God and in the Last Day," yet they are not believers.
versets.commentaire: The hypocrites persisted in their duplicity and went on deceiving the believers; so God rent their veils with this word: "they are not believers." Qushayrī cites the verse: "Whoever adorns himself with what he is not, trial lays bare his pretensions." For, he says, when their words were emptied of all meaning, the harm they reaped from them surpassed the profit they imagined. And he concludes: when the sincerity of states failed them, the sincerity of words availed them nothing; whoever shows of himself what he has not realized is unmasked on the spot before the people of Realization.

mots.racine: ن و س / ق و ل
mots.sens: among the people are some who say
mots.def: al-nās: humankind, people.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ي و م / أ خ ر
mots.sens: we believe in God and in the Last Day
mots.def: al-yawm al-ākhir: the Last Day.
muʾmin: true believer.

versets.ar: يُخَادِعُونَ اللَّهَ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَمَا يَخْدَعُونَ إِلَّا أَنفُسَهُمْ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: yukhādiʿūna llāha wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-mā yakhdaʿūna illā anfusahum wa-mā yashʿurūn
versets.trad: They seek to deceive God and those who believe, but they deceive only themselves, without being aware of it.
versets.commentaire: The harm of their ruse, says Qushayrī, and its chastisement return upon themselves, so that in truth they have deceived none but themselves: they have lowered none but their own rank, lightened none but themselves, and no other has tasted the bitterness of their act. And he yields the pearl of the sūra: whoever forgets the grace that preceded him and says "my Lord, by me, from me, I" — that one falls into the illusion of believing that all is "to You, by You, from You, You." This is the harshest of chastisements: he sees a mirage and takes it for water; but when he comes to it he finds nothing — and finds God beside him, calling him to account.

mots.def: khādaʿa: to use guile, to dupe.

mots.racine: خ د ع / ن ف س
mots.def: nafs: soul, self.
shaʿara: to perceive, to sense.

versets.ar: فِي قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ فَزَادَهُمُ اللَّهُ مَرَضًا ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْذِبُونَ
versets.tr: fī qulūbihim maraḍun fa-zādahumu llāhu maraḍan wa-lahum ʿadhābun alīmun bi-mā kānū yakdhibūn
versets.trad: In their hearts is a sickness, and God increases that sickness. A painful chastisement shall be theirs for their lying.
versets.commentaire: In the hearts of the hypocrites, says Qushayrī, is the sickness of doubt, and God increases it through the illusion they nurse of having saved themselves by deceiving the believers. And he transposes: he who mingles his intention with his portion, who mixes his will with his passion, advances one foot in desire and draws back the other through craving — he is neither a sincere aspirant nor a steadfast sage. For, he adds, were the aspirant sincere in his desire, his heart would reach the realities of union; but whoever has corrupted his worship is held back from the degrees of the Garden, and whoever has corrupted his will is held back from the intimacies of nearness. The painful chastisement, lastly, is the regret on the Day of Unveiling, when they shall see how their fellows, the sincere, have arrived, and how they themselves were lost.

mots.racine: ق ل ب / م ر ض
mots.def: maraḍ: sickness, here doubt.
zāda: to increase.

mots.sens: for that they used to lie
mots.def: kadhaba: to lie.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ لَا تُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ قَالُوا إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُصْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā qīla lahum lā tufsidū fī l-arḍi qālū innamā naḥnu muṣliḥūn
versets.trad: When it is said to them, "Do not sow corruption upon the earth," they reply, "We are only reformers!"
versets.commentaire: The allusion, says Qushayrī, is that when an inward warner, from the depths of their hidden thoughts, summons them to what would set them aright, they pursue the easy paths of interpretation and disguise from their own eyes what attests the hardness of their hearts. And when they denied the proof of the True springing from the thoughts of their heart, God removed the blessing from their states, gave them deafness to the truth in its place, and tried them with objection against the Way. As the apostate is the fiercest of enemies, he who turns from desire back to the world and to habit becomes the most ferocious gainsayer of this Way and the most distant from its people.

mots.racine: ف س د / أ ر ض
mots.sens: do not sow corruption upon the earth
mots.def: afsada: to corrupt, to lay waste.
muṣliḥ: one who amends, reforms.

versets.ar: أَلَا إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ الْمُفْسِدُونَ وَلَٰكِن لَّا يَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: alā innahum humu l-mufsidūna wa-lākin lā yashʿurūn
versets.trad: O truly, they are the corrupters, but they do not perceive it.
versets.commentaire: It is enough, says Qushayrī, for the man of falsehood to be confounded: that one say to his face, "you have lied." When they said, "we are only reformers," the True belied them, proclaiming: "They are the corrupters, but they do not perceive it" — that is: We know them and We unmask them. Their very unawareness is part of their chastisement, for they sow disorder while believing themselves artisans of order.

mots.def: mufsid: one who corrupts.
shaʿara: to perceive.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ آمِنُوا كَمَا آمَنَ النَّاسُ قَالُوا أَنُؤْمِنُ كَمَا آمَنَ السُّفَهَاءُ ۗ أَلَا إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ السُّفَهَاءُ وَلَٰكِن لَّا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā qīla lahum āminū kamā āmana n-nāsu qālū a-nuʾminu kamā āmana s-sufahāʾu alā innahum humu s-sufahāʾu wa-lākin lā yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: When it is said to them, "Believe as the people have believed," they reply, "Shall we believe as the fools believe?" O truly, they are the fools, but they do not know it.
versets.commentaire: The hypocrites, says Qushayrī, when summoned to the call of the True, branded the believers as fools; just as the people of wealth, when invited to forsake the world, charge the people of uprightness with sloth and incapacity. But the fools, in truth, are they themselves: they have raised palaces and inhabited tombs, adorned the cradle and are already folded in the shroud, run in the field of heedlessness and stumbled in the valleys of regret. And he seals it: soon they shall know — but when their knowing avails them nothing.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ن و س
mots.sens: believe as the people have believed
mots.def: al-nās: people, here the believers.
sufahāʾ: fools, the witless.

mots.racine: س ف ه / ع ل م
mots.sens: they are the fools, but they do not know
mots.def: safah: folly, witlessness.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا لَقُوا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قَالُوا آمَنَّا وَإِذَا خَلَوْا إِلَىٰ شَيَاطِينِهِمْ قَالُوا إِنَّا مَعَكُمْ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُسْتَهْزِئُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā laqū lladhīna āmanū qālū āmannā wa-idhā khalaw ilā shayāṭīnihim qālū innā maʿakum innamā naḥnu mustahziʾūn
versets.trad: Whenever they meet the believers, they say, "We believe!" But when they withdraw to their devils, they say, "Indeed, we are with you; we were only mocking!"
versets.commentaire: The hypocrites, says Qushayrī, wished to join the company of the unbelievers to that of the believers: before the believers they said "we are with you," and alone with their own kind they flaunted their complicity. But God declared them "tossed between the two, neither toward these nor toward those." And he transposes: whoever wishes to join the path of desire to the path of habit does not succeed, for the two contraries cannot be reconciled; "the freedman remains a slave so long as a single dirham of his ransom remains to be paid." A heart divided among all camps never knows peace nor true sustenance.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / أ م ن
mots.def: laqiya: to meet.

mots.racine: خ ل و / ش ط ن
mots.def: shayāṭīn: devils, henchmen.
istahzaʾa: to mock.

versets.ar: اللَّهُ يَسْتَهْزِئُ بِهِمْ وَيَمُدُّهُمْ فِي طُغْيَانِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ
versets.tr: allāhu yastahziʾu bihim wa-yamudduhum fī ṭughyānihim yaʿmahūn
versets.trad: It is God who mocks them and prolongs them in their rebellion, while they wander blindly.
versets.commentaire: When the hypocrites said "we are only mocking," says Qushayrī, God replied: "It is God who mocks them" — that is: He requites them for their mockery. Likewise, those who cast the reins of their being into the hands of the passions were swept by them into the valleys of dispersion; no foot found a fixed dwelling for them, and they strayed in the deserts of absence. And he warns: to prolong them in their straying, to make their vile deeds fair in their own eyes, is the harshest of chastisements — and their contentment with this state is their greatest misfortune.

mots.def: istahzaʾa: here, to requite mockery.

mots.racine: م د د / ط غ ي
mots.sens: and He prolongs them in their rebellion
mots.def: ṭughyān: excess, rebellion.
ʿamiha: to wander as one blind.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ اشْتَرَوُا الضَّلَالَةَ بِالْهُدَىٰ فَمَا رَبِحَت تِّجَارَتُهُمْ وَمَا كَانُوا مُهْتَدِينَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna shtarawu ḍ-ḍalālata bi-l-hudā fa-mā rabiḥat tijāratuhum wa-mā kānū muhtadīn
versets.trad: These are they who have bartered error for guidance. Their trade has brought no gain, and they are not rightly guided.
versets.commentaire: The allusion, says Qushayrī, is that whoever remains far from the divine rights by lingering in the abodes of his portions has lost his trade; and whoever prefers the world, or even the hereafter, to the True — exalted is He — is in a still graver loss. For, he adds, the most afflicted is not he who misses the delights, but he who is stricken by remoteness from the intimate converse, whose heart has turned from its Master and who remains captive to the passions: no message reaches his heart, no access opens to his spirit. And he seals it: whoever has missed a moment has missed his Lord, for moments have neither replacement nor substitute.

mots.racine: ش ر ي / ض ل ل / ه د ي
mots.def: ishtarā: to buy, to barter; ḍalāla: error.

mots.racine: ر ب ح / ت ج ر
mots.def: tijāra: commerce, trade.

mots.sens: and they were not rightly guided
mots.def: muhtadī: rightly guided.

versets.ar: مَثَلُهُمْ كَمَثَلِ الَّذِي اسْتَوْقَدَ نَارًا فَلَمَّا أَضَاءَتْ مَا حَوْلَهُ ذَهَبَ اللَّهُ بِنُورِهِمْ وَتَرَكَهُمْ فِي ظُلُمَاتٍ لَّا يُبْصِرُونَ
versets.tr: mathaluhum ka-mathali lladhī stawqada nāran fa-lammā aḍāʾat mā ḥawlahu dhahaba llāhu bi-nūrihim wa-tarakahum fī ẓulumātin lā yubṣirūn
versets.trad: They are like one who kindled a fire: when it had lit up all around him, God took away their light and left them in darkness, unable to see;
versets.commentaire: Behold, says Qushayrī, one who kindled a fire at the beginning of his night, then the flames died and he was left in darkness: thus the hypocrite tasted some respite in this world through his outward show, then was tried in the next by chastisement. And from this he draws the most piercing allusion: one who has a fair beginning, who sets out upon the path of desire, toils for a time, endures trial upon trial — then returns to the world before attaining Reality, and reverts to the darkness of his former state. His branch turned green and then bore no fruit, his bough flowered and then he gathered none of its fruits; and the hand of constraint thrust him back after the tongue of grace had drawn him near.

mots.racine: و ق د / ن و ر
mots.def: istawqada: to set fire ablaze.

mots.racine: ض و ء / ح و ل
mots.sens: it lit up all around him
mots.def: aḍāʾa: to illuminate.

mots.racine: ذ ه ب / ن و ر
mots.def: nūr: light.

mots.racine: ت ر ك / ظ ل م
mots.def: ẓulumāt: darknesses.

versets.ar: صُمٌّ بُكْمٌ عُمْيٌ فَهُمْ لَا يَرْجِعُونَ
versets.tr: ṣummun bukmun ʿumyun fa-hum lā yarjiʿūn
versets.trad: deaf, dumb, blind — they do not return [to God].
themes: the three infirmities of the heart
versets.commentaire: Deaf, says Qushayrī, to the calls of the True through the ears of their heart; dumb of any intimate converse with the True through the tongue of their secret; blind to the contemplation of the unfolding of the decrees through the eyes of their inner sight. Therefore they do not return from their drifting, nor let themselves be turned from their surrender to error. And he says it again otherwise: deaf to hearing through the True, dumb to speaking through the True, blind to beholding creation through the True — for no decree of renunciation went before them.

mots.racine: ص م م / ب ك م / ع م ي
mots.def: ṣumm: deaf; bukm: dumb; ʿumy: blind.
rajaʿa: to return, to turn back.

versets.ar: أَوْ كَصَيِّبٍ مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ فِيهِ ظُلُمَاتٌ وَرَعْدٌ وَبَرْقٌ يَجْعَلُونَ أَصَابِعَهُمْ فِي آذَانِهِم مِّنَ الصَّوَاعِقِ حَذَرَ الْمَوْتِ ۚ وَاللَّهُ مُحِيطٌ بِالْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: aw ka-ṣayyibin mina s-samāʾi fīhi ẓulumātun wa-raʿdun wa-barqun yajʿalūna aṣābiʿahum fī ādhānihim mina ṣ-ṣawāʿiqi ḥadhara l-mawti wa-llāhu muḥīṭun bi-l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: Or they are like those caught beneath a rain-laden cloud from the sky, full of darkness, thunder, and lightning. They thrust their fingers into their ears against the thunderbolts, in dread of death. But God encompasses the disbelievers on every side.
versets.commentaire: The Qurʾān, says Qushayrī, is likened to a rain that descends from the sky; its promises and threats to the thunder and lightning of the downpour; and their flight to the terror that seizes them at the crash of thunder. Likewise the people of heedlessness: when the exhortation of the preachers strikes their ears, or the lights of felicity glimmer to their hearts, were they to renounce their torpor they would be happy — but they cling to their lying hopes, persist in their spoilt way, and hide behind vain pretexts.

mots.racine: ص و ب / س م و
mots.sens: like a downpour from the sky
mots.def: ṣayyib: beating rain.

mots.racine: ر ع د / ب ر ق
mots.def: raʿd: thunder; barq: lightning.

mots.racine: ح ذ ر / م و ت
mots.def: ḥadhar: caution, dread.

mots.racine: ح و ط / ك ف ر
mots.def: muḥīṭ: that which surrounds, encompasses.

versets.ar: يَكَادُ الْبَرْقُ يَخْطَفُ أَبْصَارَهُمْ ۖ كُلَّمَا أَضَاءَ لَهُم مَّشَوْا فِيهِ وَإِذَا أَظْلَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ قَامُوا ۚ وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَذَهَبَ بِسَمْعِهِمْ وَأَبْصَارِهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: yakādu l-barqu yakhṭafu abṣārahum kullamā aḍāʾa lahum mashaw fīhi wa-idhā aẓlama ʿalayhim qāmū wa-law shāʾa llāhu la-dhahaba bi-samʿihim wa-abṣārihim inna llāha ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: The lightning all but snatches away their sight. Each time it flashes for them they walk on by its light, and when darkness falls upon them they stand still. Had God so willed, He would have taken away their hearing and their sight. Truly God is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: So it is with the people of heedlessness, says Qushayrī: when they attend the assemblies of exhortation, when their hearts incline toward tenderness, their state draws near to repentance and their longing for return revives; but as soon as they go back to their calculations and consult their kin, these counsel them to return to the world, threaten them with weakness and incapacity, and their resolve collapses. And "had God so willed, He would have taken away their hearing and their sight": as He has made them deaf and blind in their secret, so He is able to withdraw, even from those content with an Islam of mere appearances, the very grace of their outward deeds.

mots.racine: ب ر ق / خ ط ف / ب ص ر
mots.sens: the lightning all but snatches their sight
mots.def: khaṭafa: to snatch, to seize.

mots.racine: ض و ء / م ش ي
mots.sens: each time it flashes they walk on by it
mots.def: mashā: to walk.

mots.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
mots.tr: inna llāha ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
mots.sens: God is powerful over all things
mots.def: qadīr: all-powerful.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اعْبُدُوا رَبَّكُمُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ وَالَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nāsu ʿbudū rabbakumu lladhī khalaqakum wa-lladhīna min qablikum laʿallakum tattaqūn
versets.trad: O humankind! Worship your Lord, who created you and those who came before you. Perhaps you will attain to piety!
versets.commentaire: Worship, says Qushayrī, is accord with the Command, the spending of all one's energy in the quest for the verification of the Unseen; and he includes within it unification through the heart, laying bare through the secret, withdrawal through intention, the humbling of the soul, and surrender to the decree. Worship Him, he adds, by stripping yourselves of what is forbidden, by persevering in acts of obedience, and by facing the obligations with recollection and submission. As for "perhaps" (laʿalla), he sees in it a gentleness: by this word God holds them upon the frontier of fear and hope.

mots.racine: ع ب د / ر ب ب
mots.def: ʿabada: to worship, to serve.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ق ب ل
mots.sens: who created you and those before you
mots.def: khalaqa: to create.
ittaqā: to guard oneself through reverential awe.

versets.ar: الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ فِرَاشًا وَالسَّمَاءَ بِنَاءً وَأَنزَلَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً فَأَخْرَجَ بِهِ مِنَ الثَّمَرَاتِ رِزْقًا لَّكُمْ ۖ فَلَا تَجْعَلُوا لِلَّهِ أَندَادًا وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: alladhī jaʿala lakumu l-arḍa firāshan wa-s-samāʾa bināʾan wa-anzala mina s-samāʾi māʾan fa-akhraja bihi mina th-thamarāti rizqan lakum fa-lā tajʿalū li-llāhi andādan wa-antum taʿlamūn
versets.trad: He who made the earth a couch for you and the firmament a canopy, and sent down water from the sky, bringing forth thereby fruits for your sustenance. So do not set up rivals to God, while you know...
versets.commentaire: God, says Qushayrī, made Himself known to them by recalling His bounties: the sky raised as a roof, the earth spread as a couch, the plants drawn forth by the rain as sustenance. And from this he draws the inward detachment: He freed them from dependence upon their fellows, made Himself sufficient for them — the sky for a covering, the earth for a bed, the lawful for sustenance, obedience for a craft, worship for an occupation, remembrance for a companion, and the Lord for a guarantor. Thus: do not bind your hearts to others in seeking your needs, for if you believe that profit or harm comes from a creature, that in truth is an association.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / أ ر ض / ف ر ش
mots.sens: He made the earth a couch for you
mots.def: firāsh: bed, carpet.

mots.racine: س م و / ب ن ي
mots.def: bināʾ: construction, vault.
rizq: sustenance.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ن د د
mots.sens: do not set up rivals to God
mots.def: andād: equals, rivals.

versets.ar: وَإِن كُنتُمْ فِي رَيْبٍ مِّمَّا نَزَّلْنَا عَلَىٰ عَبْدِنَا فَأْتُوا بِسُورَةٍ مِّن مِّثْلِهِ وَادْعُوا شُهَدَاءَكُم مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-in kuntum fī raybin mimmā nazzalnā ʿalā ʿabdinā fa-ʾtū bi-sūratin min mithlihi wa-dʿū shuhadāʾakum min dūni llāhi in kuntum ṣādiqīn
versets.trad: If you are in doubt concerning what We have sent down upon Our servant, then bring a sūra like it, and call upon your witnesses besides God, if you are truthful.
versets.commentaire: The inner sight of the strangers, says Qushayrī, was veiled, so that they did not contemplate His Beloved — upon him be the prayer of God — and strayed in the valleys of conjecture, having lost the light of solicitude. Thus the more the Messenger multiplied signs and miracles, the more they added doubt upon doubt: such is the way of one who turns from the True, the radiance of the proofs only increases his blindness to Reality. And God, to perfect the argument, disclosed to them their impotence to rival the miracle of the Qurʾān, which overwhelmed humankind from first to last.

mots.racine: ر ي ب / ن ز ل
mots.sens: in doubt about what We have sent down
mots.def: rayb: doubt.
ʿabd: servant (the Prophet).

mots.racine: أ ت ي / م ث ل
mots.def: mithl: like, similar.
ṣādiq: sincere, truthful.

versets.ar: فَإِن لَّمْ تَفْعَلُوا وَلَن تَفْعَلُوا فَاتَّقُوا النَّارَ الَّتِي وَقُودُهَا النَّاسُ وَالْحِجَارَةُ ۖ أُعِدَّتْ لِلْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-in lam tafʿalū wa-lan tafʿalū fa-ttaqū n-nāra llatī waqūduhā n-nāsu wa-l-ḥijāratu uʿiddat li-l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: And if you do not — and you never will — then fear the Fire whose fuel is people and stones; it has been prepared for the disbelievers.
themes: good news veiled for the believers
versets.commentaire: And when, says Qushayrī, the hearts of the believers drew near to the height of dread at the mention of the Fire, He overtook them with reassurance, saying: "it has been prepared for the disbelievers" — and this is good news for the believers. Such, he adds, is the custom of the True: when He frightens His enemies, He gladdens at the same moment His beloved. And just as the ruse of the unbelievers vanishes before the miracles of the messengers, the pretensions of the impostors dissolve at the appearance of the lights of the truthful.

mots.sens: if you do not — and you never will
mots.def: faʿala: to do.

mots.racine: ن و ر / و ق د / ح ج ر
mots.sens: the Fire whose fuel is people and stones
mots.def: waqūd: fuel; ḥijāra: stones.

mots.racine: ع د د / ك ف ر
mots.def: aʿadda: to make ready, to prepare.

versets.ar: وَبَشِّرِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ أَنَّ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ ۖ كُلَّمَا رُزِقُوا مِنْهَا مِن ثَمَرَةٍ رِّزْقًا قَالُوا هَٰذَا الَّذِي رُزِقْنَا مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَأُتُوا بِهِ مُتَشَابِهًا ۖ وَلَهُمْ فِيهَا أَزْوَاجٌ مُّطَهَّرَةٌ ۖ وَهُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-bashshiri lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti anna lahum jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru kullamā ruziqū minhā min thamaratin rizqan qālū hādhā lladhī ruziqnā min qablu wa-utū bihi mutashābihan wa-lahum fīhā azwājun muṭahharatun wa-hum fīhā khālidūn
versets.trad: Give glad tidings to those who believe and do righteous deeds: theirs shall be Gardens beneath which rivers flow. Each time they are given fruit therein for nourishment, they will say, "This is what we were given before," for they are given likenesses of it. Therein shall they have purified spouses, and there they shall abide forever.
versets.commentaire: This tiding, says Qushayrī, holds two promises: for the common believers, Gardens of the deferred reward; and for the elect, hastened graces — not the gardens of recompense, but the gardens of nearness. And he unfolds the pairs: those are the meadows of respite, these the meadows of intimacy; those the raising of the degrees, these the breath of the secret converse; those the unveiling of the Bounty to the outward, these the unveiling of Beauty and Majesty to the inmost secrets. As for the fruit ever recognized yet ever higher, he sees in it perpetual ascent: the states of the people of Reality are in unceasing ascension; each time one of them rises, he supposes that what he is about to taste resembles the past — and finds it a thousand times greater.

mots.racine: ب ش ر / ع م ل / ص ل ح
mots.sens: give glad tidings to those who believe and do good
mots.def: bashshara: to announce good news.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ج ر ي / ن ه ر
mots.def: janna: garden, paradise.

mots.racine: ز و ج / ط ه ر
mots.def: muṭahhara: purified.
khālid: eternal, abiding.

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَسْتَحْيِي أَن يَضْرِبَ مَثَلًا مَّا بَعُوضَةً فَمَا فَوْقَهَا ۚ فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فَيَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهُ الْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ ۖ وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَيَقُولُونَ مَاذَا أَرَادَ اللَّهُ بِهَٰذَا مَثَلًا ۘ يُضِلُّ بِهِ كَثِيرًا وَيَهْدِي بِهِ كَثِيرًا ۚ وَمَا يُضِلُّ بِهِ إِلَّا الْفَاسِقِينَ
versets.tr: inna llāha lā yastaḥyī an yaḍriba mathalan mā baʿūḍatan fa-mā fawqahā fa-ammā lladhīna āmanū fa-yaʿlamūna annahu l-ḥaqqu min rabbihim wa-ammā lladhīna kafarū fa-yaqūlūna mādhā arāda llāhu bi-hādhā mathalan yuḍillu bihi kathīran wa-yahdī bihi kathīran wa-mā yuḍillu bihi illā l-fāsiqīn
versets.trad: God does not disdain to coin a parable, be it of a gnat or of what is greater. Those who believe know that it is the Truth from their Lord. But those who disbelieve say, "What did God mean by this parable?" Thereby He leads many astray and thereby He guides many; yet He leads astray by it none but the corrupt.
themes: the two readings of the sign
versets.commentaire: Creation, says Qushayrī, measured against the existence of the True, is less than a mote of dust in the air; so the Throne and the gnat are equal in His power: to create the Throne is no harder for Him, to create the gnat no lighter, for He transcends effort and ease. And he distinguishes the two regards: he whose inner eyes have opened beholds beings and traces only with the eye of meditation, and gains thereby nothing but increase of insight; but he whose eyes are intoxicated with heedlessness draws from the parables only an increase of ignorance and bewilderment. This Book, he concludes, is for some healing and mercy, for others misfortune and trial.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / ض ر ب / م ث ل
mots.sens: He does not disdain to coin a parable
mots.def: istaḥyā: to feel scruple; here, to refrain.

mots.racine: ب ع ض / ف و ق
mots.sens: a gnat or what is greater
mots.def: baʿūḍa: mosquito, gnat.

mots.racine: ح ق ق / ر ب ب
mots.sens: that it is the Truth from their Lord
mots.def: al-ḥaqq: the Truth.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ف س ق
mots.sens: He leads astray by it none but the corrupt
mots.def: fāsiq: transgressor, corrupt.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَنقُضُونَ عَهْدَ اللَّهِ مِن بَعْدِ مِيثَاقِهِ وَيَقْطَعُونَ مَا أَمَرَ اللَّهُ بِهِ أَن يُوصَلَ وَيُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْخَاسِرُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna yanquḍūna ʿahda llāhi min baʿdi mīthāqihi wa-yaqṭaʿūna mā amara llāhu bihi an yūṣala wa-yufsidūna fī l-arḍi ulāʾika humu l-khāsirūn
versets.trad: Those who break the covenant of God after having accepted His pact, who sever what God has commanded to be joined, and who sow corruption upon the earth — these are the losers.
versets.commentaire: The allusion, says Qushayrī, is to one who has walked the path of desire and then returned to the path of habit, who renounced his soul and then did not keep his word at the hour of resolve, and who descended from the indication of Reality to the easy paths of the Law. And of "joining what God has commanded to be joined" he makes a subtle rule: to join the means of the True by severing the means of creation — for the joining of what is His is not perfected save by severing what is yours. Indeed: among the breakings of the covenant is that your secret swerve a single moment from His contemplation; and your corruption upon the earth is an hour that passes over you without your seeing Him therein.

mots.racine: ن ق ض / ع ه د
mots.sens: they break the covenant of God
mots.def: naqaḍa: to undo, to break; ʿahd: covenant.
mīthāq: solemn pact.

mots.ar: يَقْطَعُونَ مَا أَمَرَ اللَّهُ بِهِ أَن يُوصَلَ
mots.tr: yaqṭaʿūna mā amara llāhu bihi an yūṣala
mots.racine: ق ط ع / و ص ل
mots.sens: they sever what God commanded to be joined
mots.def: qaṭaʿa: to sever; waṣala: to join.
khāsir: loser.

versets.ar: كَيْفَ تَكْفُرُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَكُنتُمْ أَمْوَاتًا فَأَحْيَاكُمْ ۖ ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ ثُمَّ يُحْيِيكُمْ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: kayfa takfurūna bi-llāhi wa-kuntum amwātan fa-aḥyākum thumma yumītukum thumma yuḥyīkum thumma ilayhi turjaʿūn
versets.trad: How can you deny God, when you were dead and He gave you life? Then He will cause you to die, then He will bring you back to life, and then to Him you shall be returned.
versets.commentaire: It is, says Qushayrī, a word of wonder and grandeur upon the state of the servant: it does not befit, before the evidence of the signs, that his heart should incline toward denial. And he yields a mystical reading: "you were dead" through your ignorance of Us, "He gave you life" through your knowledge of Us, "then He will cause you to die" to your own testimonies, "then He will bring you to life" through Him by tearing you from yourselves, "then to Him you shall be returned" by your turning in His hand. Thus, he adds, they are ceaselessly between negation and affirmation, between subsistence and annihilation, between lucidity and effacement: such is the custom of the True with them.

mots.racine: ك ي ف / ك ف ر
mots.def: kafara: to deny, to disbelieve.

mots.racine: م و ت / ح ي ي
mots.sens: you were dead, He gave you life
mots.def: amwāt: dead; aḥyā: to give life.

mots.racine: م و ت / ح ي ي
mots.sens: then causes you to die, then to live
mots.def: amāta: to cause to die.

mots.sens: then to Him you shall be returned
mots.def: rujiʿa: to be returned.

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ لَكُم مَّا فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ اسْتَوَىٰ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ فَسَوَّاهُنَّ سَبْعَ سَمَاوَاتٍ ۚ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī khalaqa lakum mā fī l-arḍi jamīʿan thumma stawā ilā s-samāʾi fa-sawwāhunna sabʿa samāwātin wa-huwa bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīm
versets.trad: He it is who created for you all that is upon the earth. Then He turned toward the heaven and fashioned it into seven heavens. He has knowledge of all things.
versets.commentaire: He has, says Qushayrī, subjected to them all creatures that they might draw profit from them: upon the earth they dwell, beneath the sky they abide, by the stars they take their bearings; indeed, there is no trace nor sign upon which they meditate without recognizing therein the perfection of His power and the manifestation of His Lordship. And of "He turned toward the heaven" he sets aside all localization: beings have arisen by His power — not that the True, in His essence, settles upon a creature; for Oneness and Absoluteness are His due, and what they imagine of a place containing Him is impossible: place exists through Him, not He through place.

mots.ar: خَلَقَ لَكُم مَّا فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا
mots.tr: khalaqa lakum mā fī l-arḍi jamīʿan
mots.racine: خ ل ق / أ ر ض / ج م ع
mots.sens: He created for you all that is upon the earth
mots.def: jamīʿ: the totality.

mots.racine: س و ي / س م و
mots.sens: then He turned toward the heaven
mots.def: istawā: to turn toward, to set Himself.

mots.racine: س و ي / س ب ع
mots.sens: and fashioned them into seven heavens
mots.def: sawwā: to order, to perfect.

mots.sens: He has knowledge of all things
mots.def: ʿalīm: all-knowing.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ إِنِّي جَاعِلٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ خَلِيفَةً ۖ قَالُوا أَتَجْعَلُ فِيهَا مَن يُفْسِدُ فِيهَا وَيَسْفِكُ الدِّمَاءَ وَنَحْنُ نُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِكَ وَنُقَدِّسُ لَكَ ۖ قَالَ إِنِّي أَعْلَمُ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh qāla rabbuka li-l-malāʾikati innī jāʿilun fī l-arḍi khalīfatan qālū a-tajʿalu fīhā man yufsidu fīhā wa-yasfiku d-dimāʾa wa-naḥnu nusabbiḥu bi-ḥamdika wa-nuqaddisu laka qāla innī aʿlamu mā lā taʿlamūn
versets.trad: When your Lord said to the angels, "I am setting upon the earth a vicegerent," they said, "Will You set therein one who will sow corruption and shed blood, while we glorify You with praise and sanctify You?" He said, "I know what you do not know."
themes: the secret the angels did not know
versets.commentaire: Here, says Qushayrī, is the beginning of the manifestation of His secret in Adam and his progeny. God, he observes, created the Throne and the Gardens without saying "I am about to create"; but in honor and election of Adam He said: "I am setting upon the earth a vicegerent." And he unveils the sense of "I know what you do not know" through a cascade of glosses: I know the purity of the hearts of the believers in My love, and the subtlety of their secrets in keeping My covenants, though their outward be stained with disobedience. Then: I have clothed you in your obedience and have clothed them in My mercy; you are in the adornment of your sanctification, they beneath the veil of My pardon. And the pearl: the brokenness of the hearts of the servants has with Us a mighty due.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / خ ل ف
mots.sens: I am setting upon the earth a vicegerent
mots.def: khalīfa: deputy, vicegerent.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ف س د
mots.sens: will You set therein one who corrupts it
mots.def: afsada: to corrupt.

mots.racine: س ب ح / ح م د / ق د س
mots.sens: we glorify You and sanctify You
mots.def: sabbaḥa: to glorify; qaddasa: to sanctify.

mots.sens: I know what you do not know
mots.def: ʿalima: to know.

versets.ar: وَعَلَّمَ آدَمَ الْأَسْمَاءَ كُلَّهَا ثُمَّ عَرَضَهُمْ عَلَى الْمَلَائِكَةِ فَقَالَ أَنبِئُونِي بِأَسْمَاءِ هَٰؤُلَاءِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ʿallama ādama l-asmāʾa kullahā thumma ʿaraḍahum ʿalā l-malāʾikati fa-qāla anbiʾūnī bi-asmāʾi hāʾulāʾi in kuntum ṣādiqīn
versets.trad: He taught Adam the names of all things, then presented them to the angels, saying, "Tell Me the names of these, if you are truthful."
versets.commentaire: "All the names," says Qushayrī, marks the total embracing: and just as He taught Adam the names of the creatures, He taught him the Names of the True — yet He manifested to the angels only the place of his privilege in the knowledge of the names of creatures, and by this measure his preeminence over them appeared; as for his singular knowledge of the divine Names, that is a secret no angel brought near has contemplated. And he sets the two gifts in opposition: the proper portion of the angels is glorification and sanctification — acts that befit creatures, for obedience is the mark of servants; but knowledge is an attribute of praise that belongs to the True. Thus he concludes: excellence through knowledge is more perfect than excellence through act.

mots.racine: ع ل م / س م و
mots.sens: He taught Adam all the names
mots.def: ʿallama: to teach; asmāʾ: names.

mots.racine: ع ر ض / م ل ك
mots.sens: then He presented them to the angels
mots.def: ʿaraḍa: to set forth, to present.

mots.sens: tell Me the names of these
mots.def: anbaʾa: to inform.
ṣādiq: truthful.

versets.ar: قَالُوا سُبْحَانَكَ لَا عِلْمَ لَنَا إِلَّا مَا عَلَّمْتَنَا ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ الْعَلِيمُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: qālū subḥānaka lā ʿilma lanā illā mā ʿallamtanā innaka anta l-ʿalīmu l-ḥakīm
versets.trad: They said, "Glory be to You! We have no knowledge save what You have taught us; You, truly, are the All-Knowing, the All-Wise."
themes: knowledge that comes from Him alone
versets.commentaire: They set, says Qushayrī, praise before excuse, and declared the purity of His decree above all objection: that is, we have no knowledge of what You ask of us, and no reproach falls upon You for having charged the helpless with what he cannot bear. And of "You are the All-Knowing, the All-Wise" he makes the perfect acquiescence: what You do is true and just; none holds judgment over You, and from You comes neither unreason nor ugliness.

mots.def: subḥān: proclaimed transcendence.

mots.ar: لَا عِلْمَ لَنَا إِلَّا مَا عَلَّمْتَنَا
mots.tr: lā ʿilma lanā illā mā ʿallamtanā
mots.sens: we have no knowledge save what You have taught us
mots.def: ʿilm: knowledge.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ح ك م
mots.sens: You are the All-Knowing, the All-Wise
mots.def: ʿalīm: knowing; ḥakīm: wise.

versets.ar: قَالَ يَا آدَمُ أَنبِئْهُم بِأَسْمَائِهِمْ ۖ فَلَمَّا أَنبَأَهُم بِأَسْمَائِهِمْ قَالَ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ إِنِّي أَعْلَمُ غَيْبَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَأَعْلَمُ مَا تُبْدُونَ وَمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْتُمُونَ
versets.tr: qāla yā ādamu anbiʾhum bi-asmāʾihim fa-lammā anbaʾahum bi-asmāʾihim qāla a-lam aqul lakum innī aʿlamu ghayba s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-aʿlamu mā tubdūna wa-mā kuntum taktumūn
versets.trad: He said, "O Adam, tell them their names." When Adam had informed the angels of their names, the Lord said, "Did I not tell you that I know the unseen of the heavens and the earth, and that I know what you disclose and what you conceal?"
versets.commentaire: Among the marks of solicitude toward Adam, says Qushayrī: when He said to the angels "tell Me," the majesty of the address seized them and ravished them from themselves; but when Adam's turn came, He entrusted to him the telling — "inform them" — and the address to the angels did not lay upon him the same gripping of awe. And he meditates upon the mystery of the decree: when God willed to save Adam, He preserved him, taught him, and made the traces of grace appear upon him; but when He willed to accomplish His ruling in him, He let forgetfulness enter him, until he forgot his covenant in the very Presence. Such are the decrees of the True: by them the servants are humbled, and He does what He wills.

mots.racine: ن ب أ / س م و
mots.sens: O Adam, inform them of their names
mots.def: anbaʾa: to inform.
qāla: to say.

mots.racine: ع ل م / غ ي ب
mots.sens: I know the unseen of the heavens and the earth
mots.def: ghayb: the invisible.

mots.racine: ب د و / ك ت م
mots.sens: what you disclose and what you concealed
mots.def: abdā: to reveal; katama: to hide.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ اسْجُدُوا لِآدَمَ فَسَجَدُوا إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ أَبَىٰ وَاسْتَكْبَرَ وَكَانَ مِنَ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idh qulnā li-l-malāʾikati sjudū li-ādama fa-sajadū illā iblīsa abā wa-stakbara wa-kāna mina l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: When We said to the angels, "Prostrate yourselves before Adam!" they prostrated themselves, except Iblīs, who refused and swelled with pride: he was among the disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: The prostration, Qushayrī makes plain, was not worship of Adam, but accord with the divine Command: their prostration before Adam was worship of God, since it was by His command, and homage to Adam, since He commanded it in honor of his rank. And he unveils the root of the fall: Iblīs dwelt a while in the complacency of his obedience, parading in the adornment of his conformity; he had been granted the rank of the first, he was thought worthy of election — and his case was as the poet said: "the lamp of union shone between us, then a wind of separation blew, and it went out." The pearl: a human fault escaped Adam, and the mercy of the One overtook him; but Iblīs was struck with an eternal misfortune — whoever is overcome by the decree, no toil can save him.

mots.def: sajada: to prostrate.
Iblīs: the devil, the refuser.

mots.racine: أ ب ي / ك ب ر
mots.sens: he refused and swelled with pride
mots.def: abā: to refuse; istakbara: to grow proud.
kāfir: denier.

versets.ar: وَقُلْنَا يَا آدَمُ اسْكُنْ أَنتَ وَزَوْجُكَ الْجَنَّةَ وَكُلَا مِنْهَا رَغَدًا حَيْثُ شِئْتُمَا وَلَا تَقْرَبَا هَٰذِهِ الشَّجَرَةَ فَتَكُونَا مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qulnā yā ādamu skun anta wa-zawjuka l-jannata wa-kulā minhā raghadan ḥaythu shiʾtumā wa-lā taqrabā hādhihi sh-shajarata fa-takūnā mina ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: We said, "O Adam, dwell, you and your spouse, in the Garden; eat of its fruits at ease, wherever you will; but do not approach this tree, lest you be among the wrongdoers."
versets.commentaire: He set him, says Qushayrī, in the Garden, but with his entry He planted there the tree of trial: without the prior decree, that tree would have withered beneath his hand, his hand would not have reached its leaves — and what was to come to pass would not have come to pass. No place is more excellent than the Garden, no man more discerning than Adam, no resolve firmer than his — but power lets itself not be defied, and the decree lets itself not be gainsaid. And he observes the law of the human heart: all that is forbidden the son of Adam, his desires rush toward it: the whole earth of the Garden was made lawful to him, a single tree was forbidden him — and it was toward that one that his patience forsook him.

mots.racine: س ك ن / ز و ج / ج ن ن
mots.sens: dwell, you and your spouse, in the Garden
mots.def: sakana: to dwell; zawj: spouse.

mots.racine: أ ك ل / ر غ د
mots.def: raghad: abundance, ease.

mots.racine: ق ر ب / ش ج ر
mots.def: shajara: tree.

mots.sens: lest you be among the wrongdoers
mots.def: ẓālim: unjust, wrongdoer.

versets.ar: فَأَزَلَّهُمَا الشَّيْطَانُ عَنْهَا فَأَخْرَجَهُمَا مِمَّا كَانَا فِيهِ ۖ وَقُلْنَا اهْبِطُوا بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ ۖ وَلَكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ مُسْتَقَرٌّ وَمَتَاعٌ إِلَىٰ حِينٍ
versets.tr: fa-azallahumā sh-shayṭānu ʿanhā fa-akhrajahumā mimmā kānā fīhi wa-qulnā hbiṭū baʿḍukum li-baʿḍin ʿaduwwun wa-lakum fī l-arḍi mustaqarrun wa-matāʿun ilā ḥīn
versets.trad: Then Satan made them slip from it and brought them out of where they were. We said, "Go down, enemies one of another! On the earth there shall be for you a dwelling-place and enjoyment for a time."
versets.commentaire: "He made them slip," says Qushayrī, brought them to the fault; but in truth, nothing moved them save the Power, and their turning came to pass only within the Decree. And he unveils the paradox of this fall: He brought them out of their rank and their degree openly — yet, in the judgment of the True, their dignity and their worth were only increased thereby. As for the enmity, he makes clear: He established hostility between them and Satan, but God was with Adam. And he seals it: Satan had no real power; for the privilege of the True is inviolable — "over My servants you have no authority."

mots.racine: ز ل ل / ش ط ن
mots.sens: Satan made them slip from it
mots.def: azalla: to make slip, to cause to err.

mots.racine: ه ب ط / ع د و
mots.sens: go down, enemies one of another
mots.def: habaṭa: to descend; ʿaduww: enemy.

mots.ar: وَلَكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ مُسْتَقَرٌّ وَمَتَاعٌ إِلَىٰ حِينٍ
mots.tr: wa-lakum fī l-arḍi mustaqarrun wa-matāʿun ilā ḥīn
mots.racine: ق ر ر / م ت ع / ح ي ن
mots.sens: on the earth a dwelling and enjoyment for a time
mots.def: mustaqarr: abode; matāʿ: enjoyment.

versets.ar: فَتَلَقَّىٰ آدَمُ مِن رَّبِّهِ كَلِمَاتٍ فَتَابَ عَلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّهُ هُوَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: fa-talaqqā ādamu min rabbihi kalimātin fa-tāba ʿalayhi innahu huwa t-tawwābu r-raḥīm
versets.trad: Then Adam received words from his Lord and turned to Him in repentance. He, truly, is the Ever-Relenting, the Merciful.
versets.commentaire: Words, says Qushayrī, ran upon the tongue of Adam toward the True, and the True caused Adam to hear words. And God, he observes, left this saying undefined, that the story might remain veiled, and that a place might be left for allusion and hidden meaning. He sees in it the exchange of lovers: the words of Adam were excuse and avowal, the words of the True welcome and favor. And from this he draws the consolation of the exiled traveler: when He expelled him from the Garden, He made of the precious converse He gave him to hear a provision, that it might be for him remembrance and sustenance. For the converse of the beloved admits no explanation, and no stranger embraces it.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ك ل م
mots.sens: Adam received words from his Lord
mots.def: talaqqā: to receive, to welcome; kalimāt: words.
tāba ʿalā: to accept repentance.

mots.racine: ت و ب / ر ح م
mots.sens: He is the Ever-Relenting, the Merciful
mots.def: tawwāb: He who returns ceaselessly to pardon; raḥīm: merciful.

versets.ar: قُلْنَا اهْبِطُوا مِنْهَا جَمِيعًا ۖ فَإِمَّا يَأْتِيَنَّكُم مِّنِّي هُدًى فَمَن تَبِعَ هُدَايَ فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
versets.tr: qulnā hbiṭū minhā jamīʿan fa-immā yaʾtiyannakum minnī hudan fa-man tabiʿa hudāya fa-lā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūn
versets.trad: We said, "Go down from it, all of you! A guidance will surely come to you from Me, and those who follow My guidance shall feel neither fear nor sorrow."
versets.commentaire: A lapse of courtesy upon the carpet, says Qushayrī, obliges a sending back to the door: when Adam failed in good conduct in the very heart of nearness, God said "go down." Yet at once, when He brought him out of the Garden and sent him down upon the earth, He announced to him that He would restore him to his state should he incline his heart toward return, saying: "if a guidance comes to you from Me, those who follow My guidance shall have neither fear nor sorrow." Thus the fall is not abandonment: the door of return remains open to whoever inclines the heart toward Him.

mots.racine: ه ب ط / ج م ع
mots.sens: go down from it, all of you
mots.def: habaṭa: to descend.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ه د ي
mots.sens: if a guidance comes to you from Me
mots.def: hudā: right direction.
tabiʿa: to follow.

mots.ar: فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
mots.tr: fa-lā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūn
mots.racine: خ و ف / ح ز ن
mots.sens: no fear upon them, no sorrow
mots.def: khawf: fear; ḥuzn: sorrow.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kafarū wa-kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā ulāʾika aṣḥābu n-nāri hum fīhā khālidūn
versets.trad: But those who disbelieve and deny Our signs as lies — they shall be the companions of the Fire; therein they shall abide forever.
versets.commentaire: Those, says Qushayrī, who set against grace something other than gratitude, and were heedless of attestation and verification — these shall have a deferred painful chastisement and a hastened separation. The denial of the signs is thus the exact reverse of the guidance promised in the foregoing verse: whoever follows the guidance has neither fear nor sorrow, whoever belies the signs inherits the fire and the separation.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / ك ذ ب / أ ي ي
mots.sens: they disbelieved and denied Our signs
mots.def: kadhdhaba: to treat as a lie; āyāt: signs.

mots.racine: ص ح ب / ن و ر
mots.sens: those are the companions of the Fire
mots.def: aṣḥāb al-nār: the people of the Fire.
khālid: eternal.

versets.ar: يَا بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتِيَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَأَوْفُوا بِعَهْدِي أُوفِ بِعَهْدِكُمْ وَإِيَّايَ فَارْهَبُونِ
versets.tr: yā banī isrāʾīla dhkurū niʿmatiya llatī anʿamtu ʿalaykum wa-awfū bi-ʿahdī ūfi bi-ʿahdikum wa-iyyāya fa-rhabūn
versets.trad: O Children of Israel! Remember the favor with which I have blessed you. Be faithful to My covenant; I will be faithful to your covenant. And fear Me, Me alone!
versets.commentaire: The true favor, says Qushayrī, for the people of Reality, is what makes you contemplate the Giver, or what reminds you of the Giver, or what leads you to the Giver, or what does not veil you from the Giver. And he notes a subtlety: to the Children of Israel He commanded the remembrance of the favors, but to the community of Muḥammad — upon him be peace — He commanded the remembrance of the Benefactor: "remember Me, and I will remember you." Upon the reciprocal covenant he unfolds a litany: His covenant is to keep the knowledge, ours the joining of pardon; keep My covenant by guarding the secret, I will keep yours by beautiful conduct; keep My covenant by preferring no other to Me, I will keep yours by withdrawing no grace from you; keep My covenant by saying without ceasing "my Lord, my Lord," I will keep yours by saying "My servant, My servant."

mots.def: banū Isrāʾīl: the Israelites.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ن ع م
mots.def: niʿma: favor, grace.

mots.racine: و ف ي / ع ه د
mots.sens: keep My covenant, I will keep yours
mots.def: awfā: to keep, to honor; ʿahd: covenant.
rahiba: to fear, to dread.

versets.ar: وَآمِنُوا بِمَا أَنزَلْتُ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا مَعَكُمْ وَلَا تَكُونُوا أَوَّلَ كَافِرٍ بِهِ ۖ وَلَا تَشْتَرُوا بِآيَاتِي ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا وَإِيَّايَ فَاتَّقُونِ
versets.tr: wa-āminū bi-mā anzaltu muṣaddiqan li-mā maʿakum wa-lā takūnū awwala kāfirin bihi wa-lā tashtarū bi-āyātī thamanan qalīlan wa-iyyāya fa-ttaqūni
versets.trad: Believe in what I have sent down, confirming what you already possess. Do not be the first to deny it; do not barter My signs for a paltry price, and fear Me!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes the degrees of faith: the common believe by proof, at the cost of reasoning; the elect believe by inward evidence, because the Real has turned toward them; and the ultimate term is faith by contemplation, reserved for the elect of the elect. Do not be the first to deny, He says, for he who inaugurates a way of denial bears a burden heavier than the one who merely follows. "Do not barter My signs for a paltry price": do not prefer your meager portion to the immensity of My due. And "fear Me": many are they who dread His chastisement, few who tremble before His regard and His vision.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ن ز ل
mots.sens: believe in what I have sent down
mots.def: injunction to believe in the Qurʾān sent down.
muṣaddiq: that which attests the truth.

mots.sens: do not barter My signs for a paltry price
mots.def: ishtarā: to exchange, to sell.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَلْبِسُوا الْحَقَّ بِالْبَاطِلِ وَتَكْتُمُوا الْحَقَّ وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā talbisū l-ḥaqqa bi-l-bāṭili wa-taktumū l-ḥaqqa wa-antum taʿlamūna
versets.trad: Do not clothe the truth with falsehood. Do not conceal the truth, while you know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī warns: do not imagine that the contraries can be reconciled, nor that one can be in a single state in two abodes at once. The servant is either expanded by the Real or fettered by his carnal portion; to join the two is a chimera. To mingle the true with the false is to defile it; to silence the true is to disguise it; while you know that the due of the Real is that He be sanctified.

mots.racine: ل ب س / ح ق ق / ب ط ل
mots.sens: do not clothe the truth with falsehood
mots.def: labasa: to clothe, to mingle so as to obscure.

mots.sens: and do not conceal the truth
mots.def: katama: to silence, to hide.
mots.gram: verb in the subjunctive under the prohibition

mots.def: aggravates the fault by the awareness one has of it.

versets.ar: وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكَاةَ وَارْكَعُوا مَعَ الرَّاكِعِينَ
versets.tr: wa-aqīmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātū z-zakāta wa-rkaʿū maʿa r-rākiʿīna
versets.trad: Perform the prayer, give the alms, and bow with those who bow.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: observe the courtesies of the Presence, for the keeping of good manners is more perfect, in service, than the service itself. The alms (zakāt) is not only that of goods: there is an alms of aspirations, as there is an alms of the graces received — the man of God pours upon those who follow him the overflow of his impulses and the subtlety of his regard. "Bow with those who bow": follow the traces of the ancients and flee the path of isolation, for to stand within the throng of the gathering is surer than to set oneself apart from it.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و
mots.def: aqāma ṣ-ṣalāt: to establish the prayer in all its conditions.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ز ك و
mots.def: zakāt: purification through giving.
a call to prayer in common.

versets.ar: أَتَأْمُرُونَ النَّاسَ بِالْبِرِّ وَتَنسَوْنَ أَنفُسَكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ تَتْلُونَ الْكِتَابَ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: a-taʾmurūna n-nāsa bi-l-birri wa-tansawna anfusakum wa-antum tatlūna l-kitāba a-fa-lā taʿqilūna
versets.trad: Will you enjoin virtue upon others while you forget yourselves, even as you recite the Book? Will you not reason?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the reproach in a cascade: do you urge others to hasten toward Him while you are content to stay behind? Do you invite the creatures toward Us while you remain seated far from Us? Do you send the delegations to the spring without drinking from it yourselves? And again: do you discern in others the weight of an atom before the Real, yet close your eyes to mountains of failings within yourselves? You see the speck in another's eye and see not the beam in your own.

mots.racine: أ م ر / ب ر ر
mots.sens: do you enjoin virtue upon the people
mots.def: birr: active piety, the good.

mots.racine: ن س ي / ن ف س
mots.def: nasiya: to forget, to neglect.
ʿaqala: to grasp through intelligence.

versets.ar: وَاسْتَعِينُوا بِالصَّبْرِ وَالصَّلَاةِ ۚ وَإِنَّهَا لَكَبِيرَةٌ إِلَّا عَلَى الْخَاشِعِينَ
versets.tr: wa-staʿīnū bi-ṣ-ṣabri wa-ṣ-ṣalāti wa-innahā la-kabīratun illā ʿalā l-khāshiʿīna
versets.trad: Seek help in patience and in prayer; truly, the prayer is heavy, save for the humble,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: patience is the weaning of the soul from its habits; prayer is the exposing of oneself to receive the unions. Patience points toward the abandonment of all other than Him, prayer toward the abiding station before the Unseen. This twofold help is heavy, save for the one to whom the Real has unveiled Himself in the inmost secret, for when the Real manifests, what weighed upon the creatures becomes light and easy. And He says as though teaching: seek help in Me to endure with Me, and in My safeguarding to hold your prayer toward Me, that the irruptions of the unveiling and of the majesty may not sweep you away.

mots.racine: ع و ن / ص ب ر
mots.def: istaʿāna: to seek assistance.
the second recourse offered to the believer.
khāshiʿ: one whose heart bows and grows still.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَظُنُّونَ أَنَّهُم مُّلَاقُو رَبِّهِمْ وَأَنَّهُمْ إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna yaẓunnūna annahum mulāqū rabbihim wa-annahum ilayhi rājiʿūna
versets.trad: those who are certain that they shall meet their Lord and that to Him they shall return.
versets.commentaire: The humble whom the foregoing verse intends are described here: they are those whose inner eye holds assured the meeting with the Lord and the return to Him. For Qushayrī, this "thinking" (ẓann) is no uncertain conjecture but a vivid foretaste of the Presence: he who lives in the expectation of the face of his Lord finds prayer light, for his heart is already stretched toward the end of the journey.

mots.def: ẓann: here, inward certainty, not mere doubt.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ر ب ب
mots.def: liqāʾ: the meeting with God at the end.
rujūʿ: the final return to God.

versets.ar: يَا بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتِيَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَأَنِّي فَضَّلْتُكُمْ عَلَى الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: yā banī isrāʾīla dhkurū niʿmatiya llatī anʿamtu ʿalaykum wa-annī faḍḍaltukum ʿalā l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: O Children of Israel! Remember the favor with which I have blessed you, and remember that I preferred you above all the worlds!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sets two regards in opposition: to the Children of Israel He gave to contemplate their own merit — "I preferred you"; to the community of Muḥammad He gave to contemplate His own merit — "rejoice in the grace of God and in His mercy." What a distance between the one whose gaze rests upon his own favor and the one whose gaze rests upon the favor of his Lord! To see one's favor calls forth gratitude, but exposes one to the peril of self-satisfaction; to see the favor of the Real calls forth praise and delivers one from self.

mots.def: address to the descendants of Jacob.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ن ع م
mots.def: niʿma: grace, divine favor.

mots.racine: ف ض ل / ع ل م
mots.sens: I preferred you above the worlds
mots.def: faḍḍala: to grant a preference.

versets.ar: وَاتَّقُوا يَوْمًا لَّا تَجْزِي نَفْسٌ عَن نَّفْسٍ شَيْئًا وَلَا يُقْبَلُ مِنْهَا شَفَاعَةٌ وَلَا يُؤْخَذُ مِنْهَا عَدْلٌ وَلَا هُمْ يُنصَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ttaqū yawman lā tajzī nafsun ʿan nafsin shayʾan wa-lā yuqbalu minhā shafāʿatun wa-lā yuʾkhadhu minhā ʿadlun wa-lā hum yunṣarūna
versets.trad: Fear a Day when no soul shall avail another in any way, when no intercession and no ransom shall be accepted from it, and when none shall be helped.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī grades fear according to the ranks: the common He frightens through His acts — "fear a Day," "fear the Fire"; the elect He frightens through His attributes — "act, for God will see your work"; and the elect of the elect He frightens through Himself — "God warns you of Himself." The nearer the servant draws, the more the fear is purified: from trembling before the chastisement to reverence before the very Person of the Real.

mots.racine: و ق ي / ي و م
mots.def: ittaqā: to guard against, to fear.

mots.ar: لَّا تَجْزِي نَفْسٌ عَن نَّفْسٍ شَيْئًا
mots.tr: lā tajzī nafsun ʿan nafsin shayʾan
mots.racine: ج ز ي / ن ف س
mots.sens: no soul shall avail another in anything
mots.def: jazā: to stand in stead of, to satisfy.

mots.racine: ق ب ل / ش ف ع
mots.sens: no intercession shall be accepted from it
mots.def: shafāʿa: intercession.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ نَجَّيْنَاكُم مِّنْ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَسُومُونَكُمْ سُوءَ الْعَذَابِ يُذَبِّحُونَ أَبْنَاءَكُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيُونَ نِسَاءَكُمْ ۚ وَفِي ذَٰلِكُم بَلَاءٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-idh najjaynākum min āli firʿawna yasūmūnakum sūʾa l-ʿadhābi yudhabbiḥūna abnāʾakum wa-yastaḥyūna nisāʾakum wa-fī dhālikum balāʾun min rabbikum ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: And remember when We delivered you from the people of Pharaoh, who afflicted you with the worst of torments, slaughtering your sons and sparing your women. In that was a tremendous trial from your Lord.
themes: the trial as a veiled grace
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever bears patiently, for God's sake, the torment of His enemies, God rewards him with the company of His beloved and floods him with the beauty of His gifts. Thus the Children of Israel, having endured the malice of Pharaoh, received prophets, kings, and graces granted to no other world. As for "a tremendous trial": it has been read at times as a blessing, at times as a harsh affliction. Yet in truth, that which comes from God and outwardly appears as affliction is, for the one who knows Him, a grace and a favour.

mots.sens: We delivered you from the people of Pharaoh
mots.def: najjā: to rescue, to save.

mots.racine: ذ ب ح / ب ن ي
mots.def: dhabbaḥa: to massacre en masse.

mots.racine: ب ل و / ع ظ م
mots.sens: a tremendous trial from your Lord
mots.def: balāʾ: trial, which may be grace or affliction.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ فَرَقْنَا بِكُمُ الْبَحْرَ فَأَنجَيْنَاكُمْ وَأَغْرَقْنَا آلَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَأَنتُمْ تَنظُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh faraqnā bikumu l-baḥra fa-anjaynākum wa-aghraqnā āla firʿawna wa-antum tanẓurūna
versets.trad: And remember when We parted the sea for you and saved you, and drowned the people of Pharaoh before your very eyes.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the inner vision of the Children of Israel was short, so He showed them wonders to the naked eye; but the vision of this community is piercing, so He unveils His signs to them in secret. Such is the way of the Real: the keener the inner sight, the more subtle and veiled the thing becomes for it — the grace of the first was the visible miracle, that of the last, the intimate unveiling.

mots.racine: ف ر ق / ب ح ر
mots.sens: We parted the sea for you
mots.def: faraqa: to separate, to cleave.

mots.sens: We drowned the people of Pharaoh
mots.def: aghraqa: to engulf in water.
naẓara: to see with one's own eyes.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ وَاعَدْنَا مُوسَىٰ أَرْبَعِينَ لَيْلَةً ثُمَّ اتَّخَذْتُمُ الْعِجْلَ مِن بَعْدِهِ وَأَنتُمْ ظَالِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh wāʿadnā mūsā arbaʿīna laylatan thumma ttakhadhtumu l-ʿijla min baʿdihi wa-antum ẓālimūna
versets.trad: And when We made a covenant with Moses for forty nights, you took the calf to worship in his absence; thus were you wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts two communities: the community of Moses, whose prophet was absent for forty days, at once took the calf as an idol and was content with such a thing as a god. But the community of Muḥammad, centuries after its prophet, were it to hear anyone ascribe to its Beloved the least likeness, would not let a single breath of life remain in it rather than tolerate this, though it cost it its very soul.

mots.sens: We appointed for Moses forty nights
mots.def: wāʿada: to agree upon a term.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ع ج ل
mots.def: ʿijl: the golden calf that was worshipped.
ẓālim: one who commits injustice.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ عَفَوْنَا عَنكُم مِّن بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: thumma ʿafawnā ʿankum min baʿdi dhālika laʿallakum tashkurūna
versets.trad: Then, after that, We pardoned you, that perhaps you might be thankful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes a subtle remark: the swiftness of pardon, set against the enormity of the crime, sometimes betrays the slight worth of the one pardoned. As proof, he says, consider the contrast of the divine words: to the Children of Israel who worshipped the calf, "then We pardoned you"; but to this community, "whoever does an atom's weight of evil shall see it" — the demand is in proportion to the dignity.

mots.def: ʿafā: to efface the fault.
after the worship of the calf.
shakara: to render thanks.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ آتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْكِتَابَ وَالْفُرْقَانَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh ātaynā mūsā l-kitāba wa-l-furqāna laʿallakum tahtadūna
versets.trad: And when We gave Moses the Book and the Criterion, that perhaps you might be guided.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the furqān proper to this community is a light within the hearts, by which they discern the true from the false. He recalls the Prophet's word to Wābiṣa, "consult your heart," and this: "beware the discernment of the believer, for he sees by the light of God." And God has said: "if you fear God, He will grant you a criterion" — and that discernment, he adds, is the inheritance of the excellence of conduct they first offered.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ك ت ب
mots.def: kitāb: here the Torah.
furqān: that which separates true from false.
ihtadā: to find the right way.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِ يَا قَوْمِ إِنَّكُمْ ظَلَمْتُمْ أَنفُسَكُم بِاتِّخَاذِكُمُ الْعِجْلَ فَتُوبُوا إِلَىٰ بَارِئِكُمْ فَاقْتُلُوا أَنفُسَكُمْ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ عِندَ بَارِئِكُمْ فَتَابَ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُ هُوَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-idh qāla mūsā li-qawmihi yā qawmi innakum ẓalamtum anfusakum bi-ttikhādhikumu l-ʿijla fa-tūbū ilā bāriʾikum fa-qtulū anfusakum dhālikum khayrun lakum ʿinda bāriʾikum fa-tāba ʿalaykum innahu huwa t-tawwābu r-raḥīmu
versets.trad: Remember when Moses said to his people: "O my people! You have wronged yourselves by taking the calf. Turn back to your Creator and slay your own souls; that is better for you in the sight of your Creator, and He will turn towards you. Truly He is the Ever-Relenting, the Merciful."
themes: the inviolable glory of the Real
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: you have harmed none but yourselves by the sins you committed; as for the Real, glorious in His attribute, nothing of the injustice of the unjust touches His glory. Then this warning about the inner idol: whoever follows his passion and pursues his desire has made of that desire his calf — he has hung his concern upon it and devoted to it the whole of his aim. To slay one's soul is to sacrifice within oneself this hidden cult.

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ن ف س
mots.def: ẓalama n-nafs: to harm one's own soul.
a command of immolation, also read as the death of the ego.

mots.racine: ت و ب / ر ح م
mots.sens: He is the Ever-Relenting, the Merciful
mots.def: tawwāb: who turns again and again towards the penitent.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قُلْتُمْ يَا مُوسَىٰ لَن نُّؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّىٰ نَرَى اللَّهَ جَهْرَةً فَأَخَذَتْكُمُ الصَّاعِقَةُ وَأَنتُمْ تَنظُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh qultum yā mūsā lan nuʾmina laka ḥattā narā llāha jahratan fa-akhadhatkumu ṣ-ṣāʿiqatu wa-antum tanẓurūna
versets.trad: And when you said: "O Moses! We will not believe you until we see God openly." Then the thunderbolt seized you while you looked on.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses it thus: to claim a vision of the Essence without a prior divine grace is to proclaim the abandonment of reverence; and this counts among the signs of estrangement and misfortune. The thunderbolt chastised their audacity: one does not seize the vision of the Real as a thing owed; it is a gift, not a conquest.

mots.def: a categorical refusal to believe.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / ج ه ر
mots.def: jahra: in the open, visibly.
ṣāʿiqa: thunderbolt, a stunning blow.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَاكُم مِّن بَعْدِ مَوْتِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: thumma baʿathnākum min baʿdi mawtikum laʿallakum tashkurūna
versets.trad: Then We raised you up after your death, that perhaps you might be thankful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He restored them to sensation after the assaults of the chastisement had spent them — by a respite granted according to His wisdom, and so that the custom of pardoning the fault might run its course. And he concludes upon the divine nobility: it is of the generosity of the Most High to cast a veil over the failings of those who serve Him.

mots.def: baʿatha: to revive, to bring back to life.
mawt: the death that followed the thunderbolt.
a renewed call to gratitude.

versets.ar: وَظَلَّلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ الْغَمَامَ وَأَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَنَّ وَالسَّلْوَىٰ ۖ كُلُوا مِن طَيِّبَاتِ مَا رَزَقْنَاكُمْ ۖ وَمَا ظَلَمُونَا وَلَٰكِن كَانُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ẓallalnā ʿalaykumu l-ghamāma wa-anzalnā ʿalaykumu l-manna wa-s-salwā kulū min ṭayyibāti mā razaqnākum wa-mā ẓalamūnā wa-lākin kānū anfusahum yaẓlimūna
versets.trad: And We spread the white cloud over you as shade, and sent down upon you the manna and the quails, saying: "Eat of the good things We have provided for you." They did not wrong Us, but it was themselves they wronged.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the divine solicitude in the wilderness: when He cast them into the wanderings of exile, He was content with nothing less than to shade them, to clothe them in the garments of sufficiency, to spare them the toil of earning, and to take care of all that they needed — their hair did not grow, their garments did not wear out, the sun did not burn them. And such is His custom, he says: for the one He severs from his own choosing, what He chooses is better than what man would choose for himself.

mots.racine: ظ ل ل / غ م م
mots.sens: We shaded you with the cloud
mots.def: ghamām: a bright cloud casting shade.

mots.racine: م ن ن / س ل و
mots.def: nourishment sent down from heaven.
injustice falls back only upon themselves.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قُلْنَا ادْخُلُوا هَٰذِهِ الْقَرْيَةَ فَكُلُوا مِنْهَا حَيْثُ شِئْتُمْ رَغَدًا وَادْخُلُوا الْبَابَ سُجَّدًا وَقُولُوا حِطَّةٌ نَّغْفِرْ لَكُمْ خَطَايَاكُمْ ۚ وَسَنَزِيدُ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idh qulnā dkhulū hādhihi l-qaryata fa-kulū minhā ḥaythu shiʾtum raghadan wa-dkhulū l-bāba sujjadan wa-qūlū ḥiṭṭatun naghfir lakum khaṭāyākum wa-sa-nazīdu l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: Remember when We said: "Enter this town and eat freely of whatever you wish therein. Enter the gate prostrating and say: 'Relieve us!' We shall forgive you your faults, and We shall increase the reward of those who do good."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī shows the Children of Israel spoiling every command: they were enjoined a word of pardon, and they changed it; they were commanded to enter prostrate, and they reversed the posture. They exposed themselves to the arrows of the Mystery without being able to bear their shaft, and rushed towards the sanction without standing firm under the shock of its coming. The gate of humility they did not know how to enter.

mots.racine: د خ ل / س ج د
mots.def: sujjad: in a posture of humble submission.
ḥiṭṭa: a plea for the effacement of faults.

mots.racine: ز ي د / ح س ن
mots.sens: and We shall increase the doers of good
mots.def: muḥsin: one who acts with excellence.

versets.ar: فَبَدَّلَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا قَوْلًا غَيْرَ الَّذِي قِيلَ لَهُمْ فَأَنزَلْنَا عَلَى الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا رِجْزًا مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ بِمَا كَانُوا يَفْسُقُونَ
versets.tr: fa-baddala lladhīna ẓalamū qawlan ghayra lladhī qīla lahum fa-anzalnā ʿalā lladhīna ẓalamū rijzan mina s-samāʾi bi-mā kānū yafsuqūna
versets.trad: But the wrongdoers substituted other words for those that had been spoken to them, so We sent down upon the wrongdoers a harsh affliction from heaven for the perversity they had committed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they could neither repel the gate of heaven by their cunning, nor avert from themselves, by the states they leaned upon, the causes of affliction. When the tooth of pain bit them, they took refuge in remorse; but it was too late for this to avail them, for it was a matter beyond all reckoning — regret coming after the event does not undo the decree.

mots.racine: ب د ل / ظ ل م
mots.def: baddala: to replace one thing with another.
rijz: chastisement, calamity.
fasaqa: to depart from obedience.

versets.ar: وَإِذِ اسْتَسْقَىٰ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِ فَقُلْنَا اضْرِب بِّعَصَاكَ الْحَجَرَ ۖ فَانفَجَرَتْ مِنْهُ اثْنَتَا عَشْرَةَ عَيْنًا ۖ قَدْ عَلِمَ كُلُّ أُنَاسٍ مَّشْرَبَهُمْ ۖ كُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا مِن رِّزْقِ اللَّهِ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idhi staqā mūsā li-qawmihi fa-qulnā ḍrib bi-ʿaṣāka l-ḥajara fa-nfajarat minhu thnatā ʿashrata ʿaynan qad ʿalima kullu unāsin mashrabahum kulū wa-shrabū min rizqi llāhi wa-lā taʿthaw fī l-arḍi mufsidīna
versets.trad: Remember when Moses asked for water for his people, and We said: "Strike the rock with your staff." Twelve springs gushed forth from it, and each group knew its drinking place. "Eat and drink of what God has provided, and do not roam the earth spreading corruption."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He who could draw water from the mute rock could have quenched their thirst with no water at all; but He willed to manifest the imprint of the miracle and to grant Moses, in the wielding of the staff, his share of effort. Then this page upon the ways: the springs are diverse, the drinking places distinct — sweet and clear water, brackish water, pure water, murky water; and each goes to its own. He assigned to every tribe its mark, so that none would crowd at another's drinking place: such is the wisdom that orders the states of men towards God.

mots.sens: Moses asked for water for his people
mots.def: istasqā: to implore water.

mots.racine: ض ر ب / ع ص و / ح ج ر
mots.sens: strike the rock with your staff
mots.def: ʿaṣā: the staff of Moses.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ش ر ب
