mots.sens: each group knew its drinking place
mots.def: mashrab: the place where one drinks, one's share.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قُلْتُمْ يَا مُوسَىٰ لَن نَّصْبِرَ عَلَىٰ طَعَامٍ وَاحِدٍ فَادْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُخْرِجْ لَنَا مِمَّا تُنبِتُ الْأَرْضُ مِن بَقْلِهَا وَقِثَّائِهَا وَفُومِهَا وَعَدَسِهَا وَبَصَلِهَا ۖ قَالَ أَتَسْتَبْدِلُونَ الَّذِي هُوَ أَدْنَىٰ بِالَّذِي هُوَ خَيْرٌ ۚ اهْبِطُوا مِصْرًا فَإِنَّ لَكُم مَّا سَأَلْتُمْ ۗ وَضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الذِّلَّةُ وَالْمَسْكَنَةُ وَبَاءُوا بِغَضَبٍ مِّنَ اللَّهِ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا يَكْفُرُونَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَيَقْتُلُونَ النَّبِيِّينَ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا عَصَوا وَّكَانُوا يَعْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh qultum yā mūsā lan naṣbira ʿalā ṭaʿāmin wāḥidin fa-dʿu lanā rabbaka yukhrij lanā mimmā tunbitu l-arḍu min baqlihā wa-qiththāʾihā wa-fūmihā wa-ʿadasihā wa-baṣalihā qāla a-tastabdilūna lladhī huwa adnā bi-lladhī huwa khayrun ihbiṭū miṣran fa-inna lakum mā saʾaltum wa-ḍuribat ʿalayhimu dh-dhillatu wa-l-maskanatu wa-bāʾū bi-ghaḍabin mina llāhi dhālika bi-annahum kānū yakfurūna bi-āyāti llāhi wa-yaqtulūna n-nabiyyīna bi-ghayri l-ḥaqqi dhālika bi-mā ʿaṣaw wa-kānū yaʿtadūna
versets.trad: And when you said: "O Moses! We can no longer endure a single food. Call upon your Lord for us, that He may bring forth for us some of what the earth grows: its herbs, cucumbers, garlic, lentils, and onions." He said: "Would you exchange the better for what is worse? Then go down into Egypt, and there you shall have what you ask for!" Humiliation and misery were stamped upon them, and they incurred the wrath of God; this because they disbelieved in the signs of God and slew the prophets unjustly, and because they were disobedient and transgressing.
themes: the base preferred to the noble
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they did not accept the beautiful choice He made for them, nor were they patient under His provision of their food and their clothing; so, in their straying, they descended towards what habit made them prefer — the base food, the mean condition. He then returned them to the trial of debasement and bound them to abandonment. And he adds, upon their inner dispersion: the Children of Israel had scattered cares and dispersed aims — they could not be content with a single food, just as they could not suffice themselves with a single Adored One.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / ط ع م
mots.sens: we will not endure a single food
mots.def: a refusal to be content with the manna.

mots.ar: أَتَسْتَبْدِلُونَ الَّذِي هُوَ أَدْنَىٰ بِالَّذِي هُوَ خَيْرٌ
mots.tr: a-tastabdilūna lladhī huwa adnā bi-lladhī huwa khayrun
mots.racine: ب د ل / د ن و / خ ي ر
mots.sens: would you exchange the lesser for the better
mots.def: adnā: lower, baser.

mots.racine: ض ر ب / ذ ل ل / س ك ن
mots.sens: humiliation and misery were stamped upon them
mots.def: dhilla: abasement; maskana: destitution.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَالَّذِينَ هَادُوا وَالنَّصَارَىٰ وَالصَّابِئِينَ مَنْ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا فَلَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna āmanū wa-lladhīna hādū wa-n-naṣārā wa-ṣ-ṣābiʾīna man āmana bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-ʿamila ṣāliḥan fa-lahum ajruhum ʿinda rabbihim wa-lā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūna
versets.trad: Truly, those who believe, and those who follow the Jewish faith, and the Christians and the Sabeans — whoever believes in God and the Last Day and works righteousness — these shall have their reward with their Lord, and no fear shall be upon them, nor shall they grieve.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the diversity of ways, when the foundation is one, does not bar the beautiful welcome; whoever bears witness to the Real in His signs and believes what He has announced of His right and His attributes, the diversity of laws in no way diminishes his merit of acceptance. The believer, he says, is the one who abides within the safekeeping of the Real; and whoever is in His safekeeping, it is fitting that he have upon him neither fear nor sorrow.

mots.sens: those who follow the Jewish faith
mots.def: hāda: to practise Judaism.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ي و م
mots.sens: whoever believes in God and the Last Day
mots.def: the condition of acceptance, beyond mere belonging.

mots.ar: وَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
mots.tr: wa-lā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūna
mots.racine: خ و ف / ح ز ن
mots.sens: no fear upon them, nor shall they grieve
mots.def: the security of those placed under divine protection.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَاقَكُمْ وَرَفَعْنَا فَوْقَكُمُ الطُّورَ خُذُوا مَا آتَيْنَاكُم بِقُوَّةٍ وَاذْكُرُوا مَا فِيهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh akhadhnā mīthāqakum wa-rafaʿnā fawqakumu ṭ-ṭūra khudhū mā ātaynākum bi-quwwatin wa-dhkurū mā fīhi laʿallakum tattaqūna
versets.trad: [O Children of Israel, remember:] We took your covenant and raised the Mount above you, saying: "Hold fast to what We have given you and remember what is in it, that perhaps you may be God-fearing."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He took the covenant of all beings held to accountability; but some answered willingly, because He made Himself known to them and they declared His Oneness; others answered under compulsion, because He veiled Himself from them and they denied Him. No proof is stronger than the sight of the Mount raised above their heads; but they were deprived of the light of inner vision — and to whom inner sight is wanting, the sight of the eyes does not profit.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / و ث ق
mots.def: mīthāq: a solemn pledge.

mots.racine: ر ف ع / ط و ر
mots.sens: We raised the Mount above you
mots.def: ṭūr: the mountain (Sinai).

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ق و ي
mots.sens: hold fast to what We have given you
mots.def: bi-quwwa: with resolve.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ تَوَلَّيْتُم مِّن بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ فَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ لَكُنتُم مِّنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: thumma tawallaytum min baʿdi dhālika fa-lawlā faḍlu llāhi ʿalaykum wa-raḥmatuhu la-kuntum mina l-khāsirīna
versets.trad: Thereafter you turned away. Had it not been for the grace of God upon you and His mercy, you would have been among the losers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: you returned to disobedience after beholding these signs with your own eyes. And were it not for His decree to be patient with you and His forbearance that overwhelms you, He would have hastened the chastisement upon you and brought down the immensity of calamity, and your transaction would have been wholly a loss. The grace that defers is here the sole rampart against ruin.

mots.def: tawallā: to turn away, to turn about.

mots.racine: ف ض ل / ر ح م
mots.sens: had it not been for God's grace and His mercy
mots.def: faḍl: favour; raḥma: mercy.

mots.sens: you would have been among the losers
mots.def: khāsir: one who suffers total loss.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْتُمُ الَّذِينَ اعْتَدَوْا مِنكُمْ فِي السَّبْتِ فَقُلْنَا لَهُمْ كُونُوا قِرَدَةً خَاسِئِينَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ʿalimtumu lladhīna ʿtadaw minkum fī s-sabti fa-qulnā lahum kūnū qiradatan khāsiʾīna
versets.trad: You have surely known those among you who transgressed the Sabbath, to whom We said: "Be abject apes!"
versets.commentaire: The verse strikes those who schemed with the prohibition of the Sabbath: when a man abdicates his dignity of worship to rush upon his craving, his inner form unravels in the measure of his fall. For Qushayrī, the metamorphosis of the body is but the outward sign of a graver inner metamorphosis: the one whom passion lowers to the rank of the beast has first taken on its face within his heart.

mots.racine: ع د و / س ب ت
mots.sens: those among you who transgressed the Sabbath
mots.def: sabt: the sacred day of rest.

mots.racine: ك و ن / ق ر د / خ س أ
mots.def: khāsiʾ: cast down, driven off with contempt.

versets.ar: فَجَعَلْنَاهَا نَكَالًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهَا وَمَا خَلْفَهَا وَمَوْعِظَةً لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: fa-jaʿalnāhā nakālan li-mā bayna yadayhā wa-mā khalfahā wa-mawʿiẓatan li-l-muttaqīna
versets.trad: We made of it an exemplary punishment for their contemporaries and for those who came after, and an admonition for those who fear God.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: such is the lot of the one tried by abandonment and marked with the seal of forsaking: his states become a lesson, and, beholding his own downfall, he drinks the cup of regret. The wretch, once exalted, becomes after his glory an object of derision to the basest. Such are the traces of the wrath of kings and the turning-away of masters from the lowly: the example is set up so that those who fear may tremble.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ن ك ل
mots.sens: We made of it an exemplary punishment
mots.def: nakāl: a deterrent penalty.

mots.racine: ي د ي / خ ل ف
mots.sens: for the contemporaries and the posterity
mots.def: that which precedes and that which follows.

mots.racine: و ع ظ / و ق ي
mots.sens: and an admonition for the God-fearing
mots.def: mawʿiẓa: edifying warning.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَن تَذْبَحُوا بَقَرَةً ۖ قَالُوا أَتَتَّخِذُنَا هُزُوًا ۖ قَالَ أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ الْجَاهِلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idh qāla mūsā li-qawmihi inna llāha yaʾmurukum an tadhbaḥū baqaratan qālū a-tattakhidhunā huzuwan qāla aʿūdhu bi-llāhi an akūna mina l-jāhilīna
versets.trad: And remember when Moses said to his people: "Truly, God commands you to slaughter a cow." They said: "Are you mocking us?" He replied: "God forbid that I should be among the ignorant!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their duty was to receive the command by embracing it; but they lingered in questions, supposing they might thereby win some exemption from the charge — and the burden multiplied upon them, and there befell them the disgrace they had feared. In place of prompt obedience, the bargaining mind ensnares itself. To embrace the command is to spare oneself the trap of the soul's recoilings.

mots.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَن تَذْبَحُوا بَقَرَةً
mots.tr: inna llāha yaʾmurukum an tadhbaḥū baqaratan
mots.racine: أ م ر / ذ ب ح / ب ق ر
mots.sens: God commands you to slaughter a cow
mots.def: baqara: cow (the title of the sura).

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ه ز أ
mots.sens: are you taking us in mockery
mots.def: huzuw: mockery, derision.

mots.ar: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ الْجَاهِلِينَ
mots.tr: aʿūdhu bi-llāhi an akūna mina l-jāhilīna
mots.racine: ع و ذ / ج ه ل
mots.sens: I take refuge in God from being among the ignorant
mots.def: jāhil: ignorant, one who mocks.

versets.ar: قَالُوا ادْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُبَيِّن لَّنَا مَا هِيَ ۚ قَالَ إِنَّهُ يَقُولُ إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ لَّا فَارِضٌ وَلَا بِكْرٌ عَوَانٌ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ فَافْعَلُوا مَا تُؤْمَرُونَ
versets.tr: qālū dʿu lanā rabbaka yubayyin lanā mā hiya qāla innahu yaqūlu innahā baqaratun lā fāriḍun wa-lā bikrun ʿawānun bayna dhālika fa-fʿalū mā tuʾmarūna
versets.trad: They said: "Call upon your Lord for us, that He may make clear to us what it should be." He answered: "He says it is a cow neither too old nor too young, but of middling age. So do what you are commanded!"
themes: the way of the just mean
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from the cow's age "between the two" a sign: the one fit for this Way is the one whom no extreme carries off — neither the greenness without experience, nor the senescence without ardour, but a poised equilibrium. "So do what you are commanded": deliverance lay not in the surplus of questions but in simple execution. The soul prolix in demands postpones the hour of obedience and weighs down its own burden.

mots.ar: ادْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُبَيِّن لَّنَا مَا هِيَ
mots.tr: udʿu lanā rabbaka yubayyin lanā mā hiya
mots.racine: د ع و / ب ي ن
mots.sens: call upon your Lord to make clear to us what it is
mots.def: bayyana: to set forth clearly.

mots.racine: ف ر ض / ب ك ر
mots.sens: neither old nor a young heifer
mots.def: fāriḍ: aged; bikr: very young.

mots.sens: of middling age, between the two
mots.def: ʿawān: of middle age.

versets.ar: قَالُوا ادْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُبَيِّن لَّنَا مَا لَوْنُهَا ۚ قَالَ إِنَّهُ يَقُولُ إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ صَفْرَاءُ فَاقِعٌ لَّوْنُهَا تَسُرُّ النَّاظِرِينَ
versets.tr: qālū dʿu lanā rabbaka yubayyin lanā mā lawnuhā qāla innahu yaqūlu innahā baqaratun ṣafrāʾu fāqiʿun lawnuhā tasurru n-nāẓirīna
versets.trad: They said: "Call upon your Lord for us, that He may make clear to us what its colour should be." He answered: "He says it is a cow of a bright yellow, its colour gladdening to the beholders."
themes: the one whose sight recalls God
versets.commentaire: From this hue that "gladdens those who behold," Qushayrī draws an indication: the one who is of the people of this story enraptures hearts by that with which he is clothed of the mantle of the divine majesty and by the witness of the Mystery raised within him — so much so that whoever beholds him forgets the states of humanity and is overcome by the remembrance of the Real. Such, he says, citing the tradition, is the friend of God: those whose sight recalls God.

mots.def: lawn: colour, hue.

mots.racine: ص ف ر / ف ق ع
mots.def: fāqiʿ: of a bright, pure yellow.

mots.racine: س ر ر / ن ظ ر
mots.sens: it gladdens those who behold it
mots.def: sarra: to gladden, to charm.

versets.ar: قَالُوا ادْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُبَيِّن لَّنَا مَا هِيَ إِنَّ الْبَقَرَ تَشَابَهَ عَلَيْنَا وَإِنَّا إِن شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَمُهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: qālū dʿu lanā rabbaka yubayyin lanā mā hiya inna l-baqara tashābaha ʿalaynā wa-innā in shāʾa llāhu la-muhtadūna
versets.trad: They said: "Call upon your Lord for us, that He may make clear to us what it should be, for to us all cows look alike; and, if God wills, we shall indeed be guided."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī pursues the indication: just as its colour captivated the gazes, so the one who belongs to this story absorbs the hearts; but here the confession "the cows look alike to us" betrays the veil that thickens through procrastination. Yet the "if God wills" is a gleam: none is guided save by the divine will, and it is by entrusting oneself to it that the way is reopened.

mots.racine: ب ق ر / ش ب ه
mots.sens: the cows look alike to us
mots.def: tashābaha: to become confused, alike.
a clause of submission to the divine will.
muhtadī: one who finds guidance.

versets.ar: قَالَ إِنَّهُ يَقُولُ إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ لَّا ذَلُولٌ تُثِيرُ الْأَرْضَ وَلَا تَسْقِي الْحَرْثَ مُسَلَّمَةٌ لَّا شِيَةَ فِيهَا ۚ قَالُوا الْآنَ جِئْتَ بِالْحَقِّ ۚ فَذَبَحُوهَا وَمَا كَادُوا يَفْعَلُونَ
versets.tr: qāla innahu yaqūlu innahā baqaratun lā dhalūlun tuthīru l-arḍa wa-lā tasqī l-ḥartha musallamatun lā shiyata fīhā qālū l-āna jiʾta bi-l-ḥaqqi fa-dhabaḥūhā wa-mā kādū yafʿalūna
versets.trad: He answered: "He says it is a cow not broken in to plough the earth or to water the fields, sound and without blemish." They said: "Now you have brought the truth," and they slaughtered it, though they had nearly failed to do so.
themes: the soul freed from secondary causes
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this cow "that no labour has tamed, without blemish" the portrait of the saints: the people of sanctity have not abased themselves before creatures to obtain their desires; their hearts have not inclined towards any form or likeness; they have relied neither on choosing nor on cunning. No prey for cravings, no quarry for the claw of this lower world, no dominion of passion over them: they stand by God, annihilated to all other than Him; their Adored One is God, and likewise their sole aim is God.

mots.racine: ذ ل ل / ث و ر
mots.sens: not broken in to plough the earth
mots.def: dhalūl: trained, subdued to labour.

mots.racine: س ل م / و ش ي
mots.def: shiya: a mark of colour, a flaw.

mots.racine: ذ ب ح / ك و د
mots.sens: they slaughtered it, having nearly failed to
mots.def: kāda: to be on the point of.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَتَلْتُمْ نَفْسًا فَادَّارَأْتُمْ فِيهَا ۖ وَاللَّهُ مُخْرِجٌ مَّا كُنتُمْ تَكْتُمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh qataltum nafsan fa-ddāraʾtum fīhā wa-llāhu mukhrijun mā kuntum taktumūna
versets.trad: And remember when you slew a man and disputed about it among yourselves; but God brought to light what you were concealing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the traitor is fearful, and in dread that his secret should burst forth he takes refuge in disguise and trickery, in denial and disavowal; but inevitably his blemish is discovered, his secrets brought to light, and the veils torn from the ugliness of his deed. For God, he says, brings forth what you used to conceal: nothing remains hidden before the One who sees the inward.

mots.racine: ق ت ل / ن ف س
mots.def: qatala nafsan: to take a life.

mots.sens: you cast the blame upon one another over it
mots.def: iddāraʾa: to throw the accusation back and forth.

mots.racine: خ ر ج / ك ت م
mots.sens: God brings to light what you were concealing
mots.def: mukhrij: who brings forth into the open.

versets.ar: فَقُلْنَا اضْرِبُوهُ بِبَعْضِهَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُحْيِي اللَّهُ الْمَوْتَىٰ وَيُرِيكُمْ آيَاتِهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-qulnā ḍribūhu bi-baʿḍihā kadhālika yuḥyī llāhu l-mawtā wa-yurīkum āyātihi laʿallakum taʿqilūna
versets.trad: So We said: "Strike the slain man with a part of it." Thus does God give life to the dead and show you His signs, that perhaps you may understand.
themes: to die in order to live
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from this dead man revived by a limb of the slaughtered beast a figure of the way: God willed to revive their dead man so that he might name his murderer, and made of the slaughter of a beast the cause of that life. Hence the indication: whoever desires the life of his heart attains it only through the immolation of his soul; whoever slaughters his soul by spiritual combats sees his heart revive by the lights of contemplation. Likewise, he says, the one whose name God wills to keep alive among the saintly substitutes, He first extinguishes his renown here below by effacement.

mots.racine: ض ر ب / ب ع ض
mots.sens: strike him with a part of it
mots.def: baʿḍ: a part of the cow.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: thus God gives life to the dead
mots.def: aḥyā: to bring back to life.
āya: sign, proof.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ قَسَتْ قُلُوبُكُم مِّن بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ فَهِيَ كَالْحِجَارَةِ أَوْ أَشَدُّ قَسْوَةً ۚ وَإِنَّ مِنَ الْحِجَارَةِ لَمَا يَتَفَجَّرُ مِنْهُ الْأَنْهَارُ ۚ وَإِنَّ مِنْهَا لَمَا يَشَّقَّقُ فَيَخْرُجُ مِنْهُ الْمَاءُ ۚ وَإِنَّ مِنْهَا لَمَا يَهْبِطُ مِنْ خَشْيَةِ اللَّهِ ۗ وَمَا اللَّهُ بِغَافِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: thumma qasat qulūbukum min baʿdi dhālika fa-hiya ka-l-ḥijārati aw ashaddu qaswatan wa-inna mina l-ḥijārati la-mā yatafajjaru minhu l-anhāru wa-inna minhā la-mā yashshaqqaqu fa-yakhruju minhu l-māʾu wa-inna minhā la-mā yahbiṭu min khashyati llāhi wa-mā llāhu bi-ghāfilin ʿammā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: Thereafter your hearts hardened; they became like rock, or harder still. For there are rocks from which streams gush forth, and there are some that split so that water flows out, and there are some that fall down for fear of God. And God is not heedless of what you do.
themes: the stone tenderer than the heart
versets.commentaire: The verse sets the hardness of the heart against the very stone: there are rocks that the fear of God makes humble themselves, while these hearts, after so many signs beheld, remain harder than rock. For the Sufi way, this is the supreme warning: the stone splits and lets water gush forth, but the heart sealed by the habit of refusal yields no spring at all. And God, the verse concludes, is not heedless of what you do: the very hardness is beneath His gaze.

mots.racine: ق س و / ق ل ب
mots.def: qasā: to become hard, dry.

mots.racine: ح ج ر / ش د د
mots.def: an aggravated comparison.

mots.racine: ه ب ط / خ ش ي
mots.sens: that falls down for fear of God
mots.def: khashya: reverential awe.

versets.ar: أَفَتَطْمَعُونَ أَن يُؤْمِنُوا لَكُمْ وَقَدْ كَانَ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُمْ يَسْمَعُونَ كَلَامَ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ يُحَرِّفُونَهُ مِن بَعْدِ مَا عَقَلُوهُ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-taṭmaʿūna an yuʾminū lakum wa-qad kāna farīqun minhum yasmaʿūna kalāma llāhi thumma yuḥarrifūnahu min baʿdi mā ʿaqalūhu wa-hum yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Do you then hope that these people will believe you, when a party of them used to hear the Word of God, then alter it knowingly after they had understood it?
themes: the one who does not revere God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: after hearing the very speech of God Himself, they distorted and changed it; how then would they believe you, who receive only through the intermediary of the prophetic mission? And this penetrating sentence: the one who has not remained in faith after the evidence, how shall he believe upon proof? the one who has had no shame before the Real, how should he have shame before you?

mots.racine: ط م ع / أ م ن
mots.sens: do you hope that they will believe you
mots.def: ṭamiʿa: to covet, to hope.

mots.racine: س م ع / ك ل م
mots.sens: they used to hear the Word of God
mots.def: kalām Allāh: the divine Word.
ḥarrafa: to distort, to falsify.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا لَقُوا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قَالُوا آمَنَّا وَإِذَا خَلَا بَعْضُهُمْ إِلَىٰ بَعْضٍ قَالُوا أَتُحَدِّثُونَهُم بِمَا فَتَحَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمْ لِيُحَاجُّوكُم بِهِ عِندَ رَبِّكُمْ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā laqū lladhīna āmanū qālū āmannā wa-idhā khalā baʿḍuhum ilā baʿḍin qālū a-tuḥaddithūnahum bi-mā fataḥa llāhu ʿalaykum li-yuḥājjūkum bihi ʿinda rabbikum a-fa-lā taʿqilūna
versets.trad: When they meet the believers they say: "We believe!"; but when they are alone among themselves they say: "Will you tell them what God has disclosed to you, so that they may use it as an argument against you before your Lord? Do you not understand?"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ranks these among those who speak of faith with their lips while betraying it within: the accord of the tongue with the discord of conviction increases nothing but separation. To display "we believe" before the believers and recant among themselves is the sign of the divided heart that nothing unifies with the Real.

mots.ar: وَإِذَا لَقُوا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قَالُوا آمَنَّا
mots.tr: wa-idhā laqū lladhīna āmanū qālū āmannā
mots.racine: ل ق ي / أ م ن
mots.sens: meeting the believers, they say: we believe
mots.def: a profession of faith of circumstance.

mots.racine: خ ل و / ب ع ض
mots.sens: when they withdraw alone among themselves
mots.def: khalā ilā: to draw aside privately with.

mots.racine: ح ج ج / ر ب ب
mots.sens: that they may argue against you with it before your Lord
mots.def: ḥājja: to dispute, to argue.

versets.ar: أَوَلَا يَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا يُسِرُّونَ وَمَا يُعْلِنُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-lā yaʿlamūna anna llāha yaʿlamu mā yusirrūna wa-mā yuʿlinūna
versets.trad: Do they not know that God knows what they conceal and what they make public?
themes: the divine knowledge of the secret
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they counselled one another to deny the truth and to veil their state from the Muslims, not knowing that God instructs His Messenger of their secrets — and that a light which the Mystery brings forth is not extinguished by the friction of others. And he recalls: the accord of the tongue with the divergence of belief increases nothing but division. Nothing is hidden from the One who knows the secret and the declared.

mots.def: an interrogation of reproach.
asarra: to conceal within oneself.
aʿlana: to make public.

versets.ar: وَمِنْهُمْ أُمِّيُّونَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ الْكِتَابَ إِلَّا أَمَانِيَّ وَإِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَظُنُّونَ
versets.tr: wa-minhum ummiyyūna lā yaʿlamūna l-kitāba illā amāniyya wa-in hum illā yaẓunnūna
versets.trad: Among them are the unlettered who know nothing of the Book but hearsay, and they do nothing but conjecture.
versets.commentaire: The verse paints those who have of the Book only "wishes" (amānī) — a knowledge of the ear, without real grasp. In the spirit of Qushayrī, they are the opposite of the man of unveiling: where the saint knows by the light cast into the heart, these possess only conjectures which they take for knowledge. The way of the Real is not content with the approximations of rumour.

mots.def: ummī: one who can neither read nor write.

mots.racine: ع ل م / م ن ي
mots.sens: knowing of the Book only wishes
mots.def: amānī: tales and desires without foundation.
ẓanna: to suppose without certainty.

versets.ar: فَوَيْلٌ لِّلَّذِينَ يَكْتُبُونَ الْكِتَابَ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ ثُمَّ يَقُولُونَ هَٰذَا مِنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ لِيَشْتَرُوا بِهِ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۖ فَوَيْلٌ لَّهُم مِّمَّا كَتَبَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَوَيْلٌ لَّهُم مِّمَّا يَكْسِبُونَ
versets.tr: fa-waylun li-lladhīna yaktubūna l-kitāba bi-aydīhim thumma yaqūlūna hādhā min ʿindi llāhi li-yashtarū bihi thamanan qalīlan fa-waylun lahum mimmā katabat aydīhim wa-waylun lahum mimmā yaksibūna
versets.trad: Woe to those who write the Book with their own hands and then say: "This is from God!" so as to trade it for a paltry price. Woe to them, then, for what their hands have written, and woe to them for the gain they have made.
themes: the bartering of the soul at a vile price
versets.commentaire: The verse pronounces woe (wayl) upon those who forge a writing with their hands and ascribe it to God for a wretched gain. To the Sufi sensibility of Qushayrī, this is the gravest betrayal: to exchange the immensity of the right of God for the paltry portion of this world, as he said of verse 41. The double "woe" strikes both the act of the hand and the gain drawn from it: the falsification of the sacred ruins the one who engages in it.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ي د ي
mots.sens: they write the Book with their hands
mots.def: a falsification ascribed to God.
a lie about the divine source.

mots.racine: ش ر ي / ث م ن
mots.sens: to barter it for a vile price
mots.def: thaman qalīl: a paltry gain of this world.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا لَن تَمَسَّنَا النَّارُ إِلَّا أَيَّامًا مَّعْدُودَةً ۚ قُلْ أَتَّخَذْتُمْ عِندَ اللَّهِ عَهْدًا فَلَن يُخْلِفَ اللَّهُ عَهْدَهُ ۖ أَمْ تَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū lan tamassanā n-nāru illā ayyāman maʿdūdatan qul a-ttakhadhtum ʿinda llāhi ʿahdan fa-lan yukhlifa llāhu ʿahdahu am taqūlūna ʿalā llāhi mā lā taʿlamūna
versets.trad: And they said: "The Fire will touch us only for a number of counted days." Say: "Have you taken a pledge with God? For God will never break His pledge. Or do you say of God what you do not know?"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse the portrait of the soul deceived by itself: the one across whose heart wide pretensions have passed, whom his own imagining masters, and who ascribes to himself — through excess of heedlessness — belonging to the people of election. He surrenders to the chimeras of his desires and decrees of the Mystery that it will overlook all in him: he forgets the ugliness of what he has committed and remembers the illusions of what he has supposed — he is a slave of his soul, mastered by his fair opinion of himself, while in truth the fruits of his heedlessness and his cunning overtake him. And God cites: "that opinion which you held of your Lord has ruined you."

mots.ar: لَن تَمَسَّنَا النَّارُ إِلَّا أَيَّامًا مَّعْدُودَةً
mots.tr: lan tamassanā n-nāru illā ayyāman maʿdūdatan
mots.racine: م س س / ن و ر / ع د د
mots.sens: the Fire will touch us only for counted days
mots.def: the pretension of a brief chastisement.

mots.racine: خ ل ف / ع ه د
mots.sens: God will not break His pledge
mots.def: akhlafa: to fail one's promise.

mots.ar: أَمْ تَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
mots.tr: am taqūlūna ʿalā llāhi mā lā taʿlamūna
mots.racine: ق و ل / ع ل م
mots.sens: or do you say of God what you do not know
mots.def: to assert of God without knowledge.

versets.ar: بَلَىٰ مَن كَسَبَ سَيِّئَةً وَأَحَاطَتْ بِهِ خَطِيئَتُهُ فَأُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: balā man kasaba sayyiʾatan wa-aḥāṭat bihi khaṭīʾatuhu fa-ulāʾika aṣḥābu n-nāri hum fīhā khālidūn
versets.trad: Indeed, those who commit a sin and whom their fault encompasses on every side — these are the companions of the Fire; therein they shall abide forever.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the one whom his fault hems in on every side is the man whose heart has no outlet left towards God. But he also warns his own: the people of realities are like grain on the griddle in their hours of lucidity — whoever grows calm and rests upon his own work should rather mistrust it. For the one who leans upon his obedience, supposing he draws near by it, must flee that reliance: "whoever realizes himself in Oneness knows there is no way to Him save through Him."

mots.racine: ح و ط / خ ط أ
mots.def: aḥāṭa: to hem in, to surround entirely.
khulūd: permanence without end.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ الْجَنَّةِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti ulāʾika aṣḥābu l-jannati hum fīhā khālidūn
versets.trad: And those who believe and perform righteous deeds — these are the companions of the Garden; therein they shall abide forever.
versets.commentaire: Where the preceding verse painted the encompassing of the fault, this one opens the encompassing of grace. Qushayrī condenses: from this very moment, to these the gardens of Union (jinān al-waṣl). The beatitude of the elect is not reduced to the future Garden; it begins here below, in the heart united to its Lord — the Paradise being but the unveiling of what was already granted.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح
mots.def: ṣāliḥāt: upright and good actions.

mots.racine: ص ح ب / ج ن ن
mots.def: janna: garden, paradise; veiled (from the root meaning to conceal).

versets.ar: وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَاقَ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ لَا تَعْبُدُونَ إِلَّا اللَّهَ وَبِالْوَالِدَيْنِ إِحْسَانًا وَذِي الْقُرْبَىٰ وَالْيَتَامَىٰ وَالْمَسَاكِينِ وَقُولُوا لِلنَّاسِ حُسْنًا وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكَاةَ ثُمَّ تَوَلَّيْتُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّنكُمْ وَأَنتُم مُّعْرِضُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh akhadhnā mīthāqa banī isrāʾīla lā taʿbudūna illā llāha wa-bi-l-wālidayni iḥsānan wa-dhī l-qurbā wa-l-yatāmā wa-l-masākīni wa-qūlū li-n-nāsi ḥusnan wa-aqīmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātū z-zakāta thumma tawallaytum illā qalīlan minkum wa-antum muʿriḍūn
versets.trad: And remember when We took the covenant of the Children of Israel, saying: "Worship none but God; be good to your parents, to kinsfolk, to orphans, and to the poor. Speak kindly to people; perform the prayer; give the alms!" Then you turned away, save a few of you, and you remained averse.
versets.commentaire: The covenant opens with tawḥīd — to worship none but God — then unfolds its branches: goodness to parents, kin, the orphan, the poor, the fair word. In the spirit of Qushayrī: the first covenant is that of Oneness, and every beauty of conduct is but the outward radiance of a heart that worships none but Him. The turning-away of all save the excepted "few" shows that fidelity remains the lot of an elite.

mots.def: a firm and binding pledge.
a fair and kindly word.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَاقَكُمْ لَا تَسْفِكُونَ دِمَاءَكُمْ وَلَا تُخْرِجُونَ أَنفُسَكُم مِّن دِيَارِكُمْ ثُمَّ أَقْرَرْتُمْ وَأَنتُمْ تَشْهَدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh akhadhnā mīthāqakum lā tasfikūna dimāʾakum wa-lā tukhrijūna anfusakum min diyārikum thumma aqrartum wa-antum tashhadūn
versets.trad: And remember when We took your covenant, saying: "Do not shed your blood; do not drive one another out of your homes." You assented [to Our words] and bore witness thereto.
versets.commentaire: The covenant binds blood and dwelling: not to shed the blood of one's own, not to drive one's own from their homes. In the spirit of Qushayrī, the assent and testimony of which the verse speaks become a seal: the one who acknowledges the covenant and bears witness to it has no excuse left when he betrays it. The word given to God binds the heart before it binds the hand.

mots.racine: س ف ك / د م و
mots.def: safk: the shedding of blood.
iqrār: avowal, acknowledgement.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ أَنتُمْ هَٰؤُلَاءِ تَقْتُلُونَ أَنفُسَكُمْ وَتُخْرِجُونَ فَرِيقًا مِّنكُم مِّن دِيَارِهِمْ تَظَاهَرُونَ عَلَيْهِم بِالْإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوَانِ وَإِن يَأْتُوكُمْ أُسَارَىٰ تُفَادُوهُمْ وَهُوَ مُحَرَّمٌ عَلَيْكُمْ إِخْرَاجُهُمْ ۚ أَفَتُؤْمِنُونَ بِبَعْضِ الْكِتَابِ وَتَكْفُرُونَ بِبَعْضٍ ۚ فَمَا جَزَاءُ مَن يَفْعَلُ ذَٰلِكَ مِنكُمْ إِلَّا خِزْيٌ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا ۖ وَيَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ يُرَدُّونَ إِلَىٰ أَشَدِّ الْعَذَابِ ۗ وَمَا اللَّهُ بِغَافِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: thumma antum hāʾulāʾi taqtulūna anfusakum wa-tukhrijūna farīqan minkum min diyārihim taẓāharūna ʿalayhim bi-l-ithmi wa-l-ʿudwāni wa-in yaʾtūkum usārā tufādūhum wa-huwa muḥarramun ʿalaykum ikhrājuhum a-fa-tuʾminūna bi-baʿḍi l-kitābi wa-takfurūna bi-baʿḍin fa-mā jazāʾu man yafʿalu dhālika minkum illā khizyun fī l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-yawma l-qiyāmati yuraddūna ilā ashaddi l-ʿadhābi wa-mā llāhu bi-ghāfilin ʿammā taʿmalūn
versets.trad: Yet here you are, slaying one another, driving a party of yourselves from their homes, and supporting one another against them in sin and aggression. And if they come to you as captives, you ransom them, though it was forbidden to you to expel them. Do you then believe in one part of the Book and disbelieve in another? What shall be the recompense of those among you who act thus, save disgrace in the life of this world and, on the Day of Resurrection, to be cast back to the most severe of chastisements? And God is not heedless of what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī here unfolds an inner parable of the captives (usārā): every soul is a captive — one drowned in the seas of passion, whom we deliver by orienting it towards guidance; another in the grip of the suggestions of doubt, whom we ransom by the gleams of proofs; yet another bound within its own whisperings, whose bonds we break by turning it towards the contemplation of graces. And he warns: to believe in one part of the Book and deny the other ruins the merit of the deed accomplished, for faith does not divide.

mots.def: to lend mutual aid (here in evil).
plural of asīr; symbol of the captive soul in Qushayrī.
public ignominy.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ اشْتَرَوُا الْحَيَاةَ الدُّنْيَا بِالْآخِرَةِ ۖ فَلَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابُ وَلَا هُمْ يُنصَرُونَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna shtarawu l-ḥayāta d-dunyā bi-l-ākhirati fa-lā yukhaffafu ʿanhumu l-ʿadhābu wa-lā hum yunṣarūn
versets.trad: These are they who have bartered the life to come for the life of this world. Their chastisement shall not be lightened, nor shall they be helped.
versets.commentaire: Whoever prefers anything else to God loses both abodes. Qushayrī cites a verse that tells its spirit: "Some have turned away from us without crime or cause; if they have deemed themselves in no need of us, behold, we are richer than they." To exchange the eternal for the passing is the bargain of loss: the one who has preferred a thing to Him has lost everything, here and hereafter.

mots.def: ishtirāʾ: exchange, barter.
takhfīf: alleviation.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْكِتَابَ وَقَفَّيْنَا مِن بَعْدِهِ بِالرُّسُلِ ۖ وَآتَيْنَا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ الْبَيِّنَاتِ وَأَيَّدْنَاهُ بِرُوحِ الْقُدُسِ ۗ أَفَكُلَّمَا جَاءَكُمْ رَسُولٌ بِمَا لَا تَهْوَىٰ أَنفُسُكُمُ اسْتَكْبَرْتُمْ فَفَرِيقًا كَذَّبْتُمْ وَفَرِيقًا تَقْتُلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ātaynā mūsā l-kitāba wa-qaffaynā min baʿdihi bi-r-rusuli wa-ātaynā ʿīsā bna maryama l-bayyināti wa-ayyadnāhu bi-rūḥi l-qudusi a-fa-kullamā jāʾakum rasūlun bimā lā tahwā anfusukumu stakbartum fa-farīqan kadhdhabtum wa-farīqan taqtulūn
versets.trad: In truth, We gave Moses the Book, and after him We sent a succession of messengers. We gave Jesus, son of Mary, clear proofs and supported him with the Spirit of Holiness. Was it not so that each time a messenger brought you what you did not desire, you grew arrogant — some you called liars, and others you slew?
versets.commentaire: All the messengers called to the One. But Qushayrī pierces the mechanism of refusal: they lent their ear to the callers with the hearing of passion: what the souls relished, they accepted; what weighed upon them, they denied. And he adds, terribly: when passion becomes their attribute and then their idol, the attributes of the worshipper pass over to the worshipped — hence woe upon woe.

mots.def: taqfiya: to send one after another.

mots.racine: ر و ح / ق د س
mots.def: the sanctified breath that supported Jesus.
hawā: the soul's inclination, passion.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا قُلُوبُنَا غُلْفٌ ۚ بَل لَّعَنَهُمُ اللَّهُ بِكُفْرِهِمْ فَقَلِيلًا مَّا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū qulūbunā ghulfun bal laʿanahumu llāhu bi-kufrihim fa-qalīlan mā yuʾminūn
versets.trad: And they said: "Our hearts are sealed enclosures!" Nay, God has cursed them for their disbelief. Little is it that they believe!
versets.commentaire: They plead a closed heart as an excuse. Qushayrī separates the pretension from the reality: if the realities were attained by mere claim, the attainment would be easy; but at the hour of the demands of realization, the fangs of the impostors break against sharpened teeth. And he cites: "When the tears stream down the cheeks, the one who truly weeps is told apart from the one who feigns weeping."

mots.def: plural of aghlaf: veiled, covered with a sheath.
laʿn: removal from mercy.

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا جَاءَهُمْ كِتَابٌ مِّنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ مُصَدِّقٌ لِّمَا مَعَهُمْ وَكَانُوا مِن قَبْلُ يَسْتَفْتِحُونَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَلَمَّا جَاءَهُم مَّا عَرَفُوا كَفَرُوا بِهِ ۚ فَلَعْنَةُ اللَّهِ عَلَى الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā jāʾahum kitābun min ʿindi llāhi muṣaddiqun limā maʿahum wa-kānū min qablu yastaftiḥūna ʿalā lladhīna kafarū fa-lammā jāʾahum mā ʿarafū kafarū bihi fa-laʿnatu llāhi ʿalā l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: When there came to them from God a Book confirming what they already had — though formerly they had prayed for victory over the disbelievers — when there came to them what they recognized, they denied it. So may the curse of God be upon the disbelievers!
versets.commentaire: To recognize, then to deny: such is their tragedy. Qushayrī reads in it the fate of the aspirant who falters: the one who had resolved upon purity, who had promised himself fidelity, who had unfurled the banners of ardour as he advanced to battle — and who collapses the moment the ranks close in, who breaks under the fear of the assault. And he recalls the Word: "when the matter is decided, if they had been sincere towards God, it would have been better for them."

mots.def: that which attests the truth of what precedes.
istiftāḥ: to seek the opening, the triumph.

versets.ar: بِئْسَمَا اشْتَرَوْا بِهِ أَنفُسَهُمْ أَن يَكْفُرُوا بِمَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ بَغْيًا أَن يُنَزِّلَ اللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ ۖ فَبَاءُوا بِغَضَبٍ عَلَىٰ غَضَبٍ ۚ وَلِلْكَافِرِينَ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ
versets.tr: biʾsamā shtaraw bihi anfusahum an yakfurū bimā anzala llāhu baghyan an yunazzila llāhu min faḍlihi ʿalā man yashāʾu min ʿibādihi fa-bāʾū bi-ghaḍabin ʿalā ghaḍabin wa-li-l-kāfirīna ʿadhābun muhīn
versets.trad: How wretched is that for which they have bartered their souls — denying what God has revealed, in resentment that God should send down of His bounty upon whomever He wills of His servants. So they incurred wrath upon wrath. And for the disbelievers there is a humiliating chastisement.
versets.commentaire: The root of their denial is envy. Qushayrī names it with force: jealousy hurled them from the abode of glory to the lowest of abasement, and imposed upon them the humiliation of pettiness, for they did not accept the decree. Thus did they add to an ancient wrath a new wrath — the soul that refuses that grace should descend upon another chastises first of all itself.

mots.sens: out of envy, out of revolt
mots.def: baghy: jealous covetousness, transgression.
bāʾa bi: to return laden with.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ آمِنُوا بِمَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ قَالُوا نُؤْمِنُ بِمَا أُنزِلَ عَلَيْنَا وَيَكْفُرُونَ بِمَا وَرَاءَهُ وَهُوَ الْحَقُّ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا مَعَهُمْ ۗ قُلْ فَلِمَ تَقْتُلُونَ أَنبِيَاءَ اللَّهِ مِن قَبْلُ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā qīla lahum āminū bimā anzala llāhu qālū nuʾminu bimā unzila ʿalaynā wa-yakfurūna bimā warāʾahu wa-huwa l-ḥaqqu muṣaddiqan limā maʿahum qul fa-lima taqtulūna anbiyāʾa llāhi min qablu in kuntum muʾminīn
versets.trad: When it is said to them: "Believe in what God has revealed," they reply: "We believe in what was revealed to us," and they reject what came after, though it is the Truth confirming what they already had. Say: "Why then, if you are believers, did you formerly slay the prophets of God?"
themes: the faith that stops at the self
the rejection of what lies beyond the self
versets.commentaire: They believe only in what belongs to them. Qushayrī unmasks this withdrawal: when they are told to verify by proof the agreement they display, their souls concede what accords with their leanings, then deny all that passes beyond their portion. And this is why they remain far from the circle of the elect, outside the count of those privileged with divine election.

mots.def: that which comes after or in addition.
plural of nabī: bearer of divine tidings.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ جَاءَكُم مُّوسَىٰ بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ ثُمَّ اتَّخَذْتُمُ الْعِجْلَ مِن بَعْدِهِ وَأَنتُمْ ظَالِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad jāʾakum mūsā bi-l-bayyināti thumma ttakhadhtumu l-ʿijla min baʿdihi wa-antum ẓālimūn
versets.trad: Moses came to you with clear proofs; then, in his absence, you worshipped the calf. Thus were you wrongdoers.
themes: the calf that lingered in the heart
versets.commentaire: Moses called to set the Adored One apart from every adored thing and every limit. Qushayrī observes the persistence of the evil: you inclined only towards the worship of what resembles you — a calf you fashioned for yourselves, an idol you coveted. It was taken from your hands, but its traces remained in your hearts and those of your descendants — hence, he says, the tendency to represent God in the measure of things.

mots.def: manifest, evident signs.
the golden idol of the Children of Israel.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَاقَكُمْ وَرَفَعْنَا فَوْقَكُمُ الطُّورَ خُذُوا مَا آتَيْنَاكُم بِقُوَّةٍ وَاسْمَعُوا ۖ قَالُوا سَمِعْنَا وَعَصَيْنَا وَأُشْرِبُوا فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْعِجْلَ بِكُفْرِهِمْ ۚ قُلْ بِئْسَمَا يَأْمُرُكُم بِهِ إِيمَانُكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idh akhadhnā mīthāqakum wa-rafaʿnā fawqakumu ṭ-ṭūra khudhū mā ātaynākum bi-quwwatin wa-smaʿū qālū samiʿnā wa-ʿaṣaynā wa-ushribū fī qulūbihimu l-ʿijla bi-kufrihim qul biʾsamā yaʾmurukum bihi īmānukum in kuntum muʾminīn
versets.trad: Remember when We took your covenant and raised the Mount above you, saying: "Hold fast to what We have given you, and hearken!" They replied: "We have heard, and we have disobeyed." For the calf had soaked into their hearts because of their disbelief. Say: "How wretched is that which your faith commands you, if indeed you are believers!"
versets.commentaire: "We have heard, and we have disobeyed": the scandal of an open ear and a closed heart. Qushayrī notes the obstinacy: neither did counsel penetrate them, nor did chastisement turn them from their faults; neither did blame move them, nor did command make them act. The calf had steeped them to the heart — a striking image of an idol become inner sap.

mots.def: the mountain (Sinai) raised above them.

mots.sens: they were made to drink in
mots.def: ishrāb: to make drink, to steep.

versets.ar: قُلْ إِن كَانَتْ لَكُمُ الدَّارُ الْآخِرَةُ عِندَ اللَّهِ خَالِصَةً مِّن دُونِ النَّاسِ فَتَمَنَّوُا الْمَوْتَ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: qul in kānat lakumu d-dāru l-ākhiratu ʿinda llāhi khāliṣatan min dūni n-nāsi fa-tamannawu l-mawta in kuntum ṣādiqīn
versets.trad: Say: "If the abode of the hereafter with God is reserved for you alone, to the exclusion of all other people, then long for death, if you are truthful."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of the desire for death a touchstone of love: among the signs of ardent longing is the wish for death upon the carpet of ease; whoever has the certainty that the Garden is his necessarily races towards it. And he finds in it a glad tiding (bishāra): the believers who aspire to death are already forgiven, for this ardour is granted them only with the certainty of arrival.

mots.def: pure, exclusive.
tamannī: desire, wish.

versets.ar: وَلَن يَتَمَنَّوْهُ أَبَدًا بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِالظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lan yatamannawhu abadan bimā qaddamat aydīhim wa-llāhu ʿalīmun bi-ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: But they will never long for it, because of the works their hands have sent ahead. And God knows well the wrongdoers.
themes: the shame of the failing one
versets.commentaire: The announcement that they would never long for it proved true: it was, says Qushayrī, a miracle for the Messenger — it came to pass exactly as he had said. Against their flight from death, he evokes the shame of the loving soul: "For the failing one, shame suffices, on the Day of the Encounter." The one who has not worked dreads the face-to-face; the one who loves aspires to it.

mots.def: to desire ardently.

mots.racine: ق د م / ي د ي
mots.sens: what their hands have sent ahead
mots.def: the deeds already committed.

versets.ar: وَلَتَجِدَنَّهُمْ أَحْرَصَ النَّاسِ عَلَىٰ حَيَاةٍ وَمِنَ الَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا ۚ يَوَدُّ أَحَدُهُمْ لَوْ يُعَمَّرُ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ وَمَا هُوَ بِمُزَحْزِحِهِ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ أَن يُعَمَّرَ ۗ وَاللَّهُ بَصِيرٌ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-tajidannahum aḥraṣa n-nāsi ʿalā ḥayātin wa-mina lladhīna ashrakū yawaddu aḥaduhum law yuʿammaru alfa sanatin wa-mā huwa bi-muzaḥziḥihi mina l-ʿadhābi an yuʿammara wa-llāhu baṣīrun bimā yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: You shall find them the most avid of all people for life, even more than the idolaters. Each one of them would wish to live a thousand years, yet to be granted long life would not avert the chastisement from him. And God sees well what they do.
versets.commentaire: Attachment to the life of this world is, for Qushayrī, the fruit of forgetfulness of God; and yet more blind is the one who most loves to remain in it. He gives the image: the runaway slave wishes no return to his master. But the passage towards the One whose good is hoped for is better, for the believer, than to endure with one whose evil is never safe — and all that is coming is near.

mots.def: ḥirṣ: cupidity, avidity.
zaḥzaḥa: to push away, to thrust off afar.

versets.ar: قُلْ مَن كَانَ عَدُوًّا لِّجِبْرِيلَ فَإِنَّهُ نَزَّلَهُ عَلَىٰ قَلْبِكَ بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَهُدًى وَبُشْرَىٰ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: qul man kāna ʿaduwwan li-jibrīla fa-innahu nazzalahu ʿalā qalbika bi-idhni llāhi muṣaddiqan limā bayna yadayhi wa-hudan wa-bushrā li-l-muʾminīn
versets.trad: Say: "Whoever is an enemy to Gabriel — it is he who brought it down upon your heart, by God's leave, confirming what came before it: a guidance and glad tidings for the believers,
versets.commentaire: The Jews claimed that Gabriel brings nothing but misfortune. Qushayrī confounds them: what greater good than the Qur'ān which he brought down upon your heart? And he magnifies the rank of the celestial messenger: the messenger of the Beloved to the cherished beloved is of noble station and immense honour. The descent of the Book "upon your heart" declares that revelation addresses first the most intimate place of the being.

mots.def: tanzīl: gradual revelation.
a joyful announcement.

versets.ar: مَن كَانَ عَدُوًّا لِّلَّهِ وَمَلَائِكَتِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ وَجِبْرِيلَ وَمِيكَالَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَدُوٌّ لِّلْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: man kāna ʿaduwwan li-llāhi wa-malāʾikatihi wa-rusulihi wa-jibrīla wa-mīkāla fa-inna llāha ʿaduwwun li-l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: whoever is an enemy to God, to His angels, to His messengers, to Gabriel and to Michael — let him know that God is the enemy of the disbelievers."
versets.commentaire: Whoever hates those drawn near to God, God takes him as enemy. Qushayrī concludes: the enmity of the disbelievers in no way harmed Gabriel — peace be upon him — for the Real is his protector; whoever opposes Gabriel has the Real for adversary. And he marvels: what glory this honour is, how great, how exalted! — to be held under the safekeeping of God Himself.

mots.def: ʿadāwa: hostility.
the archangel Michael.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَنزَلْنَا إِلَيْكَ آيَاتٍ بَيِّنَاتٍ ۖ وَمَا يَكْفُرُ بِهَا إِلَّا الْفَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad anzalnā ilayka āyātin bayyinātin wa-mā yakfuru bihā illā l-fāsiqūn
versets.trad: We have indeed sent down to you signs perfectly clear. None deny them save the depraved.
versets.commentaire: Before the dazzling light of the signs, only he denies whose inner sight is barred. Qushayrī: none denies the clarity of His signs save the one whose inward gaze is sealed and to whom misfortune was assigned from all eternity. And he adds: there is no reason in him who denies that day is day — thus there is no union for him whom the lights of the Real do not aid.

mots.racine: أ ي ي / ب ي ن
mots.def: manifest, dazzling verses.
fisq: departure from obedience.

versets.ar: أَوَكُلَّمَا عَاهَدُوا عَهْدًا نَّبَذَهُ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُم ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-kullamā ʿāhadū ʿahdan nabadhahu farīqun minhum bal aktharuhum lā yuʾminūn
versets.trad: Each time they conclude a covenant, a faction among them casts it away: for most of them do not believe.
versets.commentaire: The covenant cast aside is no accident but the law of their hearts. Qushayrī: each time they contracted a covenant, the decree that preceded them came to confound it, and the contrary conduct within them broke it — and God prevails over His affair. The outward failing betrays the inward absence of faith.

mots.def: muʿāhada: mutual undertaking.
nabdh: to throw far off.

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا جَاءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ مِّنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ مُصَدِّقٌ لِّمَا مَعَهُمْ نَبَذَ فَرِيقٌ مِّنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ كِتَابَ اللَّهِ وَرَاءَ ظُهُورِهِمْ كَأَنَّهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā jāʾahum rasūlun min ʿindi llāhi muṣaddiqun limā maʿahum nabadha farīqun mina lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba kitāba llāhi warāʾa ẓuhūrihim ka-annahum lā yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: When there came to them a messenger from God confirming what they already possessed, a faction of those to whom the Book had been given cast the Book of God behind their backs, as though they knew nothing.
themes: the Book cast behind the back
versets.commentaire: To cast the Book behind one's back: the outward gesture of a deeper denial. Qushayrī redoubles it: they denied the messengers of the Real to their hearts — which are the inward inspirations — just as they belied the messengers who came to them outwardly. And he cries out: O ignorance where no flash of knowledge shines! O deprivation accompanied by abandonment!

mots.def: to throw far off, behind oneself.

mots.racine: و ر ي / ظ ه ر
mots.def: far off, in token of contempt.

versets.ar: وَاتَّبَعُوا مَا تَتْلُو الشَّيَاطِينُ عَلَىٰ مُلْكِ سُلَيْمَانَ ۖ وَمَا كَفَرَ سُلَيْمَانُ وَلَٰكِنَّ الشَّيَاطِينَ كَفَرُوا يُعَلِّمُونَ النَّاسَ السِّحْرَ وَمَا أُنزِلَ عَلَى الْمَلَكَيْنِ بِبَابِلَ هَارُوتَ وَمَارُوتَ ۚ وَمَا يُعَلِّمَانِ مِنْ أَحَدٍ حَتَّىٰ يَقُولَا إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ فِتْنَةٌ فَلَا تَكْفُرْ ۖ فَيَتَعَلَّمُونَ مِنْهُمَا مَا يُفَرِّقُونَ بِهِ بَيْنَ الْمَرْءِ وَزَوْجِهِ ۚ وَمَا هُم بِضَارِّينَ بِهِ مِنْ أَحَدٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۚ وَيَتَعَلَّمُونَ مَا يَضُرُّهُمْ وَلَا يَنفَعُهُمْ ۚ وَلَقَدْ عَلِمُوا لَمَنِ اشْتَرَاهُ مَا لَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنْ خَلَاقٍ ۚ وَلَبِئْسَ مَا شَرَوْا بِهِ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۚ لَوْ كَانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ttabaʿū mā tatlū sh-shayāṭīnu ʿalā mulki sulaymāna wa-mā kafara sulaymānu wa-lākinna sh-shayāṭīna kafarū yuʿallimūna n-nāsa s-siḥra wa-mā unzila ʿalā l-malakayni bi-bābila hārūta wa-mārūta wa-mā yuʿallimāni min aḥadin ḥattā yaqūlā innamā naḥnu fitnatun fa-lā takfur fa-yataʿallamūna minhumā mā yufarriqūna bihi bayna l-marʾi wa-zawjihi wa-mā hum bi-ḍārrīna bihi min aḥadin illā bi-idhni llāhi wa-yataʿallamūna mā yaḍurruhum wa-lā yanfaʿuhum wa-laqad ʿalimū la-mani shtarāhu mā lahu fī l-ākhirati min khalāqin wa-la-biʾsa mā sharaw bihi anfusahum law kānū yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: They followed what the demons recounted concerning the kingdom of Solomon. Solomon did not disbelieve, but the demons fell into disbelief. They teach men sorcery, and what was sent down at Babylon to the two angels, Hārūt and Mārūt. These two instruct no one without saying: "We are but a trial, so do not disbelieve!" Yet there are some who learn from them the means to part a man from his wife; but they harm no one save by God's leave. They learn what harms them and brings them no benefit. They knew full well that whoever buys this power shall have no share in the life to come. Wretched is that for which they bartered their souls! Had they but known...
versets.commentaire: From Hārūt and Mārūt Qushayrī draws a lesson on contagious straying: he whom the passions disperse falls into every abyss of heedlessness; and he who long absents himself becomes a trial for the people, and a temptation for whoever follows his way. Then he teaches, citing the Prophet ("I take refuge in You from a knowledge that does not profit"): the people of discernment know that the knowledge of any object, however praiseworthy in itself, may enclose that against which one must guard.

mots.def: magic, deceiving art.
a testing that may lead astray.
a portion of good in the hereafter.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ آمَنُوا وَاتَّقَوْا لَمَثُوبَةٌ مِّنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ ۖ لَّوْ كَانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law annahum āmanū wa-ttaqaw la-mathūbatun min ʿindi llāhi khayrun law kānū yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: Whereas had they believed and been God-fearing, a reward from God would have been better. Had they but known...
versets.commentaire: Had they turned toward God rather than away from Him! Qushayrī opens the lost horizon: had they preferred orientation toward God to occupation far from Him, they would have amassed the treasure of both dwellings and attained the glory of both worlds. But, he adds, the onslaughts of compulsion seized them and fixed them in the abodes of distance.

mots.def: taqwā: pious fear, self-guarding.
thawāb: recompense for the good.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَقُولُوا رَاعِنَا وَقُولُوا انظُرْنَا وَاسْمَعُوا ۗ وَلِلْكَافِرِينَ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā taqūlū rāʿinā wa-qūlū nẓurnā wa-smaʿū wa-li-l-kāfirīna ʿadhābun alīm
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not say [to the Prophet]: "Heed us!" but say: "Regard us!" — and listen. A painful chastisement awaits the disbelievers.
themes: the distinct way of the saints
versets.commentaire: An ambiguous word the enemies twisted: the verse teaches the choosing of pure expression. Qushayrī draws from it a rule of separation: the aims of the enemies, in all their states, words as well as deeds, are base; thus the way of the saints is to guard against resembling them and to take a path other than theirs. Vigilance over the word reflects vigilance over the heart.

mots.def: an ambiguous term twisted into mockery.
an unequivocal alternative.

versets.ar: مَّا يَوَدُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ وَلَا الْمُشْرِكِينَ أَن يُنَزَّلَ عَلَيْكُم مِّنْ خَيْرٍ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يَخْتَصُّ بِرَحْمَتِهِ مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ
versets.tr: mā yawaddu lladhīna kafarū min ahli l-kitābi wa-lā l-mushrikīna an yunazzala ʿalaykum min khayrin min rabbikum wa-llāhu yakhtaṣṣu bi-raḥmatihi man yashāʾu wa-llāhu dhū l-faḍli l-ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: Neither those of the People of the Book who disbelieve, nor the idolaters, wish that any good should be sent down upon you from your Lord. God singles out for His mercy whom He will. God is the Master of immense grace.
versets.commentaire: The enmity of the enemies toward the good of the saints is, says Qushayrī, continual and perpetual; yet the envier does not prevail, nor attain his aim. And he reassures: the privileges of mercy suffice the saints, even should some impostor among the enemies claim that the shores of their joy have crumbled. The divine election depends neither on men's envy nor on their spite.

mots.def: wudd: desire, affection.
ikhtiṣāṣ: election, exclusive attribution.

versets.ar: مَا نَنسَخْ مِنْ آيَةٍ أَوْ نُنسِهَا نَأْتِ بِخَيْرٍ مِّنْهَا أَوْ مِثْلِهَا ۗ أَلَمْ تَعْلَمْ أَنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: mā nansakh min āyatin aw nunsihā naʾti bi-khayrin minhā aw mithlihā a-lam taʿlam anna llāha ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: We do not abrogate a verse, nor cause any to be forgotten, without bringing another, better or like it. Do you not know that God is powerful over all things?
versets.commentaire: Abrogation (naskh) becomes for Qushayrī a figure of spiritual ascent: naskh is translation: He transports you from one state to a higher and loftier state. And he applies it to the path: We efface nothing of the traces of worship without replacing it with lights of servanthood; nor any lights of servanthood without raising in their place the moons of pure servanthood. Every apparent loss is an elevation.

mots.def: naskh: abrogation, translation.

mots.sens: We cause it to be forgotten
mots.def: insāʾ: to cause forgetting or to defer.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَعْلَمْ أَنَّ اللَّهَ لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۗ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ مِن وَلِيٍّ وَلَا نَصِيرٍ
versets.tr: a-lam taʿlam anna llāha lahu mulku s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-mā lakum min dūni llāhi min waliyyin wa-lā naṣīr
versets.trad: Do you not know that to God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and that besides God you have neither protector nor helper?
themes: from the kingdom to the King
versets.commentaire: From the affirmation of dominion Qushayrī draws the great law of wayfaring: His custom is to draw His saints from the contemplation of His kingdom (mulk) to the vision of His sovereignty (milk), then from the contemplation of sovereignty to the contemplation of the Real — from the signs to His attributes, and from the attributes to the contemplation of His Essence. The visible is but a threshold toward Him who holds it.

mots.def: sovereignty over creation.
walī: the one who takes charge.

versets.ar: أَمْ تُرِيدُونَ أَن تَسْأَلُوا رَسُولَكُمْ كَمَا سُئِلَ مُوسَىٰ مِن قَبْلُ ۗ وَمَن يَتَبَدَّلِ الْكُفْرَ بِالْإِيمَانِ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ سَوَاءَ السَّبِيلِ
versets.tr: am turīdūna an tasʾalū rasūlakum kamā suʾila mūsā min qablu wa-man yatabaddali l-kufra bi-l-īmāni fa-qad ḍalla sawāʾa s-sabīl
versets.trad: Would you question your Messenger as Moses was questioned of old? Whoever exchanges faith for disbelief has strayed from the right way.
versets.commentaire: The verse recalls the trial Israel made Moses undergo, and enjoins reverence. Qushayrī presses it to its depth: the Muslims were commanded to honour the Messenger — peace be upon him — to the utmost of the possible; they would stand in his presence as though birds were perched upon their heads. And he seals it: the beauty of courtesy (adab) outwardly is the sign of the beauty of courtesy with God inwardly.

mots.def: to ask questions (here in defiance).
tabaddul: substitution.

versets.ar: وَدَّ كَثِيرٌ مِّنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ لَوْ يَرُدُّونَكُم مِّن بَعْدِ إِيمَانِكُمْ كُفَّارًا حَسَدًا مِّنْ عِندِ أَنفُسِهِم مِّن بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الْحَقُّ ۖ فَاعْفُوا وَاصْفَحُوا حَتَّىٰ يَأْتِيَ اللَّهُ بِأَمْرِهِ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: wadda kathīrun min ahli l-kitābi law yaruddūnakum min baʿdi īmānikum kuffāran ḥasadan min ʿindi anfusihim min baʿdi mā tabayyana lahumu l-ḥaqqu fa-ʿfū wa-ṣfaḥū ḥattā yaʾtiya llāhu bi-amrihi inna llāha ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: Many of the People of the Book would wish to turn you back into disbelievers after you have believed, out of envy from within their souls, after the Truth has become clear to them. Pardon and be forbearing until God comes with His command. God, indeed, is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī applies envy to the beginner's trial upon the Way: he who has lost his intelligence through heedlessness would wish that for no one else the star of salvation should rise; and the envier wishes that no sun should unfold over the one he envies. He warns the aspirant against false companions who divert him through fear, and counsels: the way of the murīd is to guard his secret far from strangers — and soon the Real will open his path for him.

mots.def: ḥasad: envy, the wish that another's good should cease.

mots.racine: ع ف و / ص ف ح
mots.def: ʿafw: to efface the fault; ṣafḥ: to turn away without rancour.

versets.ar: وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكَاةَ ۚ وَمَا تُقَدِّمُوا لِأَنفُسِكُم مِّنْ خَيْرٍ تَجِدُوهُ عِندَ اللَّهِ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-aqīmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātū z-zakāta wa-mā tuqaddimū li-anfusikum min khayrin tajidūhu ʿinda llāhi inna llāha bimā taʿmalūna baṣīr
versets.trad: Perform the prayer and give the alms; whatever good you send ahead for yourselves, you shall find it with God. God sees perfectly all that you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a roadmap for the aspirant: the duty of the murīd is to maintain the bonds and to perpetuate his recourse through the diverse forms of drawing near, confident that the sincerity of his struggles will gather their fruit at the appointed terms of the states. The good "sent ahead" is never lost: it awaits, intact, with God, the traveller at the end of his course.

mots.def: iqāma: to establish, to set up (the prayer).
taqdīm: to send forward.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا لَن يَدْخُلَ الْجَنَّةَ إِلَّا مَن كَانَ هُودًا أَوْ نَصَارَىٰ ۗ تِلْكَ أَمَانِيُّهُمْ ۗ قُلْ هَاتُوا بُرْهَانَكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū lan yadkhula l-jannata illā man kāna hūdan aw naṣārā tilka amāniyyuhum qul hātū burhānakum in kuntum ṣādiqīn
versets.trad: They said: "None shall enter Paradise unless he be a Jew or a Christian." Such are their idle hopes. Say: "Bring your proof, if you are truthful."
versets.commentaire: Each reassures himself by his desire alone. Qushayrī dismantles the illusion: each faction smooths the path of hope for itself, presumes salvation for its own state, and lays claim to a nearness of its own portion. But — he settles it — mere supposition, without the realization of proof, brings no fruit and obtains nothing lasting. Salvation is not decreed: it is lived and attested.

mots.def: amāniyy: groundless wishes.
burhān: decisive argument.

versets.ar: بَلَىٰ مَنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُ لِلَّهِ وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ فَلَهُ أَجْرُهُ عِندَ رَبِّهِ وَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
versets.tr: balā man aslama wajhahu li-llāhi wa-huwa muḥsinun fa-lahu ajruhu ʿinda rabbihi wa-lā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūn
versets.trad: Yes indeed: whoever surrenders his face to God while doing good shall have his reward with his Lord. No fear shall be upon them, nor shall they grieve.
themes: iḥsān, to worship as if one saw
versets.commentaire: Here is the true answer to the empty claim. Qushayrī unfolds "to surrender one's face": he has made his intention pure for God, reserved his face for God alone, and purified his understanding of all disturbance. And he founds iḥsān upon the prophetic word: excellence is to worship God as though you saw Him — surrendered in your outward, contemplating in your secret; without, effort and prostration; within, unveiling and presence.

mots.racine: س ل م / و ج ه
mots.def: to give oneself wholly to God.
iḥsān: excellence in worship.

versets.ar: وَقَالَتِ الْيَهُودُ لَيْسَتِ النَّصَارَىٰ عَلَىٰ شَيْءٍ وَقَالَتِ النَّصَارَىٰ لَيْسَتِ الْيَهُودُ عَلَىٰ شَيْءٍ وَهُمْ يَتْلُونَ الْكِتَابَ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ قَالَ الَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ مِثْلَ قَوْلِهِمْ ۚ فَاللَّهُ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ فِيمَا كَانُوا فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālati l-yahūdu laysati n-naṣārā ʿalā shayʾin wa-qālati n-naṣārā laysati l-yahūdu ʿalā shayʾin wa-hum yatlūna l-kitāba kadhālika qāla lladhīna lā yaʿlamūna mithla qawlihim fa-llāhu yaḥkumu baynahum yawma l-qiyāmati fīmā kānū fīhi yakhtalifūn
versets.trad: The Jews say: "The Christians stand on nothing!" and the Christians say: "The Jews stand on nothing!" — yet they all recite the Book. Those who know nothing speak a like speech. God will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that wherein they differed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads here a reversal between the outward and the hidden: the enemies disavow one another, and so too, in a certain way, do the saints — whence the saying: so long as the Sufis hold apart from one another without merging, they remain in the good. But the difference is radical: the enemies are all upon falsehood when they deny one another; the saints, however, are all upon the truth — their divergence is not division but the distinction of stations.

mots.def: to rest on no foundation.
ḥukm: to decide, to arbitrate.

versets.ar: وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّن مَّنَعَ مَسَاجِدَ اللَّهِ أَن يُذْكَرَ فِيهَا اسْمُهُ وَسَعَىٰ فِي خَرَابِهَا ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ مَا كَانَ لَهُمْ أَن يَدْخُلُوهَا إِلَّا خَائِفِينَ ۚ لَهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيَا خِزْيٌ وَلَهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-man aẓlamu mimman manaʿa masājida llāhi an yudhkara fīhā smuhu wa-saʿā fī kharābihā ulāʾika mā kāna lahum an yadkhulūhā illā khāʾifīna lahum fī d-dunyā khizyun wa-lahum fī l-ākhirati ʿadhābun ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: Who is more iniquitous than he who bars the mosques of God from having His name invoked in them and strives for their ruin? Such men should enter them only in fear. For them is disgrace in this world, and for them in the life to come a tremendous chastisement.
themes: ruining the dwellings of the spirit
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorizes the "mosques of God" down to the sanctuaries of the soul: the iniquitous is he who ruins the dwellings of worship through cravings — and these dwellings are the souls of the worshippers; who ruins the dwellings of gnosis through vows and attachments — and these are the hearts of the gnostics; who ruins the dwellings of love through the portions of ego — and these are the spirits of the ecstatics; who ruins the dwellings of contemplation by turning toward another — and these are the secrets of those made one.

mots.def: masjid: a place where one prostrates.
kharāb: devastation.

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ الْمَشْرِقُ وَالْمَغْرِبُ ۚ فَأَيْنَمَا تُوَلُّوا فَثَمَّ وَجْهُ اللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ وَاسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-li-llāhi l-mashriqu wa-l-maghribu fa-aynamā tuwallū fa-thamma wajhu llāhi inna llāha wāsiʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: To God belong the East and the West. Wherever you turn, there is the face of God. God is all-embracing, all-knowing.
themes: the eclipse of the self before the Real
versets.commentaire: "Wherever you turn, there is the Face of God": Qushayrī sees in it the risings and settings of hearts. The risings of the heart are the stars of the sciences, the moons of presence, the suns of gnosis; so long as these heavenly bodies rise, the qibla of the heart is clear. But when the Realities impose themselves, the reign of the radiances fades — as the stars are veiled at sunrise — and annihilation comes: no perception of a trace, no subsistence of a meaning, no rule of a reason.

mots.def: tawliya: to turn toward.
wajh: the presence, the orientation toward Him.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا اتَّخَذَ اللَّهُ وَلَدًا ۗ سُبْحَانَهُ ۖ بَل لَّهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ كُلٌّ لَّهُ قَانِتُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū ttakhadha llāhu waladan subḥānahu bal lahu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi kullun lahu qānitūn
versets.trad: They said: "God has taken a son!" Glory be to Him! Rather, to Him belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth; all are devoutly submissive to Him.
versets.commentaire: Before the ascription of a son, Qushayrī raises the tanzīh (transcendence): how a child, when He is the Unique in His Essence? No limit to His Essence, no desire possible in His attributes. Then he makes all creation speak: there is nothing in the universe, neither dependent trace nor reality, that does not cry out the marks of creation and proclaim the testimonies of the primordial nature; every mute thing therein is eloquent, and all bears witness to His oneness.

mots.def: tasbīḥ: to declare God free of all defect.
qunūt: humble and constant obedience.

versets.ar: بَدِيعُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ وَإِذَا قَضَىٰ أَمْرًا فَإِنَّمَا يَقُولُ لَهُ كُن فَيَكُونُ
versets.tr: badīʿu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-idhā qaḍā amran fa-innamā yaqūlu lahu kun fa-yakūn
versets.trad: Originator of the heavens and the earth: when He decrees a thing, He but says to it "Be!" and it is.
versets.commentaire: On the Name al-Badīʿ, Qushayrī distinguishes the scholar from the man of unveiling: for the scholars, it is He who brings being forth without prior model; for the people of allusion, it is He whom nothing resembles. This Name denies a peer to His Essence and a model to His acts: He is the Unique whom no number gathers, the Eternal whom no term cuts off, the Real whom no imagination figures, the Existent whom no intellect measures.

mots.def: al-Badīʿ: He who creates without model.
the instantaneous creative word.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ لَوْلَا يُكَلِّمُنَا اللَّهُ أَوْ تَأْتِينَا آيَةٌ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ قَالَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّثْلَ قَوْلِهِمْ ۘ تَشَابَهَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ ۗ قَدْ بَيَّنَّا الْآيَاتِ لِقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna lā yaʿlamūna lawlā yukallimunā llāhu aw taʾtīnā āyatun kadhālika qāla lladhīna min qablihim mithla qawlihim tashābahat qulūbuhum qad bayyannā l-āyāti li-qawmin yūqinūn
versets.trad: Those who know not said: "Why does God not speak to us, or a sign come to us?" Thus spoke those before them, the like of their speech; their hearts resemble one another. We have made the signs clear for a people who are certain.
themes: signs in vain for him who refuses
versets.commentaire: They demand a word their heart cannot hear. Qushayrī: the Word of God attaches to all created things in their essences and their traces; but he who has lost the hearing of understanding has become deaf to the audition of the Real. He recalls that He even made some hear His word who could not bear it, and concludes gravely: what use are proofs, however dazzling, to those for whom misfortune was decreed and ran ahead?

mots.def: a particle of incitement / reproach.
tashābuh: similitude.

versets.ar: إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ بِالْحَقِّ بَشِيرًا وَنَذِيرًا ۖ وَلَا تُسْأَلُ عَنْ أَصْحَابِ الْجَحِيمِ
versets.tr: innā arsalnāka bi-l-ḥaqqi bashīran wa-nadhīran wa-lā tusʾalu ʿan aṣḥābi l-jaḥīm
versets.trad: Indeed, We have sent you with the Truth, a bearer of glad tidings and a warner. You shall not be questioned about the inmates of the Blaze.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī hears this verse as a sovereign election: We have distinguished you with privileges We have manifested in no other; the common folk and the multitude are under your banner; accepted is he who accords with you, rejected he who contradicts you. And he seals the freedom of the Messenger: no questioning weighs upon you concerning the states of others — his charge is the announcement, not the reckoning of hearts.

mots.racine: ب ش ر / ن ذ ر
mots.sens: bearer of glad tidings and warner
mots.def: bearer of good news and of warning.
the intense fire of hell.

versets.ar: وَلَن تَرْضَىٰ عَنكَ الْيَهُودُ وَلَا النَّصَارَىٰ حَتَّىٰ تَتَّبِعَ مِلَّتَهُمْ ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ هُدَى اللَّهِ هُوَ الْهُدَىٰ ۗ وَلَئِنِ اتَّبَعْتَ أَهْوَاءَهُم بَعْدَ الَّذِي جَاءَكَ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ ۙ مَا لَكَ مِنَ اللَّهِ مِن وَلِيٍّ وَلَا نَصِيرٍ
versets.tr: wa-lan tarḍā ʿanka l-yahūdu wa-lā n-naṣārā ḥattā tattabiʿa millatahum qul inna hudā llāhi huwa l-hudā wa-la-ini ttabaʿta ahwāʾahum baʿda lladhī jāʾaka mina l-ʿilmi mā laka mina llāhi min waliyyin wa-lā naṣīr
versets.trad: Neither the Jews nor the Christians will be content with you until you follow their religion. Say: "The guidance of God is the [only true] guidance." Were you to follow their desires after the knowledge that has come to you, you would find against God neither protector nor helper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a charter of detachment: be not concerned with the good pleasure of the enemies after you have obtained Our good pleasure, for they will not be pleased with you save by following their religions. And he rises to direct invocation: proclaim your dissociation from them, and be through Us, for Us, disowning all but Us, trusting in Our succour — for you are through Us and for Us. Attachment to what contents them is, he says, the cause of an endless misfortune.

mots.def: milla: religious way, confession.
hudā: the true direction.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ آتَيْنَاهُمُ الْكِتَابَ يَتْلُونَهُ حَقَّ تِلَاوَتِهِ أُولَٰئِكَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ ۗ وَمَن يَكْفُرْ بِهِ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْخَاسِرُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna ātaynāhumu l-kitāba yatlūnahu ḥaqqa tilāwatihi ulāʾika yuʾminūna bihi wa-man yakfur bihi fa-ulāʾika humu l-khāsirūna
versets.trad: Those to whom We have given the Book, who recite it as it ought to be recited, those believe in it. As for those who deny it, they are the losers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those whose gaze We have opened to the witnessing of Our Truth, whose ear of the heart We have entrusted to the hearing of Our discourse, whom We have singled out by pouring upon them the light of solicitude, and fortified by the knowledge sealed in the secret of their being — these are they who "recite as it ought to be recited" (ḥaqqa tilāwatihi). To recite in truth is not to move the lips, it is to bear within oneself the marks of faith and of gnosis. They are the people of election; the others remain on the side of rejection.

mots.sens: they recite it as it ought to be recited
mots.def: tilāwa: sustained, meditated recitation; ḥaqq: the due, the right measure.

versets.ar: يَا بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتِيَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَأَنِّي فَضَّلْتُكُمْ عَلَى الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: yā banī isrāʾīla dhkurū niʿmatiya llatī anʿamtu ʿalaykum wa-annī faḍḍaltukum ʿalā l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: O Children of Israel! Remember the favour with which I have favoured you, and remember that I gave you preference over the worlds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes a subtlety in the address: with the people of Moses, the divine custom was to call them by the name of their lineage — "O Children of Israel," that is, O sons of Jacob; but this community He calls by the name of honour: "O you who believe". To be named by one's faith rather than by one's ancestry is already a preference.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ن ع م
mots.def: niʿma: grace, bounty.

versets.ar: وَاتَّقُوا يَوْمًا لَّا تَجْزِي نَفْسٌ عَن نَّفْسٍ شَيْئًا وَلَا يُقْبَلُ مِنْهَا عَدْلٌ وَلَا تَنفَعُهَا شَفَاعَةٌ وَلَا هُمْ يُنصَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ttaqū yawman lā tajzī nafsun ʿan nafsin shayʾan wa-lā yuqbalu minhā ʿadlun wa-lā tanfaʿuhā shafāʿatun wa-lā hum yunṣarūna
versets.trad: Fear a Day when no soul shall avail another in anything, when no ransom shall be accepted from it, when no intercession shall profit it, and when none shall be helped.
themes: the Day when none bears another
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes: from the enemies nothing shall be accepted; and the intercession of the intercessors profits not the deniers. But for the believers — and this is a privilege — the intercession of their Prophet shall profit them. On that Day when each will say "myself, myself," one alone will say "my community, my community." The verse closes all the doors of succour the better to open the one, unique door of grace.

mots.def: shafāʿa: intercession.

versets.ar: وَإِذِ ابْتَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ رَبُّهُ بِكَلِمَاتٍ فَأَتَمَّهُنَّ ۖ قَالَ إِنِّي جَاعِلُكَ لِلنَّاسِ إِمَامًا ۖ قَالَ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِي ۖ قَالَ لَا يَنَالُ عَهْدِي الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idhi btalā ibrāhīma rabbuhu bi-kalimātin fa-atammahunna qāla innī jāʿiluka li-n-nāsi imāman qāla wa-min dhurriyyatī qāla lā yanālu ʿahdī ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: Remember: when his Lord tried Abraham with certain commands and he fulfilled them, God said: "I shall make you a model for mankind." "And what of my offspring?" asked Abraham. The Lord answered: "My covenant does not extend to the iniquitous."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the trial (balāʾ) is what authenticates fidelity (walāʾ): the most sincere in love are the most tried. God charged His intimate with the burdens of prophethood and summoned him to the demands of friendship (khulla); and the hardest trial was to stand alone, cut off from every being and every thing, in secret as in the open. Thus, when Gabriel offered to succour him as he was cast into the fire — "do you need anything?" — he answered: "From you? No." Such is the perfection of the trial: that there remain in the lover no room for any other than the Beloved.

mots.def: ibtalā: to put to the test.
imām: the one who is followed, taken as model.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ جَعَلْنَا الْبَيْتَ مَثَابَةً لِّلنَّاسِ وَأَمْنًا وَاتَّخِذُوا مِن مَّقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ مُصَلًّى ۖ وَعَهِدْنَا إِلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْمَاعِيلَ أَن طَهِّرَا بَيْتِيَ لِلطَّائِفِينَ وَالْعَاكِفِينَ وَالرُّكَّعِ السُّجُودِ
versets.tr: wa-idh jaʿalnā l-bayta mathābatan li-n-nāsi wa-amnan wa-ttakhidhū min maqāmi ibrāhīma muṣallan wa-ʿahidnā ilā ibrāhīma wa-ismāʿīla an ṭahhirā baytiya li-ṭ-ṭāʾifīna wa-l-ʿākifīna wa-r-rukkaʿi s-sujūdi
versets.trad: Remember: We made the House a place of resort and a refuge for mankind, enjoining them to take the station of Abraham as a place of prayer. We made a covenant with Abraham and Ishmael that they purify My House for those who circle it, who make pious retreat there, who bow and prostrate.
versets.commentaire: The House is made a "place of return" (mathāba) and "security" (amn): hearts return to it ceaselessly, never sated, because that to which they return in truth is the Master of the House, not the stone. And the command to "purify" the sanctuary is, for the wayfarer, the sign that no circling nor any prostration is accepted so long as the heart is not purified of all other than Him: it is the inward that must first be made clean for the divine guests.

mots.sens: a place of return for mankind
mots.def: mathāba: a place to which one returns ceaselessly.
ṭahhara: to render pure.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ رَبِّ اجْعَلْ هَٰذَا بَلَدًا آمِنًا وَارْزُقْ أَهْلَهُ مِنَ الثَّمَرَاتِ مَنْ آمَنَ مِنْهُم بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ ۖ قَالَ وَمَن كَفَرَ فَأُمَتِّعُهُ قَلِيلًا ثُمَّ أَضْطَرُّهُ إِلَىٰ عَذَابِ النَّارِ ۖ وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-idh qāla ibrāhīmu rabbi jʿal hādhā baladan āminan wa-rzuq ahlahu mina th-thamarāti man āmana minhum bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri qāla wa-man kafara fa-umattiʿuhu qalīlan thumma aḍṭarruhu ilā ʿadhābi n-nāri wa-biʾsa l-maṣīru
versets.trad: And Abraham said: "My Lord! Grant security to this land, and provide its people with fruits, those of them who believe in God and the Last Day." The Lord said: "And the disbeliever I shall grant a brief enjoyment, then drive him to the chastisement of the Fire." What a wretched journey's end!
themes: the brief enjoyment of the denier
versets.commentaire: Abraham implores first only security and provision — and the heart's security precedes that of the place. Qushayrī suggests that the portion of the denier is this "brief enjoyment" (matāʿ qalīl) of the world, which lulls him before delivering him up: he thinks himself to possess, and it is he who is seized. To the believer provision is dispensed as a favour; to the denier it is left as a respite that closes again. "What a wretched journey's end" (biʾsa l-maṣīr) — whither every path that turns away from Him leads.

mots.racine: ب ل د / أ م ن
