mots.def: āmin: peaceful, sheltered.

mots.sens: I grant him a brief enjoyment
mots.def: mattaʿa: to let one enjoy a passing good.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ يَرْفَعُ إِبْرَاهِيمُ الْقَوَاعِدَ مِنَ الْبَيْتِ وَإِسْمَاعِيلُ رَبَّنَا تَقَبَّلْ مِنَّا ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-idh yarfaʿu ibrāhīmu l-qawāʿida mina l-bayti wa-ismāʿīlu rabbanā taqabbal minnā innaka anta s-samīʿu l-ʿalīmu
versets.trad: And as Abraham and Ishmael raised the foundations of the House [they offered this prayer]: "Our Lord! Accept this from us; You are the All-hearing, the All-knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the request reaches its end in the sincerity of supplication; when they took refuge in the lowliness of prayer, succour and answer came to them. They build with their own hands and yet fear that the work may not be received: such is the mark of the people of God, who never lean upon their act. "You hear" our words, "You know" our states: they entrust both the word and the heart.

mots.racine: ر ف ع / ق ع د
mots.def: qawāʿid: foundations, bases.
taqabbala: to accept graciously.

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا وَاجْعَلْنَا مُسْلِمَيْنِ لَكَ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِنَا أُمَّةً مُّسْلِمَةً لَّكَ وَأَرِنَا مَنَاسِكَنَا وَتُبْ عَلَيْنَا ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: rabbanā wa-jʿalnā muslimayni laka wa-min dhurriyyatinā ummatan muslimatan laka wa-arinā manāsikanā wa-tub ʿalaynā innaka anta t-tawwābu r-raḥīmu
versets.trad: "Our Lord! Make us two who are surrendered to You, and of our offspring a community surrendered to You; show us our rites and turn to us in forgiveness! You are, indeed, the Ever-relenting, the Merciful!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds each word. "Surrendered" (muslimayni): docile to Your decree, until not a vein stirs in us without Your good pleasure. "A surrendered community": not an heir to our goods, but a successor who holds after us our place in the service. "And turn to us": after we have fulfilled all Your command — that we may no longer contemplate our own acts, and that we may return to You from the gaze cast upon our works, for fear of the subtle idolatry (shirk khafī) that creeps in the moment one ascribes anything to oneself.

mots.def: muslim: one who gives himself wholly to God.
tāba ʿalā: to accept the return, to forgive.

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا وَابْعَثْ فِيهِمْ رَسُولًا مِّنْهُمْ يَتْلُو عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتِكَ وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ ۚ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: rabbanā wa-bʿath fīhim rasūlan minhum yatlū ʿalayhim āyātika wa-yuʿallimuhumu l-kitāba wa-l-ḥikmata wa-yuzakkīhim innaka anta l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: "Our Lord! Raise up among them a messenger from among themselves, to recite to them Your verses, to teach them the Book and Wisdom, and to purify them. You are, indeed, the Mighty, the Wise!"
themes: the way passes through the prophets
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: since obligations are known only through the messengers, and not by reason alone, Abraham asked that God not leave men forsaken, without messenger or Law. He implores that the messenger be "from among themselves" (minhum), that they may find in him greater nearness and gentleness; and it is said that, God having made known to him the coming of our Prophet, he was beseeching the fulfilment of this promise. The way to God opens through the sending of the messengers.

mots.racine: ب ع ث / ر س ل
mots.sens: raise up among them a messenger
mots.def: baʿatha: to send, to raise up.
zakkā: to purify, to make grow in good.

versets.ar: وَمَن يَرْغَبُ عَن مِّلَّةِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ إِلَّا مَن سَفِهَ نَفْسَهُ ۚ وَلَقَدِ اصْطَفَيْنَاهُ فِي الدُّنْيَا ۖ وَإِنَّهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ لَمِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: wa-man yarghabu ʿan millati ibrāhīma illā man safiha nafsahu wa-laqadi ṣṭafaynāhu fī d-dunyā wa-innahu fī l-ākhirati la-mina ṣ-ṣāliḥīna
versets.trad: Who would turn away from the religion of Abraham save he who makes a fool of his own soul? In truth, We chose him in this world, and in the next he shall be among the righteous.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God preferred His intimate above all creation: He made pure monotheism his religion, the attestation of Oneness his banner, and gnosis his very quality. Thus he who turns from his way or deviates from his custom has no dwelling left but falsehood and no abyss but denial, for the lights, all of them, are but kindled at his light. To turn from this way is to hold oneself in contempt.

mots.sens: to turn away from, to disdain
mots.def: raghiba ʿan: to feel aversion for.
iṣṭafā: to choose, to elect (from purity, ṣafwa).

versets.ar: إِذْ قَالَ لَهُ رَبُّهُ أَسْلِمْ ۖ قَالَ أَسْلَمْتُ لِرَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: idh qāla lahu rabbuhu aslim qāla aslamtu li-rabbi l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: When his Lord said to him: "Surrender!" he answered: "I surrender to the Lord of the worlds!"
themes: the extinction of one's own choice
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: islām is pure sincerity, it is total surrender; its reality is to come forth entirely from the states of the human condition, from the disputes of choice and the contestations of the soul. When Abraham says "I surrender," it is no pretension coming from him: for the truth of surrender is precisely to divest oneself of all power and strength of one's own. To say "I surrender" is to say: hold me upright in what You charge me with, realize in me what You have commanded. He spared nothing — neither wealth, nor body, nor child.

mots.def: aslama: to give oneself up, to deliver oneself wholly.

mots.racine: س ل م / ر ب ب
mots.sens: I surrender to the Lord of the worlds
mots.def: rabb al-ʿālamīn: the Master of the universes.

versets.ar: وَوَصَّىٰ بِهَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بَنِيهِ وَيَعْقُوبُ يَا بَنِيَّ إِنَّ اللَّهَ اصْطَفَىٰ لَكُمُ الدِّينَ فَلَا تَمُوتُنَّ إِلَّا وَأَنتُم مُّسْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-waṣṣā bihā ibrāhīmu banīhi wa-yaʿqūbu yā baniyya inna llāha ṣṭafā lakumu d-dīna fa-lā tamūtunna illā wa-antum muslimūna
versets.trad: Abraham enjoined the same upon his sons, and so did Jacob [saying]: "O my sons! God has chosen for you the Religion; so die not save as those surrendered to Him (muslimūn)."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: however their Laws differ in their acts, the foundation is one, and the source whence one drinks Tawhīd has no second. What Abraham and Jacob bequeath to their sons is neither gold nor land, but the one testament that is worth keeping: leave not this world save with your face turned toward Him, in a state of surrender. To die "surrendered" is to make of the final instant the mirror of a whole life given over to God.

mots.def: waṣṣā: to enjoin solemnly, to bequeath.

mots.racine: م و ت / س ل م
mots.sens: die not save in a state of surrender
mots.def: muslimūn: surrendered to God.

versets.ar: أَمْ كُنتُمْ شُهَدَاءَ إِذْ حَضَرَ يَعْقُوبَ الْمَوْتُ إِذْ قَالَ لِبَنِيهِ مَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِن بَعْدِي قَالُوا نَعْبُدُ إِلَٰهَكَ وَإِلَٰهَ آبَائِكَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْمَاعِيلَ وَإِسْحَاقَ إِلَٰهًا وَاحِدًا وَنَحْنُ لَهُ مُسْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: am kuntum shuhadāʾa idh ḥaḍara yaʿqūba l-mawtu idh qāla li-banīhi mā taʿbudūna min baʿdī qālū naʿbudu ilāhaka wa-ilāha ābāʾika ibrāhīma wa-ismāʿīla wa-isḥāqa ilāhan wāḥidan wa-naḥnu lahu muslimūna
versets.trad: Were you witnesses when death came to Jacob and he said to his sons: "What will you worship after me?" They said: "We shall worship your God and the God of your fathers — Abraham, Ishmael and Isaac — one God, and to Him we are surrendered!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: all of them, peace be upon them, wayfared upon one single way in Oneness and surrender, handing down this inheritance from generation to generation: they are the household of nearness, those entitled to drawing near, those whom God has truly purified. Note, he says, that they did not say "our God" but "your God," in regard for the singular rank of Jacob, acknowledging his pre-eminence while attaching themselves to his station; then they attested of themselves: "and to Him we are surrendered."

mots.racine: أ ل ه / و ح د
mots.def: wāḥid: one, without associate.

mots.sens: and to Him we are surrendered
mots.def: muslim: one who gives himself to God.

versets.ar: تِلْكَ أُمَّةٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ ۖ لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَلَكُم مَّا كَسَبْتُمْ ۖ وَلَا تُسْأَلُونَ عَمَّا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: tilka ummatun qad khalat lahā mā kasabat wa-lakum mā kasabtum wa-lā tusʾalūna ʿammā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: That community has passed away. To it belongs what it earned, and to you what you have earned; you shall not be questioned about what they did.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Real has placed each in his own proper place, allotting to each being a measure conforming to His decree. None bears the tidings of another, no trace of that with which one category was favoured passes to another. And he adds this sovereign image: each is a king in his own kingdom, and toward each turns the star of his felicity. One lives neither off the glory of forebears nor off their fault: what alone counts is the earning of the present heart.

mots.def: khalā: to depart, to be over.
kasaba: to earn, to gain by one's act.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا كُونُوا هُودًا أَوْ نَصَارَىٰ تَهْتَدُوا ۗ قُلْ بَلْ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا ۖ وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū kūnū hūdan aw naṣārā tahtadū qul bal millata ibrāhīma ḥanīfan wa-mā kāna mina l-mushrikīna
versets.trad: They said: "Be Jews, or be Christians, and you will be rightly guided." Say: "Rather, the religion of Abraham, a pure believer (ḥanīf), who was not among the idolaters."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the factions pull you this way and that and the demands of allegiance contradict one another, judge by setting their claims face to face, and redouble your orientation toward Us, following the way of the Intimate — Abraham — in his withdrawal from the whole multitude, were it from his own father or from whoever did not accord with his Master. This is why he said: "I dissociate myself from you and from what you invoke besides God." The ḥanīf is he who inclines toward the Real through the Real, stripped of all association.

mots.sens: a pure believer, inclined to the true
mots.def: ḥanīf: primordial monotheist, turned away from falsehood.
mushrik: one who associates other deities with God.

versets.ar: قُولُوا آمَنَّا بِاللَّهِ وَمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْنَا وَمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْمَاعِيلَ وَإِسْحَاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَالْأَسْبَاطِ وَمَا أُوتِيَ مُوسَىٰ وَعِيسَىٰ وَمَا أُوتِيَ النَّبِيُّونَ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّنْهُمْ وَنَحْنُ لَهُ مُسْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: qūlū āmannā bi-llāhi wa-mā unzila ilaynā wa-mā unzila ilā ibrāhīma wa-ismāʿīla wa-isḥāqa wa-yaʿqūba wa-l-asbāṭi wa-mā ūtiya mūsā wa-ʿīsā wa-mā ūtiya n-nabiyyūna min rabbihim lā nufarriqu bayna aḥadin minhum wa-naḥnu lahu muslimūna
versets.trad: Say: "We believe in God, in what has been revealed to us, in what was revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the Tribes; in what was given to Moses and Jesus; in what was given to the prophets from their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them; and to Him we are surrendered."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: because our Prophet believed in all that was revealed before him, he was honoured with all the honour bestowed upon those before him; having manifested his accord with the whole, all were called to stand under his banner — "Adam and whoever comes after him shall be under my banner on the Day of Resurrection." Likewise, because his community believed in all that God sent down upon His messengers, without distinguishing between any of them, it drew from the lottery of honour the highest share, outstripping all the nations.

mots.def: asbāṭ: the twelve tribes descended from Jacob.

mots.sens: we make no distinction between any of them
mots.def: farraqa: to separate, to make differences.

versets.ar: فَإِنْ آمَنُوا بِمِثْلِ مَا آمَنتُم بِهِ فَقَدِ اهْتَدَوا ۖ وَّإِن تَوَلَّوْا فَإِنَّمَا هُمْ فِي شِقَاقٍ ۖ فَسَيَكْفِيكَهُمُ اللَّهُ ۚ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: fa-in āmanū bi-mithli mā āmantum bihi fa-qadi htadaw wa-in tawallaw fa-innamā hum fī shiqāqin fa-sayakfīkahumu llāhu wa-huwa s-samīʿu l-ʿalīmu
versets.trad: If they believe in the like of what you believe, they are rightly guided; but if they turn away, then they are in schism. God will suffice you against them; He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.
themes: the succour promised to the messenger
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if they take your road and follow your way, they shall be honoured with that wherewith you are honoured and reach where you have reached; but if they refuse all but division, We hold for them nothing but abasement. And "God will suffice you against them": this sufficiency is certain, for the solicitude of God envelops you; whoever attacks you, the hands of succour break him; whoever opposes you, the decrees of the allotment subdue him. The servant who is supported has no need to fear the adversary.

mots.def: shiqāq: rupture, opposition.

mots.sens: God will suffice you against them
mots.def: kafā: to suffice, to safeguard.

versets.ar: صِبْغَةَ اللَّهِ ۖ وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ مِنَ اللَّهِ صِبْغَةً ۖ وَنَحْنُ لَهُ عَابِدُونَ
versets.tr: ṣibghata llāhi wa-man aḥsanu mina llāhi ṣibghatan wa-naḥnu lahu ʿābidūna
versets.trad: [They speak of] the dye of God? But who gives a fairer dye than God? And we are His worshippers!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads ṣibgha (the dye) as the mark deposited by God: what counts is what the Real has established, not what the servant amasses; what the creature strives to add tends toward extinction, but what the Real has fixed upon the primordial nature (fiṭra), there lies the worth. And he distinguishes the degrees of this dye: there is a dye for the bodies and the outward forms — through the traces of divine aid; and a dye for the spirits and the secrets — through the lights of realization.

mots.def: ṣibgha: colour, imprint; here the primordial nature given by God.
ʿābid: worshipper.

versets.ar: قُلْ أَتُحَاجُّونَنَا فِي اللَّهِ وَهُوَ رَبُّنَا وَرَبُّكُمْ وَلَنَا أَعْمَالُنَا وَلَكُمْ أَعْمَالُكُمْ وَنَحْنُ لَهُ مُخْلِصُونَ
versets.tr: qul a-tuḥājjūnanā fī llāhi wa-huwa rabbunā wa-rabbukum wa-lanā aʿmālunā wa-lakum aʿmālukum wa-naḥnu lahu mukhliṣūna
versets.trad: Say [to the People of the Book]: "Would you argue with us concerning God, when He is our Lord and your Lord, when to us belong our deeds and to you your deeds, and when we are wholly devoted to Him?"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī overturns the quarrel: how could a dispute of strangers be admissible, when they are beneath the veil of absence and in the shadow of the covering, while the friends are in the clarity of unveiling and the evidence of witnessing? He asks: when would the state of one who, by his absence, is in destitution be equal to the state of one who, immersed in his nearness, is in election and sincerity (ikhlāṣ)? Never — there is no equality. To argue about God with one who serves Him in sincerity is already to condemn oneself.

mots.sens: do you argue with us concerning God
mots.def: ḥājja: to argue, to dispute.
mukhliṣ: one who purifies his worship for God alone.

versets.ar: أَمْ تَقُولُونَ إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْمَاعِيلَ وَإِسْحَاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَالْأَسْبَاطَ كَانُوا هُودًا أَوْ نَصَارَىٰ ۗ قُلْ أَأَنتُمْ أَعْلَمُ أَمِ اللَّهُ ۗ وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّن كَتَمَ شَهَادَةً عِندَهُ مِنَ اللَّهِ ۗ وَمَا اللَّهُ بِغَافِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: am taqūlūna inna ibrāhīma wa-ismāʿīla wa-isḥāqa wa-yaʿqūba wa-l-asbāṭa kānū hūdan aw naṣārā qul a-antum aʿlamu ami llāhu wa-man aẓlamu mimman katama shahādatan ʿindahu mina llāhi wa-mā llāhu bi-ghāfilin ʿammā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: Would you say that Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the Tribes were Jews or Christians? Say: "Is it you, or God, who know best?" Who is more iniquitous than he who hides a testimony he has received from God? God is not heedless of what you do!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī probes the root of their error: he who looks at creation from himself imagines each in his own image and counts every being according to his own state; being themselves in the condition of the stranger, they judged the prophets by the measure of their own case. But, he asks, is the one who is rapt away from himself like the one who remains veiled within himself? Does the one taken from his self equal the one sent back to his like? Such is the conjecture of the deniers.

mots.racine: ك ت م / ش ه د
mots.def: katama: to suppress, to conceal; shahāda: attestation.
ghāfil: distracted, negligent.

versets.ar: تِلْكَ أُمَّةٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ ۖ لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَلَكُم مَّا كَسَبْتُمْ ۖ وَلَا تُسْأَلُونَ عَمَّا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: tilka ummatun qad khalat lahā mā kasabat wa-lakum mā kasabtum wa-lā tusʾalūna ʿammā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: That community has passed away. To it belongs what it earned, and to you what you have earned. You shall not be questioned about what they did.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: barriers raised by the divine allotment have interposed between you and them: they built their edifice upon separation and heedlessness, while you, you pitched your tents upon nearness and union. And he seals it with a cutting phrase: the one freed by Our favour does not resemble the one banished by Our severity — he whom grace sets free is not he whom severity leads astray.

mots.def: khalā: to be over.

mots.sens: to you what you have earned
mots.def: kasaba: to earn by one's act.

versets.ar: سَيَقُولُ السُّفَهَاءُ مِنَ النَّاسِ مَا وَلَّاهُمْ عَن قِبْلَتِهِمُ الَّتِي كَانُوا عَلَيْهَا ۚ قُل لِّلَّهِ الْمَشْرِقُ وَالْمَغْرِبُ ۚ يَهْدِي مَن يَشَاءُ إِلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: sa-yaqūlu s-sufahāʾu mina n-nāsi mā wallāhum ʿan qiblatihimu llatī kānū ʿalayhā qul li-llāhi l-mashriqu wa-l-maghribu yahdī man yashāʾu ilā ṣirāṭin mustaqīmin
versets.trad: The foolish among men will say: "What has turned them away from the Qibla they used to face?" Say: "To God belong the East and the West; He guides whom He will to a straight path."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the inner sight of the deniers has grown blunt, so that no aspect of the just appears to them in the states of the believers; they see nothing new without at once raising against it a fresh objection. And he broadens it: likewise, the people of absence and of the veil — who do not see the Real disposing of His friends — demand forms for the divine command to bend their states to; if they contemplated the Whole with a single eye, they would be delivered from the pain of a scattered thought.

mots.def: wallā: to make turn, to turn away.

mots.racine: ش ر ق / غ ر ب
mots.sens: to God belong the East and the West
mots.def: mashriq / maghrib: the rising / setting place.

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ جَعَلْنَاكُمْ أُمَّةً وَسَطًا لِّتَكُونُوا شُهَدَاءَ عَلَى النَّاسِ وَيَكُونَ الرَّسُولُ عَلَيْكُمْ شَهِيدًا ۗ وَمَا جَعَلْنَا الْقِبْلَةَ الَّتِي كُنتَ عَلَيْهَا إِلَّا لِنَعْلَمَ مَن يَتَّبِعُ الرَّسُولَ مِمَّن يَنقَلِبُ عَلَىٰ عَقِبَيْهِ ۚ وَإِن كَانَتْ لَكَبِيرَةً إِلَّا عَلَى الَّذِينَ هَدَى اللَّهُ ۗ وَمَا كَانَ اللَّهُ لِيُضِيعَ إِيمَانَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بِالنَّاسِ لَرَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika jaʿalnākum ummatan wasaṭan li-takūnū shuhadāʾa ʿalā n-nāsi wa-yakūna r-rasūlu ʿalaykum shahīdan wa-mā jaʿalnā l-qiblata llatī kunta ʿalayhā illā li-naʿlama man yattabiʿu r-rasūla mimman yanqalibu ʿalā ʿaqibayhi wa-in kānat la-kabīratan illā ʿalā lladhīna hadā llāhu wa-mā kāna llāhu li-yuḍīʿa īmānakum inna llāha bi-n-nāsi la-raʾūfun raḥīmun
versets.trad: Thus have We made you a community of the just middle, that you may be witnesses over mankind and that the Messenger may be a witness over you. We appointed the Qibla you used to face only to distinguish him who follows the Messenger from him who turns upon his heels. This [change of Qibla] was indeed a grievous trial, save for those whom God has guided; for God will not let your faith go to waste. God, in truth, is compassionate and merciful toward mankind.
themes: the community of the just middle
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "the middle" (wasaṭ) is the elite; God made of this community the elite of the nations, and of an elite within it the elite of this community — the elite of the elite. Of these he says that they are the roots and the axis: upon them all turns, they are the pole (quṭb), and through them God preserves the whole community. Whomever their hearts accept is accepted, whomever their hearts repel is rejected; true is their discernment (firāsa), just their judgement, unerring their gaze.

mots.sens: a community of the just middle
mots.def: wasaṭ: middle, the best, the balanced.

mots.racine: ق ل ب / ع ق ب
mots.def: inqalaba: to turn back; ʿaqib: heel.

versets.ar: قَدْ نَرَىٰ تَقَلُّبَ وَجْهِكَ فِي السَّمَاءِ ۖ فَلَنُوَلِّيَنَّكَ قِبْلَةً تَرْضَاهَا ۚ فَوَلِّ وَجْهَكَ شَطْرَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ ۚ وَحَيْثُ مَا كُنتُمْ فَوَلُّوا وُجُوهَكُمْ شَطْرَهُ ۗ وَإِنَّ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ لَيَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهُ الْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ ۗ وَمَا اللَّهُ بِغَافِلٍ عَمَّا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: qad narā taqalluba wajhika fī s-samāʾi fa-la-nuwalliyannaka qiblatan tarḍāhā fa-walli wajhaka shaṭra l-masjidi l-ḥarāmi wa-ḥaythu mā kuntum fa-wallū wujūhakum shaṭrahu wa-inna lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba la-yaʿlamūna annahu l-ḥaqqu min rabbihim wa-mā llāhu bi-ghāfilin ʿammā yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: We see your face turning about toward the heavens. We shall therefore turn you toward a Qibla with which you will be content. Turn now your face toward the Sacred Mosque. Wherever you may be, turn your faces in its direction. Those who have received the Book know well that this is the Truth from their Lord. And God is not heedless of what they do.
themes: the unspoken longing of the Beloved
what the Lover does for the lover
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī admires the courtesy (adab) of the Prophet: he kept the proprieties, holding silent on his tongue the wish his heart formed concerning the Qibla; he only gazed upon the heavens, because that is the way of Gabriel. And God answered: "We see your face turning toward the heavens" — that is: We knew your request before your tongue even uttered it, and We changed the Qibla for you. Then this overwhelming word: "and that is the utmost of what the Beloved does for the lover."

mots.racine: ق ل ب / و ج ه
mots.def: taqallub: turning about, to and fro.

mots.racine: ش ط ر / ح ر م
mots.def: shaṭr: side, direction; al-masjid al-ḥarām: the sanctuary of Mecca.

versets.ar: وَلَئِنْ أَتَيْتَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ بِكُلِّ آيَةٍ مَّا تَبِعُوا قِبْلَتَكَ ۚ وَمَا أَنتَ بِتَابِعٍ قِبْلَتَهُمْ ۚ وَمَا بَعْضُهُم بِتَابِعٍ قِبْلَةَ بَعْضٍ ۚ وَلَئِنِ اتَّبَعْتَ أَهْوَاءَهُم مِّن بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَكَ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ ۙ إِنَّكَ إِذًا لَّمِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-la-in atayta lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba bi-kulli āyatin mā tabiʿū qiblataka wa-mā anta bi-tābiʿin qiblatahum wa-mā baʿḍuhum bi-tābiʿin qiblata baʿḍin wa-la-ini ttabaʿta ahwāʾahum min baʿdi mā jāʾaka mina l-ʿilmi innaka idhan la-mina ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: Even if you brought to those who were given the Book every possible sign, they would not follow your Qibla, nor would you follow theirs, nor would any of them follow the Qibla of the others. And were you to yield to their desires after the knowledge that has come to you, you would surely be numbered among the wrongdoers.
themes: the partition decreed from all eternity
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it was decreed for you, from the antiquity of the verdict, that you alone would hold to the path of the Real, and that your adversaries would fall to the side of estrangement. Between the two, he says, there is an isthmus (barzakh) they shall never cross: they will not follow your Qibla, were you to show them signs more dazzling than suns and moons, nor will you follow theirs whatever cunning they deploy — by a decree of God fixed in the first eternity.

mots.def: āya: sign, proof.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ه و ي
mots.def: hawā: inclination, capricious craving.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ آتَيْنَاهُمُ الْكِتَابَ يَعْرِفُونَهُ كَمَا يَعْرِفُونَ أَبْنَاءَهُمْ ۖ وَإِنَّ فَرِيقًا مِّنْهُمْ لَيَكْتُمُونَ الْحَقَّ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna ātaynāhumu l-kitāba yaʿrifūnahu kamā yaʿrifūna abnāʾahum wa-inna farīqan minhum la-yaktumūna l-ḥaqqa wa-hum yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Those to whom We gave the Book recognize it as they recognize their own sons. Yet a party of them knowingly conceal the Truth.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unveils the mainspring of their denial: it is the lurking rancours of envy (ḥasad) that drive them to repudiate what they know with certain knowledge. He makes of this a law of the soul: so it is with one vanquished in the darkness of his ego — he has cast off the veil of shame, so that blame no longer bites upon him and no word turns him from his obstinacy. To know the Truth "as one knows one's own sons" and yet to silence it is the height of willful blindness.

mots.racine: ع ر ف / ب ن ي
mots.sens: they recognize it as their sons
mots.def: ʿarafa: to recognize with certainty.
katama: to hide, to suppress.

versets.ar: الْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۖ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُمْتَرِينَ
versets.tr: al-ḥaqqu min rabbika fa-lā takūnanna mina l-mumtarīna
versets.trad: The Truth comes from your Lord; so be not among those who waver.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: now that the suns of certainty (yaqīn) have risen for you, submit no longer to the surmises of conjecture (takhmīn). And he notes that the address is to the Prophet but concerns the whole community. Doubt is effaced not by one more argument, but by the dawning of an inward evidence: he who has seen no longer disputes.

mots.racine: ح ق ق / ر ب ب
mots.sens: the Truth comes from your Lord
mots.def: al-ḥaqq: the true, the real.
imtarā: to be in doubt.

versets.ar: وَلِكُلٍّ وِجْهَةٌ هُوَ مُوَلِّيهَا ۖ فَاسْتَبِقُوا الْخَيْرَاتِ ۚ أَيْنَ مَا تَكُونُوا يَأْتِ بِكُمُ اللَّهُ جَمِيعًا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-li-kullin wijhatun huwa muwallīhā fa-stabiqū l-khayrāti ayna mā takūnū yaʾti bikumu llāhu jamīʿan inna llāha ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: For every being there is an orientation toward which he turns. So vie with one another in good deeds. Wherever you may be, God will bring you all together. Truly, God has power over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives the allusion: every people that is absorbed away from Us in some thing, that very thing rises up between them and Us. You then, O believers, be His and by Him. And he relates this verse of a lover: "Should cares distract me from You with their tumult, I would make You my one concern — and You would make me forget all care." True orientation (wijha) is not only of the face, but of the heart strained toward Him alone.

mots.racine: و ج ه / و ل ي
mots.sens: an orientation toward which he turns
mots.def: wijha: direction, the point one turns toward.

mots.racine: س ب ق / خ ي ر
mots.def: istabaqa: to outstrip one another; khayrāt: good works.

versets.ar: وَمِنْ حَيْثُ خَرَجْتَ فَوَلِّ وَجْهَكَ شَطْرَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ ۖ وَإِنَّهُ لَلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۗ وَمَا اللَّهُ بِغَافِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-min ḥaythu kharajta fa-walli wajhaka shaṭra l-masjidi l-ḥarāmi wa-innahu la-l-ḥaqqu min rabbika wa-mā llāhu bi-ghāfilin ʿammā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: From wherever you set out, turn your face toward the Sacred Mosque: such is the Truth from your Lord. And God is not heedless of what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes the outward command into a discipline of the heart: just as you face the Qibla wherever you are — near to it or far from it — so turn toward Us with your hearts, in whatever state you may be: whether We grant you abundance or whether We try you. The orientation of the face is but the sensible sign of a more secret orientation: that nothing turn the heart from its face toward the Real.

mots.racine: ح ي ث / خ ر ج
mots.def: ḥaythu: wherever; kharaja: to go forth.

mots.sens: it is indeed the Truth from your Lord
mots.def: al-ḥaqq: the true.

versets.ar: وَمِنْ حَيْثُ خَرَجْتَ فَوَلِّ وَجْهَكَ شَطْرَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ ۚ وَحَيْثُ مَا كُنتُمْ فَوَلُّوا وُجُوهَكُمْ شَطْرَهُ لِئَلَّا يَكُونَ لِلنَّاسِ عَلَيْكُمْ حُجَّةٌ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مِنْهُمْ فَلَا تَخْشَوْهُمْ وَاخْشَوْنِي وَلِأُتِمَّ نِعْمَتِي عَلَيْكُمْ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-min ḥaythu kharajta fa-walli wajhaka shaṭra l-masjidi l-ḥarāmi wa-ḥaythu mā kuntum fa-wallū wujūhakum shaṭrahu li-allā yakūna li-n-nāsi ʿalaykum ḥujjatun illā lladhīna ẓalamū minhum fa-lā takhshawhum wa-khshawnī wa-li-utimma niʿmatī ʿalaykum wa-laʿallakum tahtadūna
versets.trad: From wherever you set out, turn your face toward the Sacred Mosque. Wherever you may be, turn your faces in its direction, so that men may have no argument against you — save those of them who have done wrong. Fear them not, but fear Me, that I may perfect My grace upon you. May you be rightly guided!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if you would have none gain hold over you, that no creature cast its shadow upon you, that no hand of misfortune reach you — then wherever you are, however you are, be His and of Him: for whoever devotes himself wholly to Us, no mishap befalls him. On "that I may perfect My grace," he says: the completion of the blessing is to add unveiling (kashf) to gentleness (luṭf) — to be filled not only by the gift, but by the presence of the Giver.

mots.def: ḥujja: an opposable argument.

mots.racine: ت م م / ن ع م
mots.sens: that I may perfect My grace
mots.def: atamma: to complete, to bring to perfection.

versets.ar: كَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا فِيكُمْ رَسُولًا مِّنكُمْ يَتْلُو عَلَيْكُمْ آيَاتِنَا وَيُزَكِّيكُمْ وَيُعَلِّمُكُمُ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَيُعَلِّمُكُم مَّا لَمْ تَكُونُوا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: kamā arsalnā fīkum rasūlan minkum yatlū ʿalaykum āyātinā wa-yuzakkīkum wa-yuʿallimukumu l-kitāba wa-l-ḥikmata wa-yuʿallimukum mā lam takūnū taʿlamūna
versets.trad: Thus have We sent among you a Messenger taken from yourselves. He recites to you Our signs; he purifies you; he teaches you the Book and Wisdom; he teaches you what you did not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the sending of the Messenger is the opening of the gates of union (wuṣūl). For it lay in God's eternal knowledge that the hearts of His friends would be athirst for the meeting with Him, and that none has a way to Him save through the mediation of the messengers. And he distinguishes two peoples: the one He burdened with weights through the sending of the messengers; the other He honoured through them with every form of nearness and approach (zulfa) — how vast the gap between the two!

mots.def: rasūl: messenger.
ḥikma: wisdom, insight into the deep meaning.

versets.ar: فَاذْكُرُونِي أَذْكُرْكُمْ وَاشْكُرُوا لِي وَلَا تَكْفُرُونِ
versets.tr: fa-dhkurūnī adhkurkum wa-shkurū lī wa-lā takfurūni
versets.trad: Remember Me, and I shall remember you! Be thankful to Me, and deny Me not!
versets.commentaire: This is the verse of dhikr, and Qushayrī plunges into it: remembrance (dhikr) is the engulfing of the one who invokes within the witnessing of the Invoked, then his annihilation in the existence of the Invoked, until there remains of you no trace of which to speak. He unfolds two ways: "Remember Me" by the abandonment of every portion, "I shall remember you" by establishing you in My right after your extinction to yourselves; and again: had not My prior remembrance of you been, your later remembrance of Me would never have been. His dhikr precedes and founds ours.

mots.sens: remember Me, and I shall remember you
mots.def: dhakara: to remember, to invoke, to mention.
shakara: to thank, to be grateful.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اسْتَعِينُوا بِالصَّبْرِ وَالصَّلَاةِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الصَّابِرِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū staʿīnū bi-ṣ-ṣabri wa-ṣ-ṣalāti inna llāha maʿa ṣ-ṣābirīna
versets.trad: O you who believe! Seek help through patience and prayer. Truly, God is with the patient.
themes: the patience that draws down grace
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī binds patience to divine favour: seek help through patience — by your endurance, when the decrees of the Real are fulfilled upon you, you become worthy that your Lord pray over you, as He says elsewhere: upon the patient "are blessings from their Lord." And he adds: the patient have merited the supreme treasure and the raising of rank, in that they have attained the companionship of God (maʿiyya) — "truly, God is with the patient." To be patient is less to clench one's teeth than to open the space where God stands with one.

mots.racine: ع و ن / ص ب ر
mots.def: istaʿāna: to ask aid; ṣabr: endurance.
maʿiyya: companionship, presence alongside.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَقُولُوا لِمَن يُقْتَلُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ أَمْوَاتٌ ۚ بَلْ أَحْيَاءٌ وَلَٰكِن لَّا تَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā taqūlū li-man yuqtalu fī sabīli llāhi amwātun bal aḥyāʾun wa-lākin lā tashʿurūna
versets.trad: Say not of those who are slain in the path of God: "They are dead!" Nay! They are living, but you are not aware of it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī multiplies the perspectives upon this hidden life: the life of this world has escaped them, yet they have attained eternal life in the hereafter; in truth they are living, finding with God every kind of favour. Higher still: they are living because God Himself is their successor — and one whose successor is God cannot be dead. And at the summit: their bodies may be extinguished through God, yet their spirits subsist through God; for one whose annihilation was through God (fanāʾ bi-llāh), his permanence too is through God (baqāʾ bi-llāh).

mots.racine: ق ت ل / س ب ل
mots.sens: who is slain in the path of God
mots.def: sabīl Allāh: the way of God.
aḥyāʾ: the living.

versets.ar: وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُم بِشَيْءٍ مِّنَ الْخَوْفِ وَالْجُوعِ وَنَقْصٍ مِّنَ الْأَمْوَالِ وَالْأَنفُسِ وَالثَّمَرَاتِ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ الصَّابِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-la-nabluwannakum bi-shayʾin mina l-khawfi wa-l-jūʿi wa-naqṣin mina l-amwāli wa-l-anfusi wa-th-thamarāti wa-bashshiri ṣ-ṣābirīna
versets.trad: We shall surely try you with something of fear and hunger, with loss of wealth, of lives, and of fruits. But give glad tidings to those who are patient,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives each trial its hidden virtue: He tries them with fear — and it is a clearing of their breasts; with hunger — and it is a purification of their bodies; with loss of wealth — whereby their souls are cleansed; with afflictions striking those dear to them — whereby their reward grows with God; with the blighting of harvests — whereby what God gives them in exchange is multiplied. And "give glad tidings to the patient": those who raise no objection to His decree in what He has ordained. He recalls first by grace, to draw forth gratitude, then by trial, to draw forth patience.

mots.def: balā: to test, to put to trial.

mots.racine: ب ش ر / ص ب ر
mots.sens: give glad tidings to the patient
mots.def: bashshara: to announce good news.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ إِذَا أَصَابَتْهُم مُّصِيبَةٌ قَالُوا إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna idhā aṣābathum muṣībatun qālū innā li-llāhi wa-innā ilayhi rājiʿūna
versets.trad: to those who say, when an affliction befalls them: "Truly, we belong to God, and to Him we return!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises the response to affliction by degrees: they met the trial with patience — nay, with gratitude — nay, with joy and pride. For, he says, whoever beholds all things as the property of the Real sees himself a stranger between them and their decree: the Author of creation has more right over creation than creation has over itself. He finally contrasts two stations: he who is God's (li-llāh) is patient and steadfast; but he who is through God (bi-llāh) has laid down all choice and all judgement — if He establishes him, he stands; if He effaces him, he is effaced; extinct to his own choices, handled in the divine Grasp.

mots.def: muṣība: trial, calamity.

mots.sens: we belong to God and to Him we return
mots.def: rājiʿūn: those who return.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ صَلَوَاتٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَرَحْمَةٌ ۖ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika ʿalayhim ṣalawātun min rabbihim wa-raḥmatun wa-ulāʾika humu l-muhtadūna
versets.trad: Upon those are blessings and mercy from their Lord; and those are the rightly guided.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reverses the apparent order of merit: it is by His blessings upon them, from the very beginning, that they attained their patience and their steadfastness under the demands of the decree — not by their patience that they reached His blessings. For, he says, without His eternal mercy, their obedience would never have fulfilled the condition of servanthood: it is His prior solicitude that won them a pure guidance. And he concludes: "those are the rightly guided" — because He took them into mercy at the beginning, they were guided in the end.

mots.def: ṣalawāt: graces, divine blessings.
muhtadī: one who has received and followed guidance.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةَ مِن شَعَائِرِ اللَّهِ ۖ فَمَنْ حَجَّ الْبَيْتَ أَوِ اعْتَمَرَ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِ أَن يَطَّوَّفَ بِهِمَا ۚ وَمَن تَطَوَّعَ خَيْرًا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ شَاكِرٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: inna ṣ-ṣafā wa-l-marwata min shaʿāʾiri llāhi fa-man ḥajja l-bayta awi ʿtamara fa-lā junāḥa ʿalayhi an yaṭṭawwafa bihimā wa-man taṭawwaʿa khayran fa-inna llāha shākirun ʿalīmun
versets.trad: Truly, Ṣafā and Marwa are among the symbols ordained by God. For whoever makes the Pilgrimage to the House, or performs the lesser visitation, there is no blame in pacing between the two. And whoever does good of his own accord — truly, God is grateful, all-knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of these two hills the relics of the beloved of God, which one honours and visits: these stations, these marked ruins, are venerated and sought out, mounts are turned toward them, because they are the traces of the beloved — there their relics gleam. And he sings: "I love the dwelling of one who once dwelt there, though in the dwelling there be neither sorrow nor joy." In his eyes, the very dust of their path, for one who loves, is worth more than the purest musk. Ṣafā and Marwa are honoured as the neighbour is honoured for the sake of his neighbour: the House.

mots.def: shaʿāʾir: sacred signs, landmarks of worship.

mots.sens: that he pace between the two
mots.def: ṭāfa: to go to and fro (saʿy between the two).

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْتُمُونَ مَا أَنزَلْنَا مِنَ الْبَيِّنَاتِ وَالْهُدَىٰ مِن بَعْدِ مَا بَيَّنَّاهُ لِلنَّاسِ فِي الْكِتَابِ ۙ أُولَٰئِكَ يَلْعَنُهُمُ اللَّهُ وَيَلْعَنُهُمُ اللَّاعِنُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yaktumūna mā anzalnā mina l-bayyināti wa-l-hudā min baʿdi mā bayyannāhu li-n-nāsi fī l-kitābi ulāʾika yalʿanuhumu llāhu wa-yalʿanuhumu l-lāʿinūna
versets.trad: Those who conceal the clear proofs and the guidance We have sent down, after We have made them plain to men in the Book — those God curses, and the cursers curse them too,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī turns the warning toward the spiritual master: the allusion is to one to whom the Real has unveiled a knowledge of the courtesies of the way (ādāb al-sulūk), who then proves miserly in transmitting it to the aspirants by counsel and direction: he merits disgrace at that very instant. And he warns: it is feared that blessing be stripped from his knowledge each time he fails his duty, for having delayed to teach one who had a right to it. To conceal the guidance received is to dry up its very spring.

mots.racine: ك ت م / ب ي ن
mots.def: bayyināt: clear proofs.
laʿana: to curse; lāʿinūn: those entitled to curse.

versets.ar: إِلَّا الَّذِينَ تَابُوا وَأَصْلَحُوا وَبَيَّنُوا فَأُولَٰئِكَ أَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ وَأَنَا التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: illā lladhīna tābū wa-aṣlaḥū wa-bayyanū fa-ulāʾika atūbu ʿalayhim wa-anā t-tawwābu r-raḥīmu
versets.trad: save those who repent, make amends, and make their repentance manifest. Those I shall turn toward in forgiveness, for I am the One who accepts all repentance, the Merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they have made up for their prior failing by a beautiful return, standing by the aspirants to counsel them, and setting forth before them — by the beauty of clarity and the establishing of proof — the rightness of their own conduct. For, he says, the most dazzling argument to authenticate your deeds, and the most truthful witness to that to which you summon men, is that your acting not contradict what you indicate by your speech. True repentance (tawba) joins inward amendment to the transparency of word and deed.

mots.racine: ت و ب / ص ل ح
mots.def: tāba: to return, to repent; aṣlaḥa: to reform, to set right.

mots.racine: ت و ب / ر ح م
mots.sens: I am the One who accepts repentance, the Merciful
mots.def: al-Tawwāb: the One who ever returns toward the repentant; al-Raḥīm: the Merciful.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَمَاتُوا وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ أُولَٰئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَعْنَةُ اللَّهِ وَالْمَلَائِكَةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū wa-mātū wa-hum kuffārun ulāʾika ʿalayhim laʿnatu llāhi wa-l-malāʾikati wa-n-nāsi ajmaʿīna
versets.trad: As for the disbelievers who die in disbelief, upon them falls the curse of God, of the angels, and of all mankind;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives the verse an inward bearing: those who, after having set out on the path of longing for God, change their minds and return to common habits, then are seized by death in that abandonment, those are "the folk of separation." No attention turns toward their souls any more, none weeps for them: they have lost in this world and the next. The worst death is not that of the body, but that of the heart that turned away after having tasted the call.

mots.def: kafara: to deny, to cover over the truth.

mots.racine: م و ت / ك ف ر
mots.def: to die in the state of denial.
laʿna: removal from mercy.

versets.ar: خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا لَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابُ وَلَا هُمْ يُنظَرُونَ
versets.tr: khālidīna fīhā lā yukhaffafu ʿanhumu l-ʿadhābu wa-lā hum yunẓarūna
versets.trad: abiding therein forever; the torment shall not be lightened for them, nor shall they be granted respite.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī glosses the "forever" as an eternal dwelling in abasement and littleness, with neither relief nor rescue, neither gentleness nor subtle grace. Where divine intimacy would have been repose, separation becomes a sojourn that never softens: it is the very weight of estrangement.

mots.def: khulūd: permanence without end.
khaffafa: to lighten, to relieve.
anẓara: to grant delay, reprieve.

versets.ar: وَإِلَٰهُكُمْ إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ ۖ لَّا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الرَّحْمَٰنُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-ilāhukum ilāhun wāḥidun lā ilāha illā huwa r-raḥmānu r-raḥīmu
versets.trad: Your God is one God! There is no god but He, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī marvels at the words "your God": by joining His divinity to you, He has crowned you with a signal honour, for His being God to you is without cause, while your servanthood toward Him mends every deficiency and breach in you. And since when has He said "your God"? Before your acts, before your motions and stillnesses, before your very essence — in the eternity before time, where there was neither when, nor instant, nor trace. The One is He whom nothing resembles, whom no like approaches, whom no peer attends; and were He not the All-Merciful, the servant would vanish at the first gleam of His glory.

mots.racine: أ ل ه / و ح د
mots.def: wāḥid: the One without second or like.
absolute negation followed by exception: tawḥīd.
raḥmān: all-encompassing mercy; raḥīm: continuous mercy.

versets.ar: إِنَّ فِي خَلْقِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَاخْتِلَافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ وَالْفُلْكِ الَّتِي تَجْرِي فِي الْبَحْرِ بِمَا يَنفَعُ النَّاسَ وَمَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مِن مَّاءٍ فَأَحْيَا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا وَبَثَّ فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ دَابَّةٍ وَتَصْرِيفِ الرِّيَاحِ وَالسَّحَابِ الْمُسَخَّرِ بَيْنَ السَّمَاءِ وَالْأَرْضِ لَآيَاتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: inna fī khalqi s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-khtilāfi l-layli wa-n-nahāri wa-l-fulki llatī tajrī fī l-baḥri bimā yanfaʿu n-nāsa wa-mā anzala llāhu mina s-samāʾi min māʾin fa-aḥyā bihi l-arḍa baʿda mawtihā wa-baththa fīhā min kulli dābbatin wa-taṣrīfi r-riyāḥi wa-s-saḥābi l-musakhkhari bayna s-samāʾi wa-l-arḍi la-āyātin li-qawmin yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: Truly, in the creation of the heavens and the earth, in the alternation of night and day, in the ship that sails the sea bearing what profits men, in the water that God sends down from the sky whereby He revives the earth after its death — that earth in which He has scattered every kind of creature — in the shifting of the winds, in the clouds made subservient between the sky and the earth, there are signs for a people who reason.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He has made Himself known to the hearts of the seekers — the people of reasoning and intellect — by the tokens of His power, the signs of His existence, the marks of His lordship. He has awakened them to wisdom and to the proofs of the divine Oneness through what He has lodged in creation: demonstrations too subtle for speech, indications too fine for allusion. And there is no being drawn from nothingness — form or trace, sky or space, sun or moon, sand or stone, star or tree — that is not a proof of the Oneness, and a path toward Him for the seeker.

mots.sens: the creation of the heavens and the earth
mots.def: khalq: the creative act, that which is created.

mots.sens: He revived the earth by it
mots.def: aḥyā: to give life, to resurrect.

mots.racine: ص ر ف / ر و ح
mots.def: taṣrīf: variation, changing direction.
ʿaqala: to grasp by the intellect.

versets.ar: وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَن يَتَّخِذُ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ أَندَادًا يُحِبُّونَهُمْ كَحُبِّ اللَّهِ ۖ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَشَدُّ حُبًّا لِّلَّهِ ۗ وَلَوْ يَرَى الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا إِذْ يَرَوْنَ الْعَذَابَ أَنَّ الْقُوَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًا وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ شَدِيدُ الْعَذَابِ
versets.tr: wa-mina n-nāsi man yattakhidhu min dūni llāhi andādan yuḥibbūnahum ka-ḥubbi llāhi wa-lladhīna āmanū ashaddu ḥubban li-llāhi wa-law yarā lladhīna ẓalamū idh yarawna l-ʿadhāba anna l-quwwata li-llāhi jamīʿan wa-anna llāha shadīdu l-ʿadhābi
versets.trad: Among men are those who set up rivals to God, loving them as one loves God; but the believers are firmer in their love of God. Could the wrongdoers but see, when they behold the torment, that all power belongs to God, and that God is severe in punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these the Real did not make people of love; so He occupied them with loving what is not Him, until they delighted in worshipping what their own hands had shaped. The point is not to praise this love of idols, but to exalt that of the believers. And why does their love prevail? Because the believers' love proceeds from the love God has for them — "He loves them and they love Him" — whereas the idolaters' love proceeds from their mere caprice; and because the believers love Him whom He has veiled in majesty from their eyes: to love unseen is higher than to love the visible.

mots.def: nidd: that which is associated with God.

mots.sens: they love them as one loves God
mots.def: ḥubb: love, attachment.

mots.racine: ش د د / ح ب ب
mots.def: ashadd: stronger, firmer.

versets.ar: إِذْ تَبَرَّأَ الَّذِينَ اتُّبِعُوا مِنَ الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوا وَرَأَوُا الْعَذَابَ وَتَقَطَّعَتْ بِهِمُ الْأَسْبَابُ
versets.tr: idh tabarraʾa lladhīna ttubiʿū mina lladhīna ttabaʿū wa-raʾawu l-ʿadhāba wa-taqaṭṭaʿat bihimu l-asbābu
versets.trad: When those who were followed disown those who followed them, when they behold the torment and the bonds that joined them are severed,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: as soon as the first signs of torment appear, it becomes clear that they never set a single foot in truthfulness. All that joined them — the ties of caprice and idol — is cut clean through. By contrast, the love that binds the believer to his Lord is severed by no torment: it is the one bond that the world's end does not undo.

mots.def: tabarraʾa min: to declare oneself innocent of, to repudiate.
the leaders, the imitated chiefs.

mots.racine: ق ط ع / س ب ب
mots.sens: the bonds were severed for them
mots.def: asbāb: ties, means, relations.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوا لَوْ أَنَّ لَنَا كَرَّةً فَنَتَبَرَّأَ مِنْهُمْ كَمَا تَبَرَّءُوا مِنَّا ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ يُرِيهِمُ اللَّهُ أَعْمَالَهُمْ حَسَرَاتٍ عَلَيْهِمْ ۖ وَمَا هُم بِخَارِجِينَ مِنَ النَّارِ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna ttabaʿū law anna lanā karratan fa-natabarraʾa minhum kamā tabarraʾū minnā kadhālika yurīhimu llāhu aʿmālahum ḥasarātin ʿalayhim wa-mā hum bi-khārijīna mina n-nāri
versets.trad: The followers will then say: "Ah, were we but able to return, we would disown them as they have disowned us." Thus will God show them their works as bitter regrets upon them; and they shall not come forth from the Fire.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, in a word: it is then that they taste the bitter savour of the company of creatures, gathering from it nothing but regrets. To have leaned upon what is not God is overturned, on the day of unveiling, into pure resentment: the very deeds become the matter of grief.

mots.def: karra: to return, to begin again.
ḥasra: anguish over what is lost.
khārij: one who goes out; here negated.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ كُلُوا مِمَّا فِي الْأَرْضِ حَلَالًا طَيِّبًا وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا خُطُوَاتِ الشَّيْطَانِ ۚ إِنَّهُ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nāsu kulū mimmā fī l-arḍi ḥalālan ṭayyiban wa-lā tattabiʿū khuṭuwāti sh-shayṭāni innahu lakum ʿaduwwun mubīnun
versets.trad: O mankind! Eat of what is lawful and wholesome on the earth. Follow not the footsteps of Satan: he is for you a manifest enemy,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī is decisive: the unlawful, though savoury for the moment, is ruinous at the end; the lawful, though harsh for the moment, is salutary at the end. And the pure lawful is that whose acquirer did not forget the Real at the moment of acquiring it: bread becomes pure when the heart stays present to God in earning it. And all that leads you to forget the Real or to disobey Him belongs to the footsteps of Satan.

mots.def: akala: to eat.

mots.racine: ح ل ل / ط ي ب
mots.def: ḥalāl: permitted; ṭayyib: pure, agreeable.

mots.racine: خ ط و / ش ط ن
mots.def: khuṭuwāt: steps, traces.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا يَأْمُرُكُم بِالسُّوءِ وَالْفَحْشَاءِ وَأَن تَقُولُوا عَلَى اللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: innamā yaʾmurukum bi-s-sūʾi wa-l-faḥshāʾi wa-an taqūlū ʿalā llāhi mā lā taʿlamūna
versets.trad: he commands you only to evil and indecency, and to say of God what you do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, in a tight phrase: because he himself dares to assault God, he calls you to forge lies about God. The enemy's first effrontery breeds the second in you: whoever heeds his steps ends by speaking of God without knowledge.

mots.def: amara: to command.

mots.racine: س و أ / ف ح ش
mots.def: sūʾ: evil; faḥshāʾ: infamy, obscenity.
to speak of God without knowledge.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ اتَّبِعُوا مَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ قَالُوا بَلْ نَتَّبِعُ مَا أَلْفَيْنَا عَلَيْهِ آبَاءَنَا ۗ أَوَلَوْ كَانَ آبَاؤُهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ شَيْئًا وَلَا يَهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā qīla lahumu ttabiʿū mā anzala llāhu qālū bal nattabiʿu mā alfaynā ʿalayhi ābāʾanā a-wa-law kāna ābāʾuhum lā yaʿqilūna shayʾan wa-lā yahtadūna
versets.trad: When it is said to them, "Follow what God has revealed," they answer: "Nay, we follow the ways of our fathers!" What! Even though their fathers understood nothing and were not rightly guided?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they never raise their gaze above their fellows and their forebears; they have built upon the same road, and thereby have been threaded onto the same string as their own. Had they but seen that their ancestors had neither a reason to restrain them nor a rectitude to gather them, they would have turned away; but they were deprived of the lights of insight and the proofs of certainty. The worst inheritance is the one received without passing it through the sieve of the heart.

mots.def: ittabaʿa: to follow, to conform to.

mots.racine: ل ف و / أ ب و
mots.sens: that upon which we found our fathers
mots.def: alfā: to find, to come upon.
absence of intellect.

versets.ar: وَمَثَلُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا كَمَثَلِ الَّذِي يَنْعِقُ بِمَا لَا يَسْمَعُ إِلَّا دُعَاءً وَنِدَاءً ۚ صُمٌّ بُكْمٌ عُمْيٌ فَهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mathalu lladhīna kafarū ka-mathali lladhī yanʿiqu bi-mā lā yasmaʿu illā duʿāʾan wa-nidāʾan ṣummun bukmun ʿumyun fa-hum lā yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: The likeness of the disbelievers is that of cattle whose herdsman can make himself heard by nothing but calls and cries. Deaf, dumb, blind, they do not reason.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they have lost the hearing of understanding and acceptance; the hearing of the outward availed them nothing, and they have fallen to the rank of beasts, void of all recollection. And whoever consents to be as a beast has no great worth left: the outward ear hears sounds, but it is the ear of the heart that receives the meaning.

mots.def: naʿaqa: to shout to summon beasts.

mots.racine: ص م م / ب ك م / ع م ي
mots.def: infirmities of the heart more than of the senses.
loss of the intellect of meaning.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُلُوا مِن طَيِّبَاتِ مَا رَزَقْنَاكُمْ وَاشْكُرُوا لِلَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ إِيَّاهُ تَعْبُدُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū kulū min ṭayyibāti mā razaqnākum wa-shkurū li-llāhi in kuntum iyyāhu taʿbudūna
versets.trad: O you who believe! Eat of the good things We have provided for you, and give thanks to God, if it is truly He whom you worship.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes: the lawful is what drags no burden behind it; the pure is that in which no creature holds a claim over you; and when the servant finds a nourishment in which both qualities meet, that is the pure lawful. As for the truth of gratitude, it is not to breathe outside the good pleasure of the Real so long as the strength drawn from that nourishment remains in you: to give thanks is to make of the food itself a fuel for worship.

mots.def: ṭayyibāt: what is pure, agreeable, lawful.
razaqa: to dispense sustenance.
shakara: to thank, to acknowledge the favour.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا حَرَّمَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةَ وَالدَّمَ وَلَحْمَ الْخِنزِيرِ وَمَا أُهِلَّ بِهِ لِغَيْرِ اللَّهِ ۖ فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ غَيْرَ بَاغٍ وَلَا عَادٍ فَلَا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: innamā ḥarrama ʿalaykumu l-maytata wa-d-dama wa-laḥma l-khinzīri wa-mā uhilla bihi li-ghayri llāhi fa-mani ḍṭurra ghayra bāghin wa-lā ʿādin fa-lā ithma ʿalayhi inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: He has forbidden you only carrion, blood, the flesh of swine, and that over which a name other than God's has been invoked. But whoever is driven by necessity, neither rebelling nor transgressing, no sin shall be laid upon him. God is forgiving, merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī doubles the outward prohibition with an inward one: He has forbidden to the outward these enumerated things; but He has forbidden to the inward the company of what is not God — nay, the mere contemplation of any other than Him. And the one driven by necessity — he who finds no way to annihilation in the realities of the Real — let him take no path but that of the Law: either effaced in God, or subsisting through God, or working for God; the fourth is but a weightless mass.

mots.def: mayta: an animal dead without ritual slaughter.

mots.sens: that immolated for other than God
mots.def: uhilla: a name invoked aloud at slaughter.

mots.racine: ض ر ر / ب غ ي / ع د و
mots.sens: driven by necessity, neither craving nor transgressing
mots.def: iḍṭurra: to be compelled by need.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْتُمُونَ مَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ مِنَ الْكِتَابِ وَيَشْتَرُونَ بِهِ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۙ أُولَٰئِكَ مَا يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ إِلَّا النَّارَ وَلَا يُكَلِّمُهُمُ اللَّهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَلَا يُزَكِّيهِمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yaktumūna mā anzala llāhu mina l-kitābi wa-yashtarūna bihi thamanan qalīlan ulāʾika mā yaʾkulūna fī buṭūnihim illā n-nāra wa-lā yukallimuhumu llāhu yawma l-qiyāmati wa-lā yuzakkīhim wa-lahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: Those who conceal what God has revealed of the Book and barter it for a paltry price — those fill their bellies with nothing but Fire. God will not speak to them on the Day of Resurrection, nor purify them, and theirs shall be a painful torment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two charges: the learned are bound to spread abroad the proofs of knowledge, and the friends of God are charged to guard the deposits of the secret. If the first silence the demonstrations of learning, a bridle of fire is set upon them; and if the second let slip a flash of the secret, they are punished by the withdrawal of secrets and the retraction of the lights they had received. Every rank has its limit, and every affair its breaking-point.

mots.def: katama: to hide, to suppress.

mots.racine: ث م ن / ق ل ل
mots.def: thaman qalīl: a contemptible recompense.
zakkā: to purify, to sanctify.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ اشْتَرَوُا الضَّلَالَةَ بِالْهُدَىٰ وَالْعَذَابَ بِالْمَغْفِرَةِ ۚ فَمَا أَصْبَرَهُمْ عَلَى النَّارِ
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna shtarawu ḍ-ḍalālata bi-l-hudā wa-l-ʿadhāba bi-l-maghfirati fa-mā aṣbarahum ʿalā n-nāri
versets.trad: Those are they who have bartered guidance for error, and pardon for torment. How great is their endurance of the Fire!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī exclaims with the verse: those who preferred the other to the Mystery, the creature to the Real, the soul to intimacy — how hard their hearts, how base their greed, how shameful their affair in the eyes of the clear-sighted! To have sold guidance for error is the most senseless exchange there is: they gave back the pearl to keep the potsherd.

mots.racine: ش ر ي / ض ل ل / ه د ي
mots.sens: they bought error at the price of guidance
mots.def: ishtarā: to exchange, to barter.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / غ ف ر
mots.sens: torment at the price of pardon
mots.def: maghfira: forgiveness, covering of faults.
an expression of wonder: what endurance of the Fire.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللَّهَ نَزَّلَ الْكِتَابَ بِالْحَقِّ ۗ وَإِنَّ الَّذِينَ اخْتَلَفُوا فِي الْكِتَابِ لَفِي شِقَاقٍ بَعِيدٍ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-anna llāha nazzala l-kitāba bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-inna lladhīna khtalafū fī l-kitābi la-fī shiqāqin baʿīdin
versets.trad: That is because God has sent down the Book with the Truth. And those who differ about the Book are surely in a far-reaching schism.
themes: the Book sent down with the Truth
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: that is because God sent down the Book with the Truth, and made the decree and the judgement pass through it with truthfulness; He led them to that for which He had predisposed them, and fixed them in the very disposition according to which He had moulded them. The schism is no flaw in the Book, but the fruit of the heart that reads it through its own veil.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ح ق ق
mots.sens: He sent down the Book with the Truth
mots.def: nazzala: to send down gradually.
ikhtalafa: to be at variance.

mots.racine: ش ق ق / ب ع د
mots.def: shiqāq: rupture, splitting; baʿīd: remote.

versets.ar: لَّيْسَ الْبِرَّ أَن تُوَلُّوا وُجُوهَكُمْ قِبَلَ الْمَشْرِقِ وَالْمَغْرِبِ وَلَٰكِنَّ الْبِرَّ مَنْ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَالْمَلَائِكَةِ وَالْكِتَابِ وَالنَّبِيِّينَ وَآتَى الْمَالَ عَلَىٰ حُبِّهِ ذَوِي الْقُرْبَىٰ وَالْيَتَامَىٰ وَالْمَسَاكِينَ وَابْنَ السَّبِيلِ وَالسَّائِلِينَ وَفِي الرِّقَابِ وَأَقَامَ الصَّلَاةَ وَآتَى الزَّكَاةَ وَالْمُوفُونَ بِعَهْدِهِمْ إِذَا عَاهَدُوا وَالصَّابِرِينَ فِي الْبَأْسَاءِ وَالضَّرَّاءِ وَحِينَ الْبَأْسِ ۗ أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ صَدَقُوا ۖ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: laysa l-birra an tuwallū wujūhakum qibala l-mashriqi wa-l-maghribi wa-lākinna l-birra man āmana bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-l-malāʾikati wa-l-kitābi wa-n-nabiyyīna wa-ātā l-māla ʿalā ḥubbihi dhawī l-qurbā wa-l-yatāmā wa-l-masākīna wa-bna s-sabīli wa-s-sāʾilīna wa-fī r-riqābi wa-aqāma ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātā z-zakāta wa-l-mūfūna bi-ʿahdihim idhā ʿāhadū wa-ṣ-ṣābirīna fī l-baʾsāʾi wa-ḍ-ḍarrāʾi wa-ḥīna l-baʾsi ulāʾika lladhīna ṣadaqū wa-ulāʾika humu l-muttaqūna
versets.trad: Piety does not consist in turning your faces toward the East or the West; rather, piety belongs to one who believes in God, the Last Day, the angels, the Book, and the prophets; who, out of love for Him, gives of his wealth to kinsfolk, orphans, the needy, the wayfarer, and beggars, and for the freeing of slaves; who performs the prayer and gives the alms; to those who keep the pledges they have made, and who are patient in adversity, hardship, and at the moment of peril. Those are the truthful, and those are the God-fearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: outward forms have little worth; what counts is the tiding that comes from God, and it is rare. To multiply litanies, however eminent, is the craft of old women; to purify one's deeds, though at great cost, is the quality of the common. All the fruits of excellence enumerated here — giving one's wealth, keeping one's covenants, enduring trial — are beloved of God and required by His command; yet that the Real subsist in your place after your annihilation, that your traces be effaced — that is higher still, for tawḥīd leaves neither trace nor vestige to subsist.

mots.def: birr: integral virtue, accomplished good.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ح ب ب
mots.sens: he gave wealth despite his love for it
mots.def: to give what one holds dear.

mots.racine: و ف ي / ع ه د
mots.def: awfā: to fulfil, to be faithful to.
ṣabr: endurance in trial.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْقِصَاصُ فِي الْقَتْلَى ۖ الْحُرُّ بِالْحُرِّ وَالْعَبْدُ بِالْعَبْدِ وَالْأُنثَىٰ بِالْأُنثَىٰ ۚ فَمَنْ عُفِيَ لَهُ مِنْ أَخِيهِ شَيْءٌ فَاتِّبَاعٌ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَأَدَاءٌ إِلَيْهِ بِإِحْسَانٍ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ تَخْفِيفٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَرَحْمَةٌ ۗ فَمَنِ اعْتَدَىٰ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فَلَهُ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū kutiba ʿalaykumu l-qiṣāṣu fī l-qatlā l-ḥurru bi-l-ḥurri wa-l-ʿabdu bi-l-ʿabdi wa-l-unthā bi-l-unthā fa-man ʿufiya lahu min akhīhi shayʾun fa-ttibāʿun bi-l-maʿrūfi wa-adāʾun ilayhi bi-iḥsānin dhālika takhfīfun min rabbikum wa-raḥmatun fa-mani ʿtadā baʿda dhālika fa-lahu ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: O you who believe! Retaliation is prescribed for you in cases of murder: the free man for the free man, the slave for the slave, the woman for the woman. But he to whom his brother grants some pardon should comply with a reasonable demand and pay the compensation in good faith. That is an alleviation and a mercy from your Lord. Whoever transgresses after this shall incur a painful torment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the right of retaliation is lawful, but pardon is better; whoever demands his due, it is granted him; whoever forgoes the claim is a doer of good — the first is a man of worship, the second a man of nobility, even of freedom. Then he rises to the allusion: for the people of the spiritual state, their blood is beyond price and their souls are surrendered, for to shed the blood of the beloved on the carpet of nearness is the prerogative of the people of union — and the Prophet said of the martyr: "the colour is that of blood, the fragrance that of musk."

mots.def: qiṣāṣ: a penalty equal to the crime.

mots.sens: he to whom pardon is granted
mots.def: ʿafā: to remit, to pardon.

mots.racine: خ ف ف / ر ح م
mots.sens: an alleviation from your Lord and a mercy
mots.def: takhfīf: easing, facilitation.

versets.ar: وَلَكُمْ فِي الْقِصَاصِ حَيَاةٌ يَا أُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lakum fī l-qiṣāṣi ḥayātun yā ulī l-albābi laʿallakum tattaqūna
versets.trad: In retaliation there is, for you, life, O people of understanding, that you may fear God!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: on the tongue of the Law, in the application of retaliation there is life, for he who knows he will be slain if he slays refrains from slaying: this is the life of the slayer and of the would-be victim alike. But on the tongue of allusion, to forgo retaliation bears a greater life still: for when the servant is lost in Him, it is He who becomes his successor, and subsistence through God is more precious than survival through oneself.

mots.def: ḥayāt: preservation of life through deterrence.

mots.racine: أ و ل / ل ب ب
mots.def: albāb: deep intellects, the kernels of the heart.
ittaqā: to guard oneself through fear.

versets.ar: كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذَا حَضَرَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ إِن تَرَكَ خَيْرًا الْوَصِيَّةُ لِلْوَالِدَيْنِ وَالْأَقْرَبِينَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ ۖ حَقًّا عَلَى الْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: kutiba ʿalaykum idhā ḥaḍara aḥadakumu l-mawtu in taraka khayran al-waṣiyyatu li-l-wālidayni wa-l-aqrabīna bi-l-maʿrūfi ḥaqqan ʿalā l-muttaqīna
versets.trad: It is prescribed for you, when death presents itself to one of you and he leaves behind wealth, to make a bequest in favour of his parents and near kin, according to what is fitting. This is a duty upon the God-fearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts two economies: the wealthy, at the end of their lives, bequeath a third of their goods; but the friends of God strip themselves of everything while still living, so that nothing remains of them but an aspiration detached from all things — for aspiration has no tie with any creature, and the Real lets Himself be reached by none. Thus their bequest is already fulfilled: they have kept nothing to bequeath, having surrendered all before the hour.

mots.racine: ح ض ر / م و ت
mots.sens: death presents itself to one of you
mots.def: ḥaḍara: to come upon, to be present.

mots.racine: ت ر ك / خ ي ر
mots.def: khayr here: riches, fortune.
waṣiyya: a disposition of last will.

versets.ar: فَمَن بَدَّلَهُ بَعْدَمَا سَمِعَهُ فَإِنَّمَا إِثْمُهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ يُبَدِّلُونَهُ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-man baddalahu baʿda mā samiʿahu fa-innamā ithmuhu ʿalā lladhīna yubaddilūnahu inna llāha samīʿun ʿalīmun
versets.trad: The sin of one who alters the bequest after hearing it shall fall only upon those who alter it. God, truly, is all-hearing, all-knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever falsifies a word uttered in truth, the ruin and misfortune of that deed fall back upon him; his punishment is to be deprived of breathing the fragrance of truthfulness. And he lays down the hidden law: whoever aids religion, God aids him; whoever leagues against religion, God forsakes him.

mots.def: baddala: to substitute, to falsify.
in full knowledge.

mots.racine: س م ع / ع ل م
mots.def: two Names: He hears and He knows all.

versets.ar: فَمَنْ خَافَ مِن مُّوصٍ جَنَفًا أَوْ إِثْمًا فَأَصْلَحَ بَيْنَهُمْ فَلَا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-man khāfa min mūṣin janafan aw ithman fa-aṣlaḥa baynahum fa-lā ithma ʿalayhi inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: But whoever, fearing that the testator has made an error or committed an injustice, sets it right to reconcile the heirs, commits no sin. God is forgiving, merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from it a rule of spiritual direction: whoever discerns in a disciple a weakness, or in a beginner a slackness of resolve, and judges it good to spare him by a dispensation, an enticement, a leniency, or by granting him something lawful — there is in that no harm, for to force people to pure truthfulness carries little merit. Gentleness toward beginners, when they have as yet no cutting drive nor sincere effort, is a major pillar in the quest for their setting-right.

mots.def: khāfa: to dread, to apprehend.

mots.racine: ج ن ف / أ ث م
mots.def: janaf: an unintended deviation; ithm: a deliberate sin.
aṣlaḥa: to restore concord.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الصِّيَامُ كَمَا كُتِبَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū kutiba ʿalaykumu ṣ-ṣiyāmu kamā kutiba ʿalā lladhīna min qablikum laʿallakum tattaqūna
versets.trad: O you who believe! Fasting is prescribed for you as it was prescribed for those before you, that you may fear God.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī grades the fast: it is of two orders — an outward fast, which is abstention from the things that break it, joined to intention; and an inward fast, which is to guard the heart from evils, then the spirit from familiarities, then the secret from stolen glances. The fast of the worshippers requires guarding the tongue from slander and the eye from the suspect gaze, while the fast of the knowers is to preserve the secret from the contemplation of any other than Him: the one breaks his fast at nightfall, the other when he beholds the Real.

mots.def: ṣiyām: abstention from food, drink, and pleasures with intention.
kataba: to prescribe, to make obligatory.
taqwā: the goal of the fast.

versets.ar: أَيَّامًا مَّعْدُودَاتٍ ۚ فَمَن كَانَ مِنكُم مَّرِيضًا أَوْ عَلَىٰ سَفَرٍ فَعِدَّةٌ مِّنْ أَيَّامٍ أُخَرَ ۚ وَعَلَى الَّذِينَ يُطِيقُونَهُ فِدْيَةٌ طَعَامُ مِسْكِينٍ ۖ فَمَن تَطَوَّعَ خَيْرًا فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّهُ ۚ وَأَن تَصُومُوا خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: ayyāman maʿdūdātin fa-man kāna minkum marīḍan aw ʿalā safarin fa-ʿiddatun min ayyāmin ukhara wa-ʿalā lladhīna yuṭīqūnahu fidyatun ṭaʿāmu miskīnin fa-man taṭawwaʿa khayran fa-huwa khayrun lahu wa-an taṣūmū khayrun lakum in kuntum taʿlamūna
versets.trad: Fast for a counted number of days. But whoever among you is sick or on a journey shall fast an equal number of other days. For those able to fast only with hardship, there is a ransom: the feeding of a poor man. Whoever does more good of his own accord, it is better for him. Yet to fast is better for you, if only you knew.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sets here one of his most beautiful distinctions: whoever beholds the month fasts for God; whoever beholds the Creator of the month fasts through God. To fast for God earns reward, to fast through God earns nearness. To fast for God is the work of every worshipper; to fast through God is the trait of every aspirant. The first abstains from the things that break it; the second abstains at all times from beholding creatures. And he evokes the promised drink: a draught not passed round in cups, but unveiled to the heart by pure subtlety — a drink of intimacy, not a beverage of the cup.

mots.racine: ي و م / ع د د
mots.def: maʿdūdāt: of limited, few number.

mots.sens: an equal number of other days
mots.def: ʿidda: a count of replacement (qaḍāʾ).

mots.sens: who can do it only with hardship
mots.def: aṭāqa: to be able only at great effort.

mots.racine: ف د ي / ط ع م
mots.sens: a ransom: the feeding of a poor man
mots.def: fidya: an expiatory compensation.

versets.ar: شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ الَّذِي أُنزِلَ فِيهِ الْقُرْآنُ هُدًى لِّلنَّاسِ وَبَيِّنَاتٍ مِّنَ الْهُدَىٰ وَالْفُرْقَانِ ۚ فَمَن شَهِدَ مِنكُمُ الشَّهْرَ فَلْيَصُمْهُ ۖ وَمَن كَانَ مَرِيضًا أَوْ عَلَىٰ سَفَرٍ فَعِدَّةٌ مِّنْ أَيَّامٍ أُخَرَ ۗ يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ بِكُمُ الْيُسْرَ وَلَا يُرِيدُ بِكُمُ الْعُسْرَ وَلِتُكْمِلُوا الْعِدَّةَ وَلِتُكَبِّرُوا اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ مَا هَدَاكُمْ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: shahru ramaḍāna lladhī unzila fīhi l-qurʾānu hudan li-n-nāsi wa-bayyinātin mina l-hudā wa-l-furqāni fa-man shahida minkumu sh-shahra fa-l-yaṣumhu wa-man kāna marīḍan aw ʿalā safarin fa-ʿiddatun min ayyāmin ukhara yurīdu llāhu bikumu l-yusra wa-lā yurīdu bikumu l-ʿusra wa-li-tukmilū l-ʿiddata wa-li-tukabbirū llāha ʿalā mā hadākum wa-laʿallakum tashkurūna
versets.trad: The month of Ramadan is that in which the Qurʾān was sent down — a guidance for mankind, a clear manifestation of right direction and of the discernment. Whoever among you is present at the new moon shall fast the whole month. Whoever is sick or on a journey shall make up the missed days afterward. God wills ease for you, He wills not hardship for you. Complete then the appointed period and magnify God for having guided you. May you be thankful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī plays on the very name: Ramadan scorches the sins of some and scorches the vestiges of others — and how great the distance between him whose sins are burned by mercy and him whose traces of self are burned by the divine Reality! He sees in it the month of the opening of discourse, of the descent of the Book, of drawing near, of the easing of toil, of salvation and intimate converse. And on "God wills ease for you": He willed ease for you while you suspected Him of willing your toil; and the sign that He wills your ease is that He has set you to seek it — for had He not willed it for you, He would not have made you desire it.

mots.racine: ش ه ر / ر م ض
mots.def: the month of the descent of the Qurʾān.
hudā: right direction.
furqān: that which separates truth from falsehood.

mots.racine: ر و د / ي س ر
mots.def: yusr: ease, alleviation.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا سَأَلَكَ عِبَادِي عَنِّي فَإِنِّي قَرِيبٌ ۖ أُجِيبُ دَعْوَةَ الدَّاعِ إِذَا دَعَانِ ۖ فَلْيَسْتَجِيبُوا لِي وَلْيُؤْمِنُوا بِي لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْشُدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā saʾalaka ʿibādī ʿannī fa-innī qarībun ujību daʿwata d-dāʿi idhā daʿāni fa-l-yastajībū lī wa-l-yuʾminū bī laʿallahum yarshudūna
versets.trad: When My servants ask you about Me — truly, I am near. I answer the call of the caller when he calls upon Me. Let them then respond to Me and believe in Me, that they may be rightly guided.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī pauses, marvelling, over this verse: they did not ask about a ruling, nor about a creature, nor about this world or the next — they asked Him about Himself. These are a chosen few apart. And the answer God reserves to Himself: He does not say "Tell them: I am near," He says "I am near" — He has lifted away every intermediary between Himself and nearness. Subtler still: His answering your call with good moves you to call upon Him, and it is not your call that moves Him to answer you — as if to say: "if you call upon Me, I answer you; so answer Me too when I call upon you."

mots.racine: س أ ل / ع ب د
mots.sens: My servants ask you about Me
mots.def: saʾala ʿan: to inquire concerning.
qarīb: near by knowledge, hearing, response.

mots.racine: ج و ب / د ع و
mots.sens: I answer the call of the caller
mots.def: ajāba: to grant, to answer.
rashada: to follow rectitude.

versets.ar: أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ لَيْلَةَ الصِّيَامِ الرَّفَثُ إِلَىٰ نِسَائِكُمْ ۚ هُنَّ لِبَاسٌ لَّكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ لِبَاسٌ لَّهُنَّ ۗ عَلِمَ اللَّهُ أَنَّكُمْ كُنتُمْ تَخْتَانُونَ أَنفُسَكُمْ فَتَابَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَعَفَا عَنكُمْ ۖ فَالْآنَ بَاشِرُوهُنَّ وَابْتَغُوا مَا كَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ ۚ وَكُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا حَتَّىٰ يَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمُ الْخَيْطُ الْأَبْيَضُ مِنَ الْخَيْطِ الْأَسْوَدِ مِنَ الْفَجْرِ ۖ ثُمَّ أَتِمُّوا الصِّيَامَ إِلَى اللَّيْلِ ۚ وَلَا تُبَاشِرُوهُنَّ وَأَنتُمْ عَاكِفُونَ فِي الْمَسَاجِدِ ۗ تِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللَّهِ فَلَا تَقْرَبُوهَا ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ آيَاتِهِ لِلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: uḥilla lakum laylata ṣ-ṣiyāmi r-rafathu ilā nisāʾikum hunna libāsun lakum wa-antum libāsun lahunna ʿalima llāhu annakum kuntum takhtānūna anfusakum fa-tāba ʿalaykum wa-ʿafā ʿankum fa-l-āna bāshirūhunna wa-btaghū mā kataba llāhu lakum wa-kulū wa-shrabū ḥattā yatabayyana lakumu l-khayṭu l-abyaḍu mina l-khayṭi l-aswadi mina l-fajri thumma atimmū ṣ-ṣiyāma ilā l-layli wa-lā tubāshirūhunna wa-antum ʿākifūna fī l-masājidi tilka ḥudūdu llāhi fa-lā taqrabūhā kadhālika yubayyinu llāhu āyātihi li-n-nāsi laʿallahum yattaqūna
versets.trad: It is made lawful for you to lie with your wives during the night of the fast. They are a garment for you, and you are a garment for them. God knew that you were betraying yourselves, but He has turned toward you and shown you His pardon. Now, then, lie with your wives and seek what God has prescribed for you. Eat and drink until you can distinguish at dawn the white thread from the black thread. Then complete the fast until nightfall, and lie not with your wives while you are in retreat in the mosques. These are the bounds of God; approach them not. Thus does God make plain His signs to men, that they may fear Him.
themes: the sharing of day and night
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees here a wisdom of solicitude: knowing that the servant cannot do without his portion, He has divided the day and night of this month between His right and your portion: for My right, "complete the fast until nightfall"; for your portion, "eat and drink until the white thread becomes distinct." On the retreat in the mosque: He has announced that the place of the divine power is hallowed from any quest of pleasure; when you are occupied with your souls, you are veiled by yourselves; when you subsist through Us, return no more from Us to yourselves.

mots.racine: ر ف ث / ن س و
mots.def: rafath: marital intimacy.

mots.sens: they are a garment for you
mots.def: libās: that which covers, protects, and adorns.
ikhtāna: to betray, to fail oneself.
ḥudūd: sacred limits not to be crossed.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوَالَكُم بَيْنَكُم بِالْبَاطِلِ وَتُدْلُوا بِهَا إِلَى الْحُكَّامِ لِتَأْكُلُوا فَرِيقًا مِّنْ أَمْوَالِ النَّاسِ بِالْإِثْمِ وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā taʾkulū amwālakum baynakum bi-l-bāṭili wa-tudlū bihā ilā l-ḥukkāmi li-taʾkulū farīqan min amwāli n-nāsi bi-l-ithmi wa-antum taʿlamūna
versets.trad: Do not devour one another's wealth in vanity, nor proffer it to the judges so as to devour wrongfully and knowingly a part of other men's wealth.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī brings the prohibition back to the presence of the Real: when you have recourse to the judges of men, know that God observes you and that His knowledge encompasses you; watch, then, over the place of shame before the Real. For though creatures know appearances, the Real governs secrets: no human judgement covers what is seen by the One who knows the innermost.

mots.def: bāṭil: vain, illegitimate.

mots.racine: د ل و / ح ك م
mots.sens: and proffer it to the judges
mots.def: adlā: to present, to bribe.
to act against the truth one knows.

versets.ar: يَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْأَهِلَّةِ ۖ قُلْ هِيَ مَوَاقِيتُ لِلنَّاسِ وَالْحَجِّ ۗ وَلَيْسَ الْبِرُّ بِأَن تَأْتُوا الْبُيُوتَ مِن ظُهُورِهَا وَلَٰكِنَّ الْبِرَّ مَنِ اتَّقَىٰ ۗ وَأْتُوا الْبُيُوتَ مِنْ أَبْوَابِهَا ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: yasʾalūnaka ʿani l-ahillati qul hiya mawāqītu li-n-nāsi wa-l-ḥajji wa-laysa l-birru bi-an taʾtū l-buyūta min ẓuhūrihā wa-lākinna l-birra mani ttaqā wa-ʾtū l-buyūta min abwābihā wa-ttaqū llāha laʿallakum tufliḥūna
versets.trad: They ask you about the new moons. Say: "They are appointed times for mankind and for the Pilgrimage." Piety does not consist in entering your houses from the rear, but piety is in fearing God. Enter your houses by their doors, and fear God, that you may prosper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the crescents are markers of time for men in their affairs, but also markers for the people of the spiritual state in the changing measure of their states; the renunciants count their litanies by them, and certain elect make of them the calendar of their inward hours. As for piety: it lies not in the observance of outward things, but in the purification of the secrets and the scouring of the consciences. To enter by the door is to approach each thing by its true way.

mots.def: hilāl, pl. ahilla: crescent moons.
mīqāt, pl. mawāqīt: fixed moments.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ب و ب
mots.sens: approach the houses by their doors
mots.def: to approach each thing by its right way.
