versets.ar: وَقَاتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ الَّذِينَ يُقَاتِلُونَكُمْ وَلَا تَعْتَدُوا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ الْمُعْتَدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qātilū fī sabīli llāhi lladhīna yuqātilūnakum wa-lā taʿtadū inna llāha lā yuḥibbu l-muʿtadīna
versets.trad: Fight in the path of God those who fight you, but do not transgress; God loves not the transgressors.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī spiritualizes the command: let your souls be for you trusts of the Real: if He commands to hold them back, hold them and preserve them; and if He commands to deliver them to battle, do not withhold them from His command. Such is the sense of "do not transgress": stand where you are placed, and do what you are commanded — neither falling short through fear, nor going beyond through excess.

mots.def: qātala: to make war.
sabīl Allāh: the cause and way of God.
iʿtadā: to exceed the just measure.

versets.ar: وَاقْتُلُوهُمْ حَيْثُ ثَقِفْتُمُوهُمْ وَأَخْرِجُوهُم مِّنْ حَيْثُ أَخْرَجُوكُمْ ۚ وَالْفِتْنَةُ أَشَدُّ مِنَ الْقَتْلِ ۚ وَلَا تُقَاتِلُوهُمْ عِندَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ حَتَّىٰ يُقَاتِلُوكُمْ فِيهِ ۖ فَإِن قَاتَلُوكُمْ فَاقْتُلُوهُمْ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ جَزَاءُ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qtulūhum ḥaythu thaqiftumūhum wa-akhrijūhum min ḥaythu akhrajūkum wa-l-fitnatu ashaddu mina l-qatli wa-lā tuqātilūhum ʿinda l-masjidi l-ḥarāmi ḥattā yuqātilūkum fīhi fa-in qātalūkum fa-qtulūhum kadhālika jazāʾu l-kāfirīna
versets.trad: Slay them wherever you encounter them; drive them out from where they drove you out. Subversion is graver than slaying. But fight them not near the Sacred Mosque unless they fight you there. If they fight you, slay them; such is the recompense of the disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes: raise enmity against My enemies as you establish alliance with My friends, and "drive them out from where they drove you out" becomes: drive out first their love and their hold from your hearts. On "subversion is graver than slaying," he reaches a summit: the trial that strikes hearts by the onslaught of veils is graver than that which strikes souls by the giving of life, for the loss of the heart's life is worse than the loss of the body's life: the soul lives by its familiarities, but the heart lives only through God — to be estranged from God is more terrible than to be deprived of one's breath.

mots.def: thaqifa: to overtake, to find.

mots.racine: ف ت ن / ق ت ل
mots.def: fitna: discord, persecution, trial.

mots.racine: س ج د / ح ر م
mots.def: the inviolable sanctuary of Mecca.

versets.ar: فَإِنِ انتَهَوْا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-ini ntahaw fa-inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: But if they cease, then God, truly, is forgiving, merciful.
themes: the rest of the inner warrior
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorizes the verse: when the clamours of your thoughts and the enemies of your soul fall silent, that which used to issue from you and jostle you — then let the prosecution of the soul cease, and abandon its battles. For he who is summoned to guard the secrets has no leisure left to wage war against the soul through the manifold forms of struggle: the stilling is itself a forgiving grace.

mots.def: intahā: to desist, to put an end.
ghafūr: He who covers and effaces faults.
raḥīm: constantly merciful.

versets.ar: وَقَاتِلُوهُمْ حَتَّىٰ لَا تَكُونَ فِتْنَةٌ وَيَكُونَ الدِّينُ لِلَّهِ ۖ فَإِنِ انتَهَوْا فَلَا عُدْوَانَ إِلَّا عَلَى الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qātilūhum ḥattā lā takūna fitnatun wa-yakūna d-dīnu li-llāhi fa-ini ntahaw fa-lā ʿudwāna illā ʿalā ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: Fight them until there is no more oppression and worship is rendered to God alone. But if they desist, let there be no hostility save against the wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads herein the greater struggle: the allusion is to the war against the souls, for your fiercest enemy is your soul, the one that lodges between your flanks. Carry the disciplines through to their end until nothing remains of the human traces, and the soul and the heart surrender to God, without dispute or rivalry, neither out of caution nor out of choice, effaced from all self-election, given over to whatever the Real decrees. And once the soul has thus submitted, "no hostility save against the unjust" — that is, against those who persist in falling short.

mots.racine: ك و ن / ف ت ن
mots.sens: until there is no more turmoil
mots.def: fitna: sedition, persecution.

mots.sens: and that worship be to God
mots.def: dīn: religion, obedience, devoted to Him alone.
ʿudwān: unjust aggression.

versets.ar: الشَّهْرُ الْحَرَامُ بِالشَّهْرِ الْحَرَامِ وَالْحُرُمَاتُ قِصَاصٌ ۚ فَمَنِ اعْتَدَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ فَاعْتَدُوا عَلَيْهِ بِمِثْلِ مَا اعْتَدَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: ash-shahru l-ḥarāmu bi-sh-shahri l-ḥarāmi wa-l-ḥurumātu qiṣāṣun fa-mani ʿtadā ʿalaykum fa-ʿtadū ʿalayhi bi-mithli mā ʿtadā ʿalaykum wa-ttaqū llāha wa-ʿlamū anna llāha maʿa l-muttaqīna
versets.trad: A sacred month for a sacred month, and to all violations of the inviolable applies the law of just retaliation. If any enemy assaults you, assault him to the very measure in which he assaulted you. Yet fear God, and know that God is with those who fear Him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from it a rule of discernment: when two rights stand face to face, and both belong to God, render to the moment what the moment demands, and beware of preferring one over the other for the least share of your own interest — be it ever so slight — lest you be veiled from the contemplation of the Real and the clear-sightedness of your heart go blind. And he sets the criterion: whatever is most contrary to your inclination, most remote from your ease, is in itself the most just. "God is with the God-fearing": those who have guarded themselves against preferring their whim to His good pleasure.

mots.racine: ش ه ر / ح ر م
mots.def: a month in which fighting is forbidden.

mots.racine: ح ر م / ق ص ص
mots.def: ḥurumāt: inviolable things.

mots.racine: م ث ل / ع د و
mots.sens: in the measure of his aggression
mots.def: mithl: equivalent, just reciprocity.

versets.ar: وَأَنفِقُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَلَا تُلْقُوا بِأَيْدِيكُمْ إِلَى التَّهْلُكَةِ ۛ وَأَحْسِنُوا ۛ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-anfiqū fī sabīli llāhi wa-lā tulqū bi-aydīkum ilā t-tahlukati wa-aḥsinū inna llāha yuḥibbu l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: Spend in the path of God; do not cast yourselves, by your own hands, into perdition. Excel in goodness. God, in truth, loves those who do good.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ranks spending in four degrees: the rich spend of their wealth; the worshippers spend of their souls, sparing them not in devotions; the knowers spend of their hearts, withholding them not from His decrees; the lovers spend of their spirits, withdrawing them not from His love. The spending of the rich brings wealth out of the purse; that of the poor brings the spirit out of the most precious of beings. And concerning perdition: to hold back your hand and hoard something for yourself is to cast yourself by your own hands into ruin. As for iḥsān, it is to worship Him without heedlessness, to worship Him in the state of contemplation.

mots.def: anfaqa: to spend, to give.
tahluka: ruin, destruction.

mots.racine: ح ب ب / ح س ن
mots.sens: He loves those who do good
mots.def: muḥsin: one who excels in goodness.

versets.ar: وَأَتِمُّوا الْحَجَّ وَالْعُمْرَةَ لِلَّهِ ۚ فَإِنْ أُحْصِرْتُمْ فَمَا اسْتَيْسَرَ مِنَ الْهَدْيِ ۖ وَلَا تَحْلِقُوا رُؤُوسَكُمْ حَتَّىٰ يَبْلُغَ الْهَدْيُ مَحِلَّهُ ۚ فَمَن كَانَ مِنكُم مَّرِيضًا أَوْ بِهِ أَذًى مِّن رَّأْسِهِ فَفِدْيَةٌ مِّن صِيَامٍ أَوْ صَدَقَةٍ أَوْ نُسُكٍ ۚ فَإِذَا أَمِنتُمْ فَمَن تَمَتَّعَ بِالْعُمْرَةِ إِلَى الْحَجِّ فَمَا اسْتَيْسَرَ مِنَ الْهَدْيِ ۚ فَمَن لَّمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيَامُ ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ فِي الْحَجِّ وَسَبْعَةٍ إِذَا رَجَعْتُمْ ۗ تِلْكَ عَشَرَةٌ كَامِلَةٌ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَن لَّمْ يَكُنْ أَهْلُهُ حَاضِرِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ
versets.tr: wa-atimmū l-ḥajja wa-l-ʿumrata li-llāhi fa-in uḥṣirtum fa-mā staysara mina l-hadyi wa-lā taḥliqū ruʾūsakum ḥattā yablugha l-hadyu maḥillahu fa-man kāna minkum marīḍan aw bihi adhan min raʾsihi fa-fidyatun min ṣiyāmin aw ṣadaqatin aw nusukin fa-idhā amintum fa-man tamattaʿa bi-l-ʿumrati ilā l-ḥajji fa-mā staysara mina l-hadyi fa-man lam yajid fa-ṣiyāmu thalāthati ayyāmin fī l-ḥajji wa-sabʿatin idhā rajaʿtum tilka ʿasharatun kāmilatun dhālika li-man lam yakun ahluhu ḥāḍirī l-masjidi l-ḥarāmi wa-ttaqū llāha wa-ʿlamū anna llāha shadīdu l-ʿiqābi
versets.trad: Accomplish, for God, the Pilgrimage and the pious Visitation. If you are prevented, send in compensation whatever offering you can. Do not shave your heads until the offering has reached its appointed place. If any of you is sick or suffers an ailment of the head [obliging him to shave], let him redeem himself by fasting, by an alms, or by a sacrifice. When, having regained security, some of you avail themselves of the Visitation up to the Pilgrimage, let them make whatever offering they can. Whoever cannot find the means shall make up for it by a fast of three days during the Pilgrimage and seven days upon his return, ten days in all. This applies to him whose family does not dwell near the Sacred Mosque. But fear God, and know that God is severe in retribution.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds an entire inner pilgrimage: the ḥajj is an act of intent — intent toward the House of the Real, and intent toward the Real Himself: the first is the pilgrimage of the common folk, the second that of the elect. As the pilgrim of the body enters sacralization, halts, circles and runs, so he who makes pilgrimage with his heart enters into consecration by a sincere undertaking, strips off the garment of his oppositions and cravings, then dons the two pieces of patience and poverty. And he reckons: the best of the ḥajj is the shajj and the ʿajj — the shedding of blood and the raising of the voice — meaning here to shed the blood of the soul with the knives of opposition to desire, and to raise the voice of the secret in unbroken appeal for succor. On hindrance: if the soul-enemy prevails and one must halt at the shelters of dispensations and the Law's interpretations, then one is loosed according to the excuse — for there is no rivalry with the decree.

mots.racine: ت م م / ح ج ج / ع م ر
mots.sens: complete the Pilgrimage and the Visitation for God
mots.def: ḥajj: greater pilgrimage; ʿumra: pious visitation.
uḥṣira: to be blocked by sickness or enemy.
hady: an animal offered during the ḥajj.

mots.racine: ش د د / ع ق ب
mots.def: according to Qushayrī: the veil for him who has not seen the crescents of union.

versets.ar: الْحَجُّ أَشْهُرٌ مَّعْلُومَاتٌ ۚ فَمَن فَرَضَ فِيهِنَّ الْحَجَّ فَلَا رَفَثَ وَلَا فُسُوقَ وَلَا جِدَالَ فِي الْحَجِّ ۗ وَمَا تَفْعَلُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ يَعْلَمْهُ اللَّهُ ۗ وَتَزَوَّدُوا فَإِنَّ خَيْرَ الزَّادِ التَّقْوَىٰ ۚ وَاتَّقُونِ يَا أُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ
versets.tr: al-ḥajju ashhurun maʿlūmātun fa-man faraḍa fīhinna l-ḥajja fa-lā rafatha wa-lā fusūqa wa-lā jidāla fī l-ḥajji wa-mā tafʿalū min khayrin yaʿlamhu llāhu wa-tazawwadū fa-inna khayra z-zādi t-taqwā wa-ttaqūni yā ulī l-albābi
versets.trad: The Pilgrimage falls in months well known. Whoever undertakes it must, throughout its duration, abstain from all carnal union, from licentiousness and from quarreling. Whatever good you do, God knows it. Take provisions for the journey; yet, in truth, the best provision is the fear of God. So fear Me, O you endowed with understanding!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī links the season of pilgrimage to the age of aspiration: as the ḥajj of bodies has its known months outside of which it is not contracted, so the ḥajj of hearts has its proper times — and these are the days of youth; whoever had no aspiration in the season of his vigor has no union in the season of his white hairs. On "no quarreling": let the one who follows the Path tarry over nothing along the way, mingle nothing with his desire; whoever disputes with him or jostles him, let him commit the whole to the All. And the provision: the piety of the common folk is to shun faults; the piety of the elect is to shun, by the secret, all that is other than Him.

mots.racine: ش ه ر / ع ل م
mots.def: months fixed for the pilgrimage.

mots.ar: فَلَا رَفَثَ وَلَا فُسُوقَ وَلَا جِدَالَ
mots.tr: fa-lā rafatha wa-lā fusūqa wa-lā jidāl
mots.racine: ر ف ث / ف س ق / ج د ل
mots.sens: no carnal union, no depravity, no dispute
mots.def: the three prohibitions of the state of sacralization.

mots.racine: ز و د / و ق ي
mots.sens: the best provision is the fear of God
mots.def: zād: viaticum; taqwā: pious fear.

versets.ar: لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَن تَبْتَغُوا فَضْلًا مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۚ فَإِذَا أَفَضْتُم مِّنْ عَرَفَاتٍ فَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ عِندَ الْمَشْعَرِ الْحَرَامِ ۖ وَاذْكُرُوهُ كَمَا هَدَاكُمْ وَإِن كُنتُم مِّن قَبْلِهِ لَمِنَ الضَّالِّينَ
versets.tr: laysa ʿalaykum junāḥun an tabtaghū faḍlan min rabbikum fa-idhā afaḍtum min ʿarafātin fa-dhkurū llāha ʿinda l-mashʿari l-ḥarāmi wa-dhkurūhu kamā hadākum wa-in kuntum min qablihi la-mina ḍ-ḍāllīna
versets.trad: No fault attaches to you in seeking a favor from your Lord. When you surge down from Arafat, invoke God at the sacred monument; and remember how He guided you, though before that you were among those who had gone astray.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sifts the intention: whatever you seek of God's favor that helps you fulfill His right, or that profits the believers and strengthens the religion, is praiseworthy; but whatever you ask to gratify your own portion or for your sole interest is tainted. And on remembrance: when you have stood firm to the fulfilling of the right of His quest, remember His grace toward you: had He not willed you, you would not have willed Him; had He not chosen you, you would not have preferred His good pleasure.

mots.def: junāḥ: sin, wrong.
afāḍa: to pour forth in a throng.

mots.racine: ش ع ر / ح ر م
mots.def: al-Mashʿar al-Ḥarām: the place of halt at Muzdalifa.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ أَفِيضُوا مِنْ حَيْثُ أَفَاضَ النَّاسُ وَاسْتَغْفِرُوا اللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: thumma afīḍū min ḥaythu afāḍa n-nāsu wa-staghfirū llāha inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: Then surge down from where the people surge down. And ask forgiveness of God, for God is forgiving, merciful!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees here a lesson of utmost humility: do not teach your soul to set itself apart from its peers in outward things, neither by a garment, nor by a frock, nor by some distinguishing mark: be as one among men. And, more subtle still: if the thought crosses you that you have accomplished something, or that there is in you, by you, or with you anything at all — ask forgiveness of God and renew your faith, for that is a hidden idolatry that has crept into your heart. To imagine oneself someone is the most veiled of idols.

mots.sens: surge down from where the people surge down
mots.def: to merge into the common stream, without singling oneself out.
istaghfara: to implore pardon.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م
mots.def: two Names of mercy.

versets.ar: فَإِذَا قَضَيْتُم مَّنَاسِكَكُمْ فَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ كَذِكْرِكُمْ آبَاءَكُمْ أَوْ أَشَدَّ ذِكْرًا ۗ فَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَن يَقُولُ رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا وَمَا لَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنْ خَلَاقٍ
versets.tr: fa-idhā qaḍaytum manāsikakum fa-dhkurū llāha ka-dhikrikum ābāʾakum aw ashadda dhikran fa-mina n-nāsi man yaqūlu rabbanā ātinā fī d-dunyā wa-mā lahu fī l-ākhirati min khalāqin
versets.trad: When you have accomplished your rites, invoke God as you were wont to invoke your forefathers, or with an invocation yet more intense. Among men there are those who say: "Our Lord, grant us bounties in this world" — and they shall have no portion in the life to come.
themes: a remembrance more intense than that of the fathers
the prayer bounded by this world
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unwinds the nuances of remembrance: the accomplishing of the rites is a station of the soul; but "remember God as you remembered your fathers" is a station of the heart, in the unbroken span of the moment and the engulfing of one's whole life. And he spins out the comparison: if others boast of their fathers, let your pride be in Us; if your fathers held over you the right of upbringing, Our right over you is more binding and Our favors more complete; you do not weary of recalling your father nor forget him — be constant in recalling Us, untouched by weariness or forgetting. Why "your fathers" and not "your mothers"? The father is recalled in reverence, the mother in tenderness — and God shows mercy without mercy being shown to Him. And "more intensely," because the Real has the greater right.

mots.racine: ق ض ي / ن س ك
mots.def: manāsik: rites of the pilgrimage.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / أ ب و
mots.sens: as your remembrance of your fathers
mots.def: dhikr: evocation, remembrance.

mots.racine: ش د د / ذ ك ر
mots.def: ashadd: stronger, more ardent.
khalāq: lot, share in the hereafter.

versets.ar: وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَقُولُ رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
versets.tr: wa-minhum man yaqūlu rabbanā ātinā fī d-dunyā ḥasanatan wa-fī l-ākhirati ḥasanatan wa-qinā ʿadhāba n-nār
versets.trad: And among them are those who say: "Our Lord, grant us a fair portion in this world and a fair portion in the life to come. And preserve us from the torment of the Fire!"
themes: the fair portion here and hereafter
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the "fair portion" of this world is that one singular grace from which all the others flow — that faith be kept to the last breath, for whoever departs this world a believer is not made eternal in the Fire; and the "fair portion" of the other world is forgiveness, after which there is only pure good. He adds, rising a degree: the fair portion of this world is contemplation through the inmost secrets; that of the next, the vision through the very eyes. The fair portion of this world is that He not distract you from Him by your own self; that of the next, that He not return you to your own self. The "torment of the Fire" from which one begs to be preserved includes the fire of separation as much as that of burning.

mots.def: the total good from which all goods derive: here below, faith kept; hereafter, forgiveness.
protection from the fire of burning and the fire of separation.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمْ نَصِيبٌ مِّمَّا كَسَبُوا ۚ وَاللَّهُ سَرِيعُ الْحِسَابِ
versets.tr: ulāʾika lahum naṣībun mimmā kasabū wa-llāhu sarīʿu l-ḥisāb
versets.trad: To such will come a portion of what they have earned. And God is swift in reckoning.
themes: the portion of what is earned
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes three sorts of men: he who claims nothing but this world, he who asks for both abodes, and a third whom the verse passes over in silence — those content with the decree, submissive to the command, set at peace beyond all request and all demand. As for "God is swift in reckoning": for the common folk, the swiftness lies in the instant of the Judgment; for the elite, it lies at every breath of life.

mots.def: the requital of what one has earned: good for good, and the reverse.

mots.racine: س ر ع / ح س ب
mots.def: for the elite, the reckoning is held at every breath.

versets.ar: وَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ فِي أَيَّامٍ مَّعْدُودَاتٍ ۚ فَمَن تَعَجَّلَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ فَلَا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ وَمَن تَأَخَّرَ فَلَا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ ۚ لِمَنِ اتَّقَىٰ ۗ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّكُمْ إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-dhkurū llāha fī ayyāmin maʿdūdātin fa-man taʿajjala fī yawmayni fa-lā ithma ʿalayhi wa-man taʾakhkhara fa-lā ithma ʿalayhi li-mani ttaqā wa-ttaqū llāha wa-ʿlamū annakum ilayhi tuḥsharūn
versets.trad: Invoke God on the appointed days. Whoever hastens to complete this rite within two days commits no sin, and whoever lingers commits no sin, provided he fears God. Fear God, and know that unto Him you shall be gathered.
versets.commentaire: These verses describe the completion of the rites. Qushayrī sees in them a sign: he whose soul has found peace, whose heart has surrendered, and who abides in the realities of contemplation — should some branch of the litanies escape him, what he keeps of the courtesies of presence stands in its place and makes up for what is absent. The outward leniency is granted only to him whose inward being remains in remembrance.

mots.racine: ي و م / ع د د
mots.def: the days of Minā, the completion of the pilgrimage rites.
the final return unto God, foundation of the fear.

versets.ar: وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَن يُعْجِبُكَ قَوْلُهُ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَيُشْهِدُ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ مَا فِي قَلْبِهِ وَهُوَ أَلَدُّ الْخِصَامِ
versets.tr: wa-mina n-nāsi man yuʿjibuka qawluhu fī l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-yushhidu llāha ʿalā mā fī qalbihi wa-huwa aladdu l-khiṣām
versets.trad: Among men there is he whose speech delights you when he talks of the life of this world. He takes God to witness over what his heart contains; yet he is the most relentless of disputants.
themes: seductive speech and a closed heart
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: there are people from whose hearts the Truth has turned away; they have been given, in appearance, a glib tongue, but upon their hearts have been knotted the causes of deprivation. They lie beneath the veil of their own ignorance: no meaning behind their speech, no support in what they say, no trust in their faith. The beauty of the word attests to nothing when the heart is barred from the light of discernment.

mots.racine: ع ج ب / ق و ل
mots.def: outward fluency of the tongue masking inward deprivation.

mots.racine: ل د د / خ ص م
mots.def: stubborn in dispute, with no support of truth.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا تَوَلَّىٰ سَعَىٰ فِي الْأَرْضِ لِيُفْسِدَ فِيهَا وَيُهْلِكَ الْحَرْثَ وَالنَّسْلَ ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ الْفَسَادَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā tawallā saʿā fī l-arḍi li-yufsida fīhā wa-yuhlika l-ḥartha wa-n-nasla wa-llāhu lā yuḥibbu l-fasād
versets.trad: As soon as he turns his back, he hastens through the land to sow corruption there and to destroy the crops and the cattle; but God loves not corruption.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this is an allusion to him whose every effort is bent on capturing his own portions; he cares nothing that the bonds of religion come undone or that the foundations of Islam grow weak, so long as the cords of his worldly life are drawn tight and the means of his cravings are set in order. The truly manifest corruption is that which ruins the inner order under the pretext of arranging the outer one.

mots.def: to turn away, to go off far from guidance.

mots.racine: ح ر ث / ن س ل
mots.def: the living goods that corruption destroys.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُ اتَّقِ اللَّهَ أَخَذَتْهُ الْعِزَّةُ بِالْإِثْمِ ۚ فَحَسْبُهُ جَهَنَّمُ ۚ وَلَبِئْسَ الْمِهَادُ
versets.tr: wa-idhā qīla lahu ttaqi llāha akhadhat-hu l-ʿizzatu bi-l-ithmi fa-ḥasbuhu jahannamu wa-la-biʾsa l-mihād
versets.trad: When it is said to him, "Fear God!", arrogance seizes him and drives him to sin. His portion shall be Hell: what a wretched resting-place!
themes: the pride that refuses the reminder
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these are people whom arrogance has invaded, who have lost the humility of fairness; their noses are raised away from any welcome of the truth. If you enjoin good upon them, they reply: "To someone like me is such a thing said?" And he makes Hell, here below already, their present share: desolation, the darkness of the soul, the narrowness of choice — before the passage toward the greater torment.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / أ ث م
mots.def: pride that drives to sin by refusing the reminder.
litter, bed: here, Hell by bitter antiphrasis.

versets.ar: وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَن يَشْرِي نَفْسَهُ ابْتِغَاءَ مَرْضَاتِ اللَّهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ رَءُوفٌ بِالْعِبَادِ
versets.tr: wa-mina n-nāsi man yashrī nafsahu btighāʾa marḍāti llāhi wa-llāhu raʾūfun bi-l-ʿibād
versets.trad: And among men there is he who sells his very soul in quest of God's good pleasure. And God is most compassionate toward His servants.
themes: selling one's soul for God's good pleasure
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these are they whom the properties of mercy have reached, whom the antecedents of the allotment have qualified; they preferred the good pleasure of the Real to their own souls, and surrendered themselves wholly to their Master. And he reverses the apparent order: it is by His tenderness that they attained these states — not by these states that they merited His tenderness.

mots.racine: ش ر ي / ن ف س
mots.def: to surrender oneself entirely in quest of the divine good pleasure.
a divine Name: the Tenderness that precedes and causes the states.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ادْخُلُوا فِي السِّلْمِ كَافَّةً وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا خُطُوَاتِ الشَّيْطَانِ ۚ إِنَّهُ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū dkhulū fī s-silmi kāffatan wa-lā tattabiʿū khuṭuwāti sh-shayṭāni innahu lakum ʿaduwwun mubīn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Enter into the peace of submission, every one of you, and do not follow the footsteps of Satan: he is, for you, a manifest enemy.
themes: entering fully into the peace of submission
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the believer is charged to make peace with every being, save with his own soul; for the soul moves only by opposing its Master. Whoever makes peace with his soul slackens in his struggle — and this is the cause that cuts off every wayfarer, the motive that halts every aspirant. The "footsteps of Satan" are his promptings: that you be made to feel your incapacity, or be driven to worthless impulses to which no heed should be paid.

mots.def: islām and entire surrender; peace with all save the rebellious soul.

mots.racine: خ ط و / ش ط ن
mots.def: his promptings and suggestions leading to error.

versets.ar: فَإِن زَلَلْتُم مِّن بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَتْكُمُ الْبَيِّنَاتُ فَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-in zalaltum min baʿdi mā jāʾatkumu l-bayyinātu fa-ʿlamū anna llāha ʿazīzun ḥakīm
versets.trad: If you should stumble after the clear evidences have come to you, then know that God is mighty and wise.
themes: the gravity of the misstep after the evidence
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: a single misstep after the unveiling of the proof is more hideous than many missteps before it; and one whom we have known to betray, we no longer trust with the deposit. He warns the great: when the trial descends upon men of high rank, it carries off everything of them — for the loftier the station, the more exacting the fidelity.

mots.def: to slip, to err after the knowledge of the proof.
manifest proofs that aggravate the responsibility for the misstep.

versets.ar: هَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا أَن يَأْتِيَهُمُ اللَّهُ فِي ظُلَلٍ مِّنَ الْغَمَامِ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ وَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ ۚ وَإِلَى اللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ
versets.tr: hal yanẓurūna illā an yaʾtiyahumu llāhu fī ẓulalin mina l-ghamāmi wa-l-malāʾikatu wa-quḍiya l-amru wa-ilā llāhi turjaʿu l-umūr
versets.trad: What do they await, save that God should come to them with the angels, in the shadows of the clouds, and that the decree should have fallen? For unto God all matters return.
versets.commentaire: Men reckoned the Hour to be far off; they are warned of its terrible imminence. Qushayrī is careful to preserve the transcendence: "the decree has fallen" means the veil of mystery has been rent over the clearness of the eternal foreordainment. The hearts of those who profess Unity, by the lights of intuition, have no need of any forced interpretation of this verse, for the Real is hallowed beyond all displacement, all decline, all attachment to a place or a time; He is holy above all motion and all coming.

mots.racine: ظ ل ل / غ م م
mots.def: an image of the majesty of the Day, assigning to God neither place nor motion.

mots.racine: ق ض ي / أ م ر
mots.def: the veil of mystery is rent over the foreordainment.

versets.ar: سَلْ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ كَمْ آتَيْنَاهُم مِّنْ آيَةٍ بَيِّنَةٍ ۗ وَمَن يُبَدِّلْ نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ مِن بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَتْهُ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ
versets.tr: sal banī isrāʾīla kam ātaynāhum min āyatin bayyinatin wa-man yubaddil niʿmata llāhi min baʿdi mā jāʾathu fa-inna llāha shadīdu l-ʿiqāb
versets.trad: Ask the Children of Israel how many irrefutable signs We gave them! But as for him who alters the favor of God after having received it, God is severe in retribution.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the use of the question is not to instruct the Messenger, but to establish the argument against them. And he scrutinizes the threat that hangs over the altering of the favor: only when that grace departs do they at last know its worth; they weep for it and reach it never again. He cites this verse: "You will forsake me, you will abandon me — then you will seek me, and find me no more."

mots.def: to substitute disbelief for the favor received.

mots.racine: ش د د / ع ق ب
mots.def: by the very withdrawal of the grace whose worth is then known.

versets.ar: زُيِّنَ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا وَيَسْخَرُونَ مِنَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ۘ وَالَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا فَوْقَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَاءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ
versets.tr: zuyyina li-lladhīna kafarū l-ḥayātu d-dunyā wa-yaskharūna mina lladhīna āmanū wa-lladhīna ttaqaw fawqahum yawma l-qiyāmati wa-llāhu yarzuqu man yashāʾu bi-ghayri ḥisāb
versets.trad: To those who disbelieve the life of this world has been made alluring. They have mocked the believers. But those who feared God shall be above them on the Day of Resurrection. God bestows His bounty upon whom He will, beyond all reckoning.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they were deceived without sensing it; the thickness of darkness over their intuitions drove them to assail His intimates and to mock them. But when the bewilderment of ignorance is dispelled from their hearts, they will at last know which of them was the loser, which was sinking in a far-flung error. The adornment of this world was itself the divine stratagem that the heedless failed to perceive.

mots.def: the embellishment of this world that veils and tries.
limitless divine bounty toward whom He will.

versets.ar: كَانَ النَّاسُ أُمَّةً وَاحِدَةً فَبَعَثَ اللَّهُ النَّبِيِّينَ مُبَشِّرِينَ وَمُنذِرِينَ وَأَنزَلَ مَعَهُمُ الْكِتَابَ بِالْحَقِّ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ فِيمَا اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ ۚ وَمَا اخْتَلَفَ فِيهِ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ أُوتُوهُ مِن بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَتْهُمُ الْبَيِّنَاتُ بَغْيًا بَيْنَهُمْ ۖ فَهَدَى اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لِمَا اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ مِنَ الْحَقِّ بِإِذْنِهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يَهْدِي مَن يَشَاءُ إِلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: kāna n-nāsu ummatan wāḥidatan fa-baʿatha llāhu n-nabiyyīna mubashshirīna wa-mundhirīna wa-anzala maʿahumu l-kitāba bi-l-ḥaqqi li-yaḥkuma bayna n-nāsi fīmā khtalafū fīhi wa-mā khtalafa fīhi illā lladhīna ūtūhu min baʿdi mā jāʾat-humu l-bayyinātu baghyan baynahum fa-hadā llāhu lladhīna āmanū li-mā khtalafū fīhi mina l-ḥaqqi bi-idhnihi wa-llāhu yahdī man yashāʾu ilā ṣirāṭin mustaqīm
versets.trad: Mankind was a single community. Then God sent the prophets to bring them glad tidings and to warn them. With them He sent down the Book and the Truth, to judge between men and to settle their disputes. Yet those very ones who had received the Book fell into disagreement about it, out of mutual jealousy, after clear evidences had come to them. But God, by His leave, guided the believers to the truth of that over which they had disputed. God guides whom He will onto a straight path.
themes: the first unity and the division
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: absence from the Real gathered them together; at the coming of the messengers they split apart according to what they had received — or were deprived of — of the lights of intuition. Then he seals it upon the decree: men were all united in His knowledge, then scattered in His judgment: these He guided, those He led astray; these drawn by grace, those bound by abandonment. For the favored, no merit earned; for the rejected, no cause — it is a decree decided and a verdict fixed.

mots.racine: أ م م / و ح د
mots.def: the first unity of men before the sending of the prophets.
the cause of the division despite the evidence received.

versets.ar: أَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ أَن تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ وَلَمَّا يَأْتِكُم مَّثَلُ الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِن قَبْلِكُم ۖ مَّسَّتْهُمُ الْبَأْسَاءُ وَالضَّرَّاءُ وَزُلْزِلُوا حَتَّىٰ يَقُولَ الرَّسُولُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ مَتَىٰ نَصْرُ اللَّهِ ۗ أَلَا إِنَّ نَصْرَ اللَّهِ قَرِيبٌ
versets.tr: am ḥasibtum an tadkhulū l-jannata wa-lammā yaʾtikum mathalu lladhīna khalaw min qablikum massat-humu l-baʾsāʾu wa-ḍ-ḍarrāʾu wa-zulzilū ḥattā yaqūla r-rasūlu wa-lladhīna āmanū maʿahu matā naṣru llāhi alā inna naṣra llāhi qarīb
versets.trad: Do you suppose you will enter the Garden, while there has not yet befallen you anything like what befell those who passed away before you? Adversity and affliction struck them, and they were so shaken that the Messenger and the believers with him cried out: "When, then, will the help of God come?" Lo, assuredly the help of God is near.
versets.commentaire: Here Qushayrī offers one of his pearls: God created the Garden and surrounded it with trials; He created the Fire and surrounded it with desires and cravings. Whoever shrinks from facing the terrors is barred from attaining the hoped-for things. And he describes the law of the intimates: the saints never encamp on the land of victory until they have first overlooked the plains of despair; when the waiting has stretched long, grace overtakes them of a sudden, and the thing desired comes to pass unawares.

mots.racine: ب أ س / ض ر ر
mots.def: the trials that surround the Garden and qualify the intimates.

mots.racine: ن ص ر / ق ر ب
mots.sens: the help of God is near
mots.def: it comes of a sudden, after one has overlooked despair.

versets.ar: يَسْأَلُونَكَ مَاذَا يُنفِقُونَ ۖ قُلْ مَا أَنفَقْتُم مِّنْ خَيْرٍ فَلِلْوَالِدَيْنِ وَالْأَقْرَبِينَ وَالْيَتَامَىٰ وَالْمَسَاكِينِ وَابْنِ السَّبِيلِ ۗ وَمَا تَفْعَلُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ بِهِ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: yasʾalūnaka mādhā yunfiqūna qul mā anfaqtum min khayrin fa-li-l-wālidayni wa-l-aqrabīna wa-l-yatāmā wa-l-masākīni wa-bni s-sabīli wa-mā tafʿalū min khayrin fa-inna llāha bihi ʿalīm
versets.trad: They ask you what they should spend. Say: "Whatever good you spend shall be for your parents, your kin, the orphans, the poor, and the wayfarer. And whatever good you do, God indeed knows it well."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they knew that the servant is not the sole author of his act, that he has power over nothing without the leave of his Master; so in spending they stopped at what the detail of the leave indicated — for servanthood is to halt where the Command halts you. And the order of the beneficiaries — parents first, then kin — marks a hierarchy: "the most entitled to your wealth are your two parents, then your kin, according to the established rank."

mots.def: the alms regulated by the divine leave and command.

mots.racine: ب ن و / س ب ل
mots.def: one who is on the road, the last of the named beneficiaries.

versets.ar: كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْقِتَالُ وَهُوَ كُرْهٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ وَعَسَىٰ أَن تَكْرَهُوا شَيْئًا وَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ وَعَسَىٰ أَن تُحِبُّوا شَيْئًا وَهُوَ شَرٌّ لَّكُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: kutiba ʿalaykumu l-qitālu wa-huwa kurhun lakum wa-ʿasā an takrahū shayʾan wa-huwa khayrun lakum wa-ʿasā an tuḥibbū shayʾan wa-huwa sharrun lakum wa-llāhu yaʿlamu wa-antum lā taʿlamūn
versets.trad: Fighting is prescribed for you, though you hold it in aversion. Yet it may be that you have aversion for a thing while it is a good for you. And it may be that you love a thing while it is an evil for you. God knows, but you, you do not know.
themes: the aversion that conceals a good
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: fighting weighed heavy upon the souls; but it was shown that the rest of the souls is deferred, for it belongs to discipline, whereas the rest of the hearts is hastened, for it belongs to nearness. Felicity lies in the contrariety of the soul: whoever follows it strays from perfect love; and happiness lies in accord with the heart: whoever turns from it wanders off the high path. The glad tidings of the ease promised by the Real deserves more to be welcomed than the dreads the soul suggests.

mots.def: made obligatory, written down as a duty.
the soul's repugnance, which may veil a good.

versets.ar: يَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الشَّهْرِ الْحَرَامِ قِتَالٍ فِيهِ ۖ قُلْ قِتَالٌ فِيهِ كَبِيرٌ ۖ وَصَدٌّ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَكُفْرٌ بِهِ وَالْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ وَإِخْرَاجُ أَهْلِهِ مِنْهُ أَكْبَرُ عِندَ اللَّهِ ۚ وَالْفِتْنَةُ أَكْبَرُ مِنَ الْقَتْلِ ۗ وَلَا يَزَالُونَ يُقَاتِلُونَكُمْ حَتَّىٰ يَرُدُّوكُمْ عَن دِينِكُمْ إِنِ اسْتَطَاعُوا ۚ وَمَن يَرْتَدِدْ مِنكُمْ عَن دِينِهِ فَيَمُتْ وَهُوَ كَافِرٌ فَأُولَٰئِكَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَالُهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ ۖ وَأُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: yasʾalūnaka ʿani sh-shahri l-ḥarāmi qitālin fīhi qul qitālun fīhi kabīrun wa-ṣaddun ʿan sabīli llāhi wa-kufrun bihi wa-l-masjidi l-ḥarāmi wa-ikhrāju ahlihi minhu akbaru ʿinda llāhi wa-l-fitnatu akbaru mina l-qatli wa-lā yazālūna yuqātilūnakum ḥattā yaruddūkum ʿan dīnikum ini staṭāʿū wa-man yartadid minkum ʿan dīnihi fa-yamut wa-huwa kāfirun fa-ulāʾika ḥabiṭat aʿmāluhum fī d-dunyā wa-l-ākhirati wa-ulāʾika aṣḥābu n-nāri hum fīhā khālidūn
versets.trad: They ask you about fighting during the sacred month. Say: "To fight in it is a grave sin; but to bar men from the path of God, to deny Him and the Sacred Mosque, and to drive out its people from it — all this is graver still before God." Subversion is graver than fighting. Those who fight you will not cease until they have turned you away from your religion, if they can. Whoever among you forsakes his religion and dies a disbeliever — their deeds shall come to nothing in this world and the next. Such are the inmates of the Fire; therein they abide forever.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: some faults are graver than others: the breach of decorum committed at the Door does not entail what the breach committed upon the Carpet entails. When the misstep comes from the soul, its penalty is deferred — it is the burning; when it comes from the heart, the penalty is hastened — it is the separation. And the effect of heedlessness upon the hearts is greater than the harm of the misstep upon the souls: for the soul abides for its own portion, but the heart abides for the Real. As for apostasy: the people of heedlessness want to draw you back to their state; whoever breaks his pact with God, his heart is transmuted.

mots.def: the trial that turns one from religion, graver than killing.
the annulment of works by apostasy persisted in until death.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَالَّذِينَ هَاجَرُوا وَجَاهَدُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ أُولَٰئِكَ يَرْجُونَ رَحْمَتَ اللَّهِ ۚ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna āmanū wa-lladhīna hājarū wa-jāhadū fī sabīli llāhi ulāʾika yarjūna raḥmata llāhi wa-llāhu ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: In truth, those who have believed, those who have emigrated and striven in the path of God — such may hope for the mercy of God. And God is forgiving, merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those who were sincere in their aim, pure in their pact, who did not turn back upon their heels in aspiration — such lived in the breath of hope until they attained the perfection of subsistence in God and the abode of the encounter. Emigration and striving are full only when borne by sincerity of aim.

mots.def: to forsake for God; inwardly, to forsake the soul and its portions.
to live in the breath of hope until the encounter.

versets.ar: يَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ ۖ قُلْ فِيهِمَا إِثْمٌ كَبِيرٌ وَمَنَافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ وَإِثْمُهُمَا أَكْبَرُ مِن نَّفْعِهِمَا ۗ وَيَسْأَلُونَكَ مَاذَا يُنفِقُونَ قُلِ الْعَفْوَ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ لَكُمُ الْآيَاتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَفَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: yasʾalūnaka ʿani l-khamri wa-l-maysiri qul fīhimā ithmun kabīrun wa-manāfiʿu li-n-nāsi wa-ithmuhumā akbaru min nafʿihimā wa-yasʾalūnaka mādhā yunfiqūna quli l-ʿafwa kadhālika yubayyinu llāhu lakumu l-āyāti laʿallakum tatafakkarūn
versets.trad: They ask you about wine and games of chance. Say: "In both there is, for men, a great sin and certain benefits, but the sin in them is greater than their benefit." They also ask you what they should spend. Say: "The surplus!" Thus does God make the signs clear to you, that you may reflect
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī widens the sense: "wine" (khamr) is whatever veils the intellects; as wine is itself unlawful, so too is intoxication. Whoever is intoxicated by the wine of heedlessness deserves, from the standpoint of the allusions, what the wine-drinker deserves: just as the drunken man is kept from prayer, the man drunk with heedlessness is veiled from the bonds with God — let whoever doubts it put it to the test. As for the surplus: it is what exceeds your need — the way of the elite; but the elite of the elite prefer another to themselves, even in their own want.

mots.def: all that veils the intellect; allusively, the intoxication of heedlessness.
what exceeds need; the elite prefer another to themselves.

versets.ar: فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ ۗ وَيَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْيَتَامَىٰ ۖ قُلْ إِصْلَاحٌ لَّهُمْ خَيْرٌ ۖ وَإِن تُخَالِطُوهُمْ فَإِخْوَانُكُمْ ۚ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ الْمُفْسِدَ مِنَ الْمُصْلِحِ ۚ وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَأَعْنَتَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: fī d-dunyā wa-l-ākhirati wa-yasʾalūnaka ʿani l-yatāmā qul iṣlāḥun lahum khayrun wa-in tukhāliṭūhum fa-ikhwānukum wa-llāhu yaʿlamu l-mufsida mina l-muṣliḥi wa-law shāʾa llāhu la-aʿnatakum inna llāha ʿazīzun ḥakīm
versets.trad: upon this world and the world to come. They ask you about the orphans. Say: "To do what is best for them is good; and if you mingle their property with yours, then they are your brethren." God knows how to tell the corrupter from the reformer. Had God so willed, He would have burdened you. God is mighty and wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to set their condition right by a thorough upbringing is better than merely making their property grow; and patience in bearing with them, while giving them sincere counsel, is better than the ease taken in evasion. Then on "God knows how to tell the corrupter from the reformer": He deals with each according to what lies at the bottom of his heart in the way of intentions, not according to the appearances of his act.

mots.def: to set their condition right, an upbringing beyond mere money.

mots.racine: ف س د / ص ل ح
mots.def: God judges by the depth of the heart, not the appearance.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَنكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكَاتِ حَتَّىٰ يُؤْمِنَّ ۚ وَلَأَمَةٌ مُّؤْمِنَةٌ خَيْرٌ مِّن مُّشْرِكَةٍ وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ ۗ وَلَا تُنكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ حَتَّىٰ يُؤْمِنُوا ۚ وَلَعَبْدٌ مُّؤْمِنٌ خَيْرٌ مِّن مُّشْرِكٍ وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَكُمْ ۗ أُولَٰئِكَ يَدْعُونَ إِلَى النَّارِ ۖ وَاللَّهُ يَدْعُو إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ وَالْمَغْفِرَةِ بِإِذْنِهِ ۖ وَيُبَيِّنُ آيَاتِهِ لِلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā tankiḥū l-mushrikāti ḥattā yuʾminna wa-la-amatun muʾminatun khayrun min mushrikatin wa-law aʿjabatkum wa-lā tunkiḥū l-mushrikīna ḥattā yuʾminū wa-la-ʿabdun muʾminun khayrun min mushrikin wa-law aʿjabakum ulāʾika yadʿūna ilā n-nāri wa-llāhu yadʿū ilā l-jannati wa-l-maghfirati bi-idhnihi wa-yubayyinu āyātihi li-n-nāsi laʿallahum yatadhakkarūn
versets.trad: Do not marry idolatresses until they believe; a believing bondwoman is better than an idolatress, even though the latter please you. And do not give your women in marriage to idolaters until they believe; a believing bondman is better than an idolater, even though the latter please you. Those people summon to the Fire; God, for His part, summons to the Garden and to forgiveness, by His leave. He makes His signs clear to men, that they may remember.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to bind anew the cable of religion and to cleave to the protecting tie of the believers is better than to consent to a union that leads someone toward disbelief. And though the legal dispensation be granted for the deed, the allusion of the Reality forbids it, from the standpoint of the innocence of the heart in its choice. The verse sets the two summonses against each other: they invite to the Fire, God invites to the Garden and to forgiveness — the contrast of death and life.

mots.def: those who associate partners with God; union forbidden before faith.

mots.racine: د ع و / ن و ر
mots.def: opposed to God's summons to the Garden and to forgiveness.

versets.ar: وَيَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْمَحِيضِ ۖ قُلْ هُوَ أَذًى فَاعْتَزِلُوا النِّسَاءَ فِي الْمَحِيضِ ۖ وَلَا تَقْرَبُوهُنَّ حَتَّىٰ يَطْهُرْنَ ۖ فَإِذَا تَطَهَّرْنَ فَأْتُوهُنَّ مِنْ حَيْثُ أَمَرَكُمُ اللَّهُ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ التَّوَّابِينَ وَيُحِبُّ الْمُتَطَهِّرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yasʾalūnaka ʿani l-maḥīḍi qul huwa adhan fa-ʿtazilū n-nisāʾa fī l-maḥīḍi wa-lā taqrabūhunna ḥattā yaṭhurna fa-idhā taṭahharna fa-ʾtūhunna min ḥaythu amarakumu llāhu inna llāha yuḥibbu t-tawwābīna wa-yuḥibbu l-mutaṭahhirīn
versets.trad: They ask you about menstruation. Say: "It is an impurity. So keep apart from women during the menstrual period, and do not approach them until they are pure. When they have purified themselves, come to them in the manner God has prescribed for you." God loves those who turn to Him in repentance, and He loves those who purify themselves.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: all that arouses shame and aversion is not of the servant's choosing; there are deficiencies in which he has no acquisition — pure beginning of the Real's decree. And he notes a gentleness: kept from the prayer that is the presence of the body, women are yet not veiled from the continuity of remembrance by the heart and the tongue — and this is to expose oneself to the carpet of proximity. Then he spins out the senses: He loves the repentant from the misstep and the purified from the illusion that their salvation comes from their own repentance; the repentant by the water of seeking forgiveness, the purified by the dew of the water of shame under the seal of brokenness.

mots.def: a state decreed by God, beyond the servant's acquisition.

mots.racine: ت و ب / ط ه ر
mots.def: repentant from the misstep, purified from illusion and impurity.
mots.gram: two nouns, objects of the divine love

versets.ar: نِسَاؤُكُمْ حَرْثٌ لَّكُمْ فَأْتُوا حَرْثَكُمْ أَنَّىٰ شِئْتُمْ ۖ وَقَدِّمُوا لِأَنفُسِكُمْ ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّكُم مُّلَاقُوهُ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: nisāʾukum ḥarthun lakum fa-ʾtū ḥarthakum annā shiʾtum wa-qaddimū li-anfusikum wa-ttaqū llāha wa-ʿlamū annakum mulāqūhu wa-bashshiri l-muʾminīn
versets.trad: Your wives are a tilth for you: come, then, to your tilth as you will, but first send ahead some good deed for yourselves. Fear God, and know that you shall meet Him; and give glad tidings to the believers.
themes: the advance laid up for oneself
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: because the souls are in absence, far from the Reality, they are permitted to find rest with their own kind, where the leave is granted; but because the hearts are in the place of presence, they are forbidden to find rest with any other than God. And on "send ahead for yourselves": send ahead the pious works that will profit you on the day of your bankruptcy; look to yourselves by sending ahead that whose finding will gladden you in the presence of your Lord.

mots.def: an image of fruitfulness; the leave within the divine frame.
the certainty that grounds the fear and the laying-up of good works.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَجْعَلُوا اللَّهَ عُرْضَةً لِّأَيْمَانِكُمْ أَن تَبَرُّوا وَتَتَّقُوا وَتُصْلِحُوا بَيْنَ النَّاسِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lā tajʿalū llāha ʿurḍatan li-aymānikum an tabarrū wa-tattaqū wa-tuṣliḥū bayna n-nāsi wa-llāhu samīʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: Do not make God a pretext in your oaths, to excuse yourselves from being upright, God-fearing, and from bringing concord among men. God is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, brief and trenchant: hallow the remembrance of your Lord from every debasement in the service of any portion whatever. And he adds: do not make the remembrance of God a snare to seize the scraps of this world. The Name is too lofty to serve as an instrument for self-interest.

mots.def: to make the Name of God an obstacle or a profane instrument.
the good end that the abusive oath obstructs.

versets.ar: لَّا يُؤَاخِذُكُمُ اللَّهُ بِاللَّغْوِ فِي أَيْمَانِكُمْ وَلَٰكِن يُؤَاخِذُكُم بِمَا كَسَبَتْ قُلُوبُكُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ حَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: lā yuʾākhidhukumu llāhu bi-l-laghwi fī aymānikum wa-lākin yuʾākhidhukum bi-mā kasabat qulūbukum wa-llāhu ghafūrun ḥalīm
versets.trad: God will not take you to task for an oath uttered thoughtlessly; but He will call you to account for what your hearts have earned. God is forgiving and forbearing.
themes: the idle oath and the heart's earning
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: what the tongue lets slip in inadvertence carries little weight, in good or ill; but what the consciences have folded away, what the hidden hearts have held — true intentions, firm resolves — that is what one is called to account for: if it is good, fair reward; if it is ill, long penalty. God looks to the depth, not to the slip.

mots.def: speech without weight, uttered in inadvertence, not imputed.

mots.racine: ك س ب / ق ل ب
mots.def: the intentions and resolves of the depth, object of the reckoning.

versets.ar: لِّلَّذِينَ يُؤْلُونَ مِن نِّسَائِهِمْ تَرَبُّصُ أَرْبَعَةِ أَشْهُرٍ ۖ فَإِن فَاءُوا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: li-lladhīna yuʾlūna min nisāʾihim tarabbuṣu arbaʿati ashhurin fa-in fāʾū fa-inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: For those who swear to abstain from their wives a respite of four months is prescribed. But if they turn back from their resolve, God is forgiving, merciful.
themes: the respite of the oath of abstinence
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws the allusion: if the right of the companionship of your equals is so guarded for you — to the point that, having neglected it, you are called to account — how much more does the right of the Real deserve to be observed. On "if they turn back": that is, that they return to reviving what they had let die, to making up what they had spoiled. And he notes the divine tenderness toward the wife, whose voice is faint because she is captive in the husband's hand: God Himself takes upon Himself the defense of her right.

mots.def: īlāʾ: an oath not to approach the wife, bounded at four months.
to revive what one had let die, to make up what one has spoiled.

versets.ar: وَإِنْ عَزَمُوا الطَّلَاقَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-in ʿazamū ṭ-ṭalāqa fa-inna llāha samīʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: And if they resolve upon divorce, God is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if the husband wearies of the right of her companionship and hardens his resolve to leave her, God is informed of his state and his secret; and should some repentance come to him, let him not hasten toward the pillars of divorce, for God knows whether he has truly divorced. And he sees in "the All-Hearing" a consolation for the woman: We have heard the desolation of this word — and it is a consolation that the Real addresses to her.

mots.def: to harden the decision to separate.

mots.racine: س م ع / ع ل م
mots.def: a consolation for the wife whose voice is faint.

versets.ar: وَالْمُطَلَّقَاتُ يَتَرَبَّصْنَ بِأَنفُسِهِنَّ ثَلَاثَةَ قُرُوءٍ ۚ وَلَا يَحِلُّ لَهُنَّ أَن يَكْتُمْنَ مَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ فِي أَرْحَامِهِنَّ إِن كُنَّ يُؤْمِنَّ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ ۚ وَبُعُولَتُهُنَّ أَحَقُّ بِرَدِّهِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ إِنْ أَرَادُوا إِصْلَاحًا ۚ وَلَهُنَّ مِثْلُ الَّذِي عَلَيْهِنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ ۚ وَلِلرِّجَالِ عَلَيْهِنَّ دَرَجَةٌ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-l-muṭallaqātu yatarabbaṣna bi-anfusihinna thalāthata qurūʾin wa-lā yaḥillu lahunna an yaktumna mā khalaqa llāhu fī arḥāmihinna in kunna yuʾminna bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-buʿūlatuhunna aḥaqqu bi-raddihinna fī dhālika in arādū iṣlāḥan wa-lahunna mithlu lladhī ʿalayhinna bi-l-maʿrūfi wa-li-r-rijāli ʿalayhinna darajatun wa-llāhu ʿazīzun ḥakīm
versets.trad: Divorced women shall wait, concerning themselves, until three menstrual periods have elapsed before they may dispose of themselves. It is not lawful for them to conceal what God has created in their wombs, if they believe in God and the Last Day. In that case, their husbands have the better right to take them back, if they desire reconciliation. Women have rights equivalent to their obligations, according to what is fitting, but men have a degree of precedence over them. God is mighty and wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the respite as an honor rendered to the past: the divorced are commanded to wait out of respect for the companionship of the husbands: if the bond has been broken between you, hold to the fidelity owed to what was; do not set another so quickly in his place, but wait the appointed time — do you not see that the woman whose marriage was not consummated owes no waiting period, for no true companionship was established? And on the men's "degree": it lies in excellence; but they have their share in the weakness and frailty of human nature — each honored according to his order.

mots.def: the waiting period, an honor rendered to the past companionship.
precedence in excellence; to women the share of delicacy.

versets.ar: الطَّلَاقُ مَرَّتَانِ ۖ فَإِمْسَاكٌ بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ تَسْرِيحٌ بِإِحْسَانٍ ۗ وَلَا يَحِلُّ لَكُمْ أَن تَأْخُذُوا مِمَّا آتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ شَيْئًا إِلَّا أَن يَخَافَا أَلَّا يُقِيمَا حُدُودَ اللَّهِ ۖ فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا يُقِيمَا حُدُودَ اللَّهِ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِمَا فِيمَا افْتَدَتْ بِهِ ۗ تِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللَّهِ فَلَا تَعْتَدُوهَا ۚ وَمَن يَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَ اللَّهِ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: aṭ-ṭalāqu marratāni fa-imsākun bi-maʿrūfin aw tasrīḥun bi-iḥsānin wa-lā yaḥillu lakum an taʾkhudhū mimmā ātaytumūhunna shayʾan illā an yakhāfā allā yuqīmā ḥudūda llāhi fa-in khiftum allā yuqīmā ḥudūda llāhi fa-lā junāḥa ʿalayhimā fīmā ftadat bihi tilka ḥudūdu llāhi fa-lā taʿtadūhā wa-man yataʿadda ḥudūda llāhi fa-ulāʾika humu ẓ-ẓālimūn
versets.trad: Divorce may be pronounced twice. After that, you may either keep your wife with kindness or release her with goodness. It is not lawful for you to take back anything of what you have given her, unless both fear that they cannot observe the limits set by God. If you fear they cannot observe the limits set by God, no fault attaches to either of them in what she gives back in exchange for her freedom. These are the limits set by God; do not transgress them. Those who transgress the limits set by God are the wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it was recommended to space out the divorce so that one would not rush toward the completion of the rupture. And on "keep with kindness or release with goodness": either fair companionship or fair separation; but discord, the ruin of the savor of life through blameworthy traits, is neither approved in the Path nor praised in the Law. As for taking back the gift: "to take back one's gift is to return to one's own vomit" — to reclaim what one has stripped oneself of is a baseness.

mots.racine: م س ك / ع ر ف
mots.def: fair companionship, one of the two honorable outcomes.

mots.racine: س ر ح / ح س ن
mots.def: fair separation, without blameworthy discord.

versets.ar: فَإِن طَلَّقَهَا فَلَا تَحِلُّ لَهُ مِن بَعْدُ حَتَّىٰ تَنكِحَ زَوْجًا غَيْرَهُ ۗ فَإِن طَلَّقَهَا فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِمَا أَن يَتَرَاجَعَا إِن ظَنَّا أَن يُقِيمَا حُدُودَ اللَّهِ ۗ وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللَّهِ يُبَيِّنُهَا لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-in ṭallaqahā fa-lā taḥillu lahu min baʿdu ḥattā tankiḥa zawjan ghayrahu fa-in ṭallaqahā fa-lā junāḥa ʿalayhimā an yatarājaʿā in ẓannā an yuqīmā ḥudūda llāhi wa-tilka ḥudūdu llāhi yubayyinuhā li-qawmin yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: If a man divorces his wife [a third time], she is no longer lawful to him until she has married another husband. But if the latter divorces her, no fault attaches to the former spouses if they return to one another, with the intention of observing the limits set by God. These are the limits God has instituted; He makes them clear for a people who understand.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it weighs upon the man that his wife should marry another; so he was dissuaded from choosing separation by the very extremity of separation — by being shown that she becomes lawful to him again, if he has left her, only at the cost of the most painful thing: a second marriage. Let him beware, then, of divorcing while he can. And he sees in it a sign of love: the grip of love upon the heart makes light the bearing of every trial; if after the separation the two fold back upon their regret for the lost union and repent to the utmost, no fault attaches to their returning to one another.

mots.racine: ن ك ح / ز و ج
mots.def: the condition making the return lawful, a curb against hasty rupture.

mots.sens: that they return to one another
mots.def: the return of the spouses moved by regret and love.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّسَاءَ فَبَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ سَرِّحُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ ۚ وَلَا تُمْسِكُوهُنَّ ضِرَارًا لِّتَعْتَدُوا ۚ وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ فَقَدْ ظَلَمَ نَفْسَهُ ۚ وَلَا تَتَّخِذُوا آيَاتِ اللَّهِ هُزُوًا ۚ وَاذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَمَا أَنزَلَ عَلَيْكُم مِّنَ الْكِتَابِ وَالْحِكْمَةِ يَعِظُكُم بِهِ ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-idhā ṭallaqtumu n-nisāʾa fa-balaghna ajalahunna fa-amsikūhunna bi-maʿrūfin aw sarriḥūhunna bi-maʿrūfin wa-lā tumsikūhunna ḍirāran li-taʿtadū wa-man yafʿal dhālika fa-qad ẓalama nafsahu wa-lā tattakhidhū āyāti llāhi huzuwan wa-dhkurū niʿmata llāhi ʿalaykum wa-mā anzala ʿalaykum mina l-kitābi wa-l-ḥikmati yaʿiẓukum bihi wa-ttaqū llāha wa-ʿlamū anna llāha bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīm
versets.trad: When you have divorced your wives and they have reached their appointed term, keep them with kindness or release them decently. Do not retain them by constraint: that would be a transgression. Whoever does so wrongs himself. Do not take the signs of God lightly. Remember God's favors toward you, and the Book and the Wisdom He has revealed to you to admonish you. Fear God! and know that, in truth, God has knowledge of all things.
themes: not to retain in order to harm
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the verse contains the command of fair companionship and the rejection of irritation against the wife, of obstinacy in a spirit of quarrel: either to free the way without harshness, or to hold to the right of companionship under the condition of fidelity. To retain in order to harm is, in truth, to harm oneself.

mots.def: to retain the wife with the intention of doing her wrong.
to take the signs of God lightly.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّسَاءَ فَبَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَلَا تَعْضُلُوهُنَّ أَن يَنكِحْنَ أَزْوَاجَهُنَّ إِذَا تَرَاضَوْا بَيْنَهُم بِالْمَعْرُوفِ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ يُوعَظُ بِهِ مَن كَانَ مِنكُمْ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ ۗ ذَٰلِكُمْ أَزْكَىٰ لَكُمْ وَأَطْهَرُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā ṭallaqtumu n-nisāʾa fa-balaghna ajalahunna fa-lā taʿḍulūhunna an yankiḥna azwājahunna idhā tarāḍaw baynahum bi-l-maʿrūfi dhālika yūʿaẓu bihi man kāna minkum yuʾminu bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri dhālikum azkā lakum wa-aṭharu wa-llāhu yaʿlamu wa-antum lā taʿlamūn
versets.trad: When you have divorced your wives and they have reached their appointed term, do not prevent them from taking new husbands, if they have agreed together in fitting manner. Thus is admonished whoever among you believes in God and the Last Day. This is more virtuous and more pure for you. God knows, and you, you do not know!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the verse forbids the guardians to harm them, commands the abandonment of the fury of ignorance (ḥamiyyat al-jāhiliyya) and submission to the judgment of God in the marrying of women if they so wish, without yielding to touchiness and clan pride. Indeed more: if she consents to a suitable match that asks for her, it is forbidden you to oppress her. And he rises to the spiritual sense: to melt down the traits of human nature by subduing the soul is the harshest of struggles, the most sincere of works for God.

mots.def: to place an obstacle to their remarriage out of clan pride.

mots.racine: ز ك و / ط ه ر
mots.def: the quality of the act conforming to the divine judgment.

versets.ar: وَالْوَالِدَاتُ يُرْضِعْنَ أَوْلَادَهُنَّ حَوْلَيْنِ كَامِلَيْنِ ۖ لِمَنْ أَرَادَ أَن يُتِمَّ الرَّضَاعَةَ ۚ وَعَلَى الْمَوْلُودِ لَهُ رِزْقُهُنَّ وَكِسْوَتُهُنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ ۚ لَا تُكَلَّفُ نَفْسٌ إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا ۚ لَا تُضَارَّ وَالِدَةٌ بِوَلَدِهَا وَلَا مَوْلُودٌ لَّهُ بِوَلَدِهِ ۚ وَعَلَى الْوَارِثِ مِثْلُ ذَٰلِكَ ۗ فَإِنْ أَرَادَا فِصَالًا عَن تَرَاضٍ مِّنْهُمَا وَتَشَاوُرٍ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِمَا ۗ وَإِنْ أَرَدتُّمْ أَن تَسْتَرْضِعُوا أَوْلَادَكُمْ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذَا سَلَّمْتُم مَّا آتَيْتُم بِالْمَعْرُوفِ ۗ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-l-wālidātu yurḍiʿna awlādahunna ḥawlayni kāmilayni li-man arāda an yutimma r-raḍāʿata wa-ʿalā l-mawlūdi lahu rizquhunna wa-kiswatuhunna bi-l-maʿrūfi lā tukallafu nafsun illā wusʿahā lā tuḍārra wālidatun bi-waladihā wa-lā mawlūdun lahu bi-waladihi wa-ʿalā l-wārithi mithlu dhālika fa-in arādā fiṣālan ʿan tarāḍin minhumā wa-tashāwurin fa-lā junāḥa ʿalayhimā wa-in aradtum an tastarḍiʿū awlādakum fa-lā junāḥa ʿalaykum idhā sallamtum mā ātaytum bi-l-maʿrūfi wa-ttaqū llāha wa-ʿlamū anna llāha bi-mā taʿmalūna baṣīr
versets.trad: Mothers shall suckle their children for two full years, for him who wishes the suckling to be complete. It falls to the father to provide for the mother's food and clothing, according to what is fitting. No soul is charged beyond what it can bear. The mother shall not be made to suffer on account of her child, nor the father on account of his child. The same obligations fall upon the heir. If, by mutual agreement and consultation, the parents wish to wean the child, no fault attaches to them. And if you wish to put your children out to a wet-nurse, no fault attaches to you, provided you pay what you have promised, according to custom. Fear God, and know that God sees perfectly all that you do!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates upon tenderness: the mercy of which one makes a proverb is the mercy of mothers. God has commanded them to perfect it by suckling the child two full years; and that the suckling should cease before that term is a sign that God's mercy toward the servant is more perfect still than that of mothers. On "no soul is charged beyond its capacity": to hoard up the possible is avarice; to halt before the impossible is excuse.

mots.def: the perfection of maternal mercy, an image of God's.
the measure: to withhold the possible is avarice, to halt at the impossible is excuse.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يُتَوَفَّوْنَ مِنكُمْ وَيَذَرُونَ أَزْوَاجًا يَتَرَبَّصْنَ بِأَنفُسِهِنَّ أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ وَعَشْرًا ۖ فَإِذَا بَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِيمَا فَعَلْنَ فِي أَنفُسِهِنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yutawaffawna minkum wa-yadharūna azwājan yatarabbaṣna bi-anfusihinna arbaʿata ashhurin wa-ʿashran fa-idhā balaghna ajalahunna fa-lā junāḥa ʿalaykum fīmā faʿalna fī anfusihinna bi-l-maʿrūfi wa-llāhu bi-mā taʿmalūna khabīr
versets.trad: Those of you who die leaving wives behind — these shall observe a waiting period of four months and ten days. Once they have reached their term, no fault attaches to you for the manner in which they dispose of themselves, in fitting wise. God is well informed of what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: because the right of the dead is greater — his separation not having been of his own choosing — the waiting period of fidelity owed is longer. In the early days of Islam, the period of widowhood was a full year; it was reduced to four months and ten days. And he adds, clear-eyed about the human heart: none prolongs his fidelity to the dead until the end of his life — as it has been said: "As faces wear away beneath the earth, so wears away the grief one bears for them."

mots.racine: ر ب ع / ش ه ر / ع ش ر
mots.def: the waiting period after the death, the fidelity owed to the deceased.
a divine Name: knowledge of the depth of acts.

versets.ar: وَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِيمَا عَرَّضْتُم بِهِ مِنْ خِطْبَةِ النِّسَاءِ أَوْ أَكْنَنتُمْ فِي أَنفُسِكُمْ ۚ عَلِمَ اللَّهُ أَنَّكُمْ سَتَذْكُرُونَهُنَّ وَلَٰكِن لَّا تُوَاعِدُوهُنَّ سِرًّا إِلَّا أَن تَقُولُوا قَوْلًا مَّعْرُوفًا ۚ وَلَا تَعْزِمُوا عُقْدَةَ النِّكَاحِ حَتَّىٰ يَبْلُغَ الْكِتَابُ أَجَلَهُ ۚ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِي أَنفُسِكُمْ فَاحْذَرُوهُ ۚ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ حَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lā junāḥa ʿalaykum fīmā ʿarraḍtum bihi min khiṭbati n-nisāʾi aw aknantum fī anfusikum ʿalima llāhu annakum satadhkurūnahunna wa-lākin lā tuwāʿidūhunna sirran illā an taqūlū qawlan maʿrūfan wa-lā taʿzimū ʿuqdata n-nikāḥi ḥattā yablugha l-kitābu ajalahu wa-ʿlamū anna llāha yaʿlamu mā fī anfusikum fa-ḥdharūhu wa-ʿlamū anna llāha ghafūrun ḥalīm
versets.trad: No fault attaches to you if you make an open proposal of marriage, or if you keep it secret. God knows that you will be thinking of such women, but make them no secret promise except in fitting words. Do not resolve to conclude the marriage before the prescribed term has expired. Know that God knows what is in your souls. So beware of Him, and know that God is forgiving and full of forbearance.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: that which draws affection and grounds the state of union was made lawful; and that which falls into forbidden things — touching upon a fault or promising a breach — was forbidden. On "do not resolve the contract before the term": that is, that the waiting period of the former husband first come to its end, for the honor of what is past is not lost. Delicacy toward the deceased and his time remains a demand of the heart.

mots.def: a veiled proposal, permitted during the waiting period.

mots.racine: ب ل غ / ك ت ب / أ ج ل
mots.sens: that the prescribed reach its term
mots.def: the end of the prescribed period before any contract.

versets.ar: لَّا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِن طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّسَاءَ مَا لَمْ تَمَسُّوهُنَّ أَوْ تَفْرِضُوا لَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً ۚ وَمَتِّعُوهُنَّ عَلَى الْمُوسِعِ قَدَرُهُ وَعَلَى الْمُقْتِرِ قَدَرُهُ مَتَاعًا بِالْمَعْرُوفِ ۖ حَقًّا عَلَى الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: lā junāḥa ʿalaykum in ṭallaqtumu n-nisāʾa mā lam tamassūhunna aw tafriḍū lahunna farīḍatan wa-mattiʿūhunna ʿalā l-mūsiʿi qadaruhu wa-ʿalā l-muqtiri qadaruhu matāʿan bi-l-maʿrūfi ḥaqqan ʿalā l-muḥsinīn
versets.trad: No fault shall be charged to you if you divorce women whom you have not touched, or for whom you have fixed no dower. Give them a fitting and decent gift, the man of means according to his means and the needy according to his means; this is a duty upon those who do good.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if a trial has knotted itself through the companionship of your equals, and then it has seemed good to you to be rid of it, no fault attaches to choosing separation if you so wish — for the only one whose separation is never permitted is the One; the companionship of creatures among themselves is not obligatory, its highest term is to be merely lawful. And he makes of the gift a balm: separation, whatever it be, is harsh; so the compensation was made a kind of substitute for it, at the hour when one drains the bitter cup of rupture.

mots.def: a consoling present that softens the rupture.

mots.racine: و س ع / ق ت ر
mots.sens: the man of means ... the needy
mots.def: each gives according to his means.

versets.ar: وَإِن طَلَّقْتُمُوهُنَّ مِن قَبْلِ أَن تَمَسُّوهُنَّ وَقَدْ فَرَضْتُمْ لَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً فَنِصْفُ مَا فَرَضْتُمْ إِلَّا أَن يَعْفُونَ أَوْ يَعْفُوَ الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ عُقْدَةُ النِّكَاحِ ۚ وَأَن تَعْفُوا أَقْرَبُ لِلتَّقْوَىٰ ۚ وَلَا تَنسَوُا الْفَضْلَ بَيْنَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-in ṭallaqtumūhunna min qabli an tamassūhunna wa-qad faraḍtum lahunna farīḍatan fa-niṣfu mā faraḍtum illā an yaʿfūna aw yaʿfuwa lladhī bi-yadihi ʿuqdatu n-nikāḥi wa-an taʿfū aqrabu li-t-taqwā wa-lā tansawu l-faḍla baynakum inna llāha bi-mā taʿmalūna baṣīr
versets.trad: If you divorce women before having touched them, while you have already fixed their dower, then give them half of what you fixed, unless they or the one who holds the marriage contract forgo this right. To forgo it is, in this case, nearer to piety. Do not forget to be generous with one another. God sees perfectly what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: pardon is more complete and more beautiful — whether on the part of the woman for the half that comes to her, or on the part of the husband for the half that reverts to him. And he praises the excess of grace: to forget generosity brings its author near to avarice; whereas the custom of the noble, when the occasions of generosity escape them, is to sharpen the gaze of giving so as to watch for the subtleties of grace and multiply their motives for acquiring its causes.

mots.sens: that they forgo ... that he forgo
mots.def: the pardon of one or the other, more complete.

mots.racine: ن س ي / ف ض ل
mots.def: to forget grace brings one near to avarice.

versets.ar: حَافِظُوا عَلَى الصَّلَوَاتِ وَالصَّلَاةِ الْوُسْطَىٰ وَقُومُوا لِلَّهِ قَانِتِينَ
versets.tr: ḥāfiẓū ʿalā ṣ-ṣalawāti wa-ṣ-ṣalāti l-wusṭā wa-qūmū li-llāhi qānitīn
versets.trad: Be assiduous in the prayers, and above all in the middle prayer. Stand before God in devotion.
versets.commentaire: Here Qushayrī gives a definition of true prayer: to guard the prayer is to enter it in awe (al-hayba), to leave it in magnification (at-taʿẓīm), and to abide in it through the permanence of contemplation under the seal of adab. As for the "middle prayer" left undetermined: it is so that you may observe them all, believing of each that it is the one — so that no neglect befall you in any.

mots.def: to enter in awe, leave in magnification, abide in contemplation.
to stand before God in humble adoration.

versets.ar: فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ فَرِجَالًا أَوْ رُكْبَانًا ۖ فَإِذَا أَمِنتُمْ فَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ كَمَا عَلَّمَكُم مَّا لَمْ تَكُونُوا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-in khiftum fa-rijālan aw rukbānan fa-idhā amintum fa-dhkurū llāha kamā ʿallamakum mā lam takūnū taʿlamūn
versets.trad: In case of danger, pray on foot or mounted on your steed. When you feel secure, invoke God in the manner He has taught you, which you did not know before.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ascribes to God this intimate word: do not fail My intimate converse at its hours, so far as you are able; for these enemies you dread, it is I who have set them against you. If you withdraw alone with Me by your hearts, their hands draw back from you and I grant you victory. Then, when the fear has dissipated and you are secure, return to your stability, devoting your times to retreat in My presence, in secret as in open day.

mots.racine: ر ج ل / ر ك ب
mots.def: ways of praying in danger without failing the remembrance.
the return to stability and to full recollection.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يُتَوَفَّوْنَ مِنكُمْ وَيَذَرُونَ أَزْوَاجًا وَصِيَّةً لِّأَزْوَاجِهِم مَّتَاعًا إِلَى الْحَوْلِ غَيْرَ إِخْرَاجٍ ۚ فَإِنْ خَرَجْنَ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي مَا فَعَلْنَ فِي أَنفُسِهِنَّ مِن مَّعْرُوفٍ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yutawaffawna minkum wa-yadharūna azwājan waṣiyyatan li-azwājihim matāʿan ilā l-ḥawli ghayra ikhrājin fa-in kharajna fa-lā junāḥa ʿalaykum fī mā faʿalna fī anfusihinna min maʿrūfin wa-llāhu ʿazīzun ḥakīm
versets.trad: Those of you who are about to die, leaving wives behind, shall make in their favor a bequest providing for their maintenance for one year, without their being obliged to leave their home. If they leave, no fault shall be charged to you for the fitting manner in which they dispose of themselves. God is mighty and wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recalls the historical sense: in the early days of Islam, the period of widowhood lasted a full year, after the custom and usage of the Arabs — as one of them said: "until the term of a year, then peace be upon you; whoever has mourned you a full year has paid his due." Then this was abrogated to four months and ten days. The bequest remains a grace that covers the widow with a roof and a loaf, without tearing her from her dwelling.

mots.def: a testamentary provision securing the widow's maintenance for a year.
without compelling her to leave the home.

versets.ar: وَلِلْمُطَلَّقَاتِ مَتَاعٌ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ ۖ حَقًّا عَلَى الْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-li-l-muṭallaqāti matāʿun bi-l-maʿrūfi ḥaqqan ʿalā l-muttaqīn
versets.trad: Divorced women are entitled to a decent provision: to assure it to them is a duty incumbent upon those who are mindful of God.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads here a subtle indication: do not add destitution to separation for them, lest their trial be doubled. The "decent" (al-maʿrūf) is no grudging minimum wrested from a reluctant hand, but the impulse of a courteous heart toward a soul one has left behind. Piety is measured by the delicacy one preserves toward those from whom one no longer expects anything.

mots.racine: م ت ع / ع ر ف
mots.def: matāʿ: maintenance, enjoyment; bi-l-maʿrūf: according to seemly custom.

mots.racine: ح ق ق / و ق ي
mots.sens: a duty for those who fear God
mots.def: ḥaqq: a due right, obligation; muttaqūn: the God-fearing.

versets.ar: كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ آيَاتِهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: kadhālika yubayyinu llāhu lakum āyātihi laʿallakum taʿqilūn
versets.trad: Thus does God make His signs clear to you, that you may understand.
versets.commentaire: God makes His proofs manifest, says Qushayrī, so that you may be schooled by what He shows you, and find success in what you grasp of the intimations of His wisdom. To "understand" here is no cold deduction, but the art of listening to the signs until one receives from them adab—the right bearing of the soul before its Lord.

mots.def: bayyana: to make plain, to manifest.
ʿaqala: to comprehend, to grasp by the intellect.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ خَرَجُوا مِن دِيَارِهِمْ وَهُمْ أُلُوفٌ حَذَرَ الْمَوْتِ فَقَالَ لَهُمُ اللَّهُ مُوتُوا ثُمَّ أَحْيَاهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى النَّاسِ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā lladhīna kharajū min diyārihim wa-hum ulūfun ḥadhara l-mawti fa-qāla lahumu llāhu mūtū thumma aḥyāhum inna llāha la-dhū faḍlin ʿalā n-nāsi wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yashkurūn
versets.trad: Have you not seen those who went forth from their dwellings, in their thousands, fearful of death? God said to them: "Die!", then He brought them back to life. God is the Dispenser of grace to mankind, yet most of them are not thankful.
versets.commentaire: Because they held it implausible that God could restore life, says Qushayrī, He showed it to them in their own selves, before their very eyes. Yet this demonstration profited them only by halves: he whose inner sight has not been honed in oneness draws nothing from prodigies, while he whose clear vision is firm has no need of such sights—for he has already been realized by what was announced to him, having believed in the Unseen.

mots.racine: ح ذ ر / م و ت
mots.def: ḥadhar: apprehension, anxious flight.

mots.racine: م و ت / ح ي ي
mots.def: the divine command that takes life away and gives it back.
faḍl: favor, gracious overflowing.

versets.ar: وَقَاتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-qātilū fī sabīli llāhi wa-ʿlamū anna llāha samīʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: Fight in the way of God, and know that God is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
themes: fighting in the way of God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī softens the verse's severity: if pain touches you and a groan rises from you, know that God hears your plaint, knows your states, and sees clearly into your affairs. The outward struggle is thus upheld by an intimacy: one who suffers upon the Path is never unheard, and to know this already lightens the trial.

mots.racine: ق ت ل / س ب ل
mots.sens: fight in the way of God
mots.def: sabīl Allāh: the path of God, His service.

mots.racine: س م ع / ع ل م
mots.def: two divine Names: the Hearing, the Omniscient.

versets.ar: مَّن ذَا الَّذِي يُقْرِضُ اللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا فَيُضَاعِفَهُ لَهُ أَضْعَافًا كَثِيرَةً ۚ وَاللَّهُ يَقْبِضُ وَيَبْسُطُ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: man dhā lladhī yuqriḍu llāha qarḍan ḥasanan fa-yuḍāʿifahu lahu aḍʿāfan kathīratan wa-llāhu yaqbiḍu wa-yabsuṭu wa-ilayhi turjaʿūn
versets.trad: Who is it that will make to God a goodly loan, which He will repay him manifold, multiplied many times over? God straitens and God enlarges [His favor], and to Him you shall be returned.
versets.commentaire: Why call almsgiving a "loan"? asks Qushayrī. It is God's tenderness toward those He loves: He spoke to you of the gift under the name of a loan, to spare the hearts of His friends. And he glosses the "goodly loan": the one given not in distraction but in full presence; the one made without watching for its reward. As for contraction (qabḍ) and expansion (basṭ), these have become the two states of the wayfarer: He straitens you away from yourself, then He dilates you through Himself.

mots.racine: ق ر ض / ح س ن
mots.def: qarḍ: loan; the root evokes the cutting off of a portion of one's wealth.

mots.racine: ق ب ض / ب س ط
mots.def: qabḍ and basṭ: contraction and expansion, cardinal terms of the mystical lexicon.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الْمَلَإِ مِن بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ مِن بَعْدِ مُوسَىٰ إِذْ قَالُوا لِنَبِيٍّ لَّهُمُ ابْعَثْ لَنَا مَلِكًا نُّقَاتِلْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۖ قَالَ هَلْ عَسَيْتُمْ إِن كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْقِتَالُ أَلَّا تُقَاتِلُوا ۖ قَالُوا وَمَا لَنَا أَلَّا نُقَاتِلَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَقَدْ أُخْرِجْنَا مِن دِيَارِنَا وَأَبْنَائِنَا ۖ فَلَمَّا كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقِتَالُ تَوَلَّوْا إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّنْهُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِالظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā l-malaʾi min banī isrāʾīla min baʿdi mūsā idh qālū li-nabiyyin lahumu bʿath lanā malikan nuqātil fī sabīli llāhi qāla hal ʿasaytum in kutiba ʿalaykumu l-qitālu allā tuqātilū qālū wa-mā lanā allā nuqātila fī sabīli llāhi wa-qad ukhrijnā min diyārinā wa-abnāʾinā fa-lammā kutiba ʿalayhimu l-qitālu tawallaw illā qalīlan minhum wa-llāhu ʿalīmun bi-ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: Have you not seen [what befell] the chiefs of the Children of Israel after Moses? They said to a prophet of theirs: "Raise up for us a king, and we will fight in the way of God." He said: "Might it be that, if fighting is prescribed for you, you would not fight?" They said: "How should we not fight in the way of God, when we have been driven from our homes and parted from our children?" Yet when fighting was prescribed for them, they turned away, all but a few of them. And God knows well the wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: They demanded battle of their own accord, observes Qushayrī, but when they were granted the very thing they had pledged themselves to, they fell back into sloth and made camp in the lands of dispute and heedlessness. Their fault lay in their motive: they fought for their goods and their dwellings.
