Had they said, "why should we not fight, since God has commanded it of us, since He is our Master"—perhaps they would have been aided to the very end of their purpose.

mots.racine: ب ع ث / م ل ك
mots.sens: raise up for us a king
mots.def: baʿatha: to raise up, to send; malik: king.

mots.racine: و ل ي / ق ل ل
mots.sens: they turned away, save a few
mots.def: tawallā: to turn one's back.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ لَهُمْ نَبِيُّهُمْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ بَعَثَ لَكُمْ طَالُوتَ مَلِكًا ۚ قَالُوا أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ عَلَيْنَا وَنَحْنُ أَحَقُّ بِالْمُلْكِ مِنْهُ وَلَمْ يُؤْتَ سَعَةً مِّنَ الْمَالِ ۚ قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ اصْطَفَاهُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَزَادَهُ بَسْطَةً فِي الْعِلْمِ وَالْجِسْمِ ۖ وَاللَّهُ يُؤْتِي مُلْكَهُ مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ وَاسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lahum nabiyyuhum inna llāha qad baʿatha lakum ṭālūta malikan qālū annā yakūnu lahu l-mulku ʿalaynā wa-naḥnu aḥaqqu bi-l-mulki minhu wa-lam yuʾta saʿatan mina l-māli qāla inna llāha ṣṭafāhu ʿalaykum wa-zādahu basṭatan fī l-ʿilmi wa-l-jismi wa-llāhu yuʾtī mulkahu man yashāʾu wa-llāhu wāsiʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: Their prophet said to them: "God has sent you Saul (Ṭālūt) as king." They said: "How can he reign over us, when we have a better right to kingship than he, and he has not been granted abundance of wealth?" He said: "God has chosen him over you and has endowed him with vast knowledge and great bodily strength." God grants His kingship to whom He wills; God is all-embracing, all-knowing.
versets.commentaire: They forgot the right of the divine choosing, says Qushayrī, and looked upon things with the eye of outward appearance: they judged Saul unworthy because he was poor. But excellence rests upon the election of the Real: though he had no goods, God filled him with knowledge. And Qushayrī notes finely that "strength of body" denotes less the stature than the nobility of the soul's qualities—for it is said, "so-and-so is but a name without a body," that is, a renown without reality.

mots.def: iṣṭafā: to elect, to single out the choicest.

mots.racine: ب س ط / ع ل م / ج س م
mots.sens: an amplitude in knowledge and body
mots.def: basṭa: extent, amplitude; here vast learning and great strength.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ لَهُمْ نَبِيُّهُمْ إِنَّ آيَةَ مُلْكِهِ أَن يَأْتِيَكُمُ التَّابُوتُ فِيهِ سَكِينَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَبَقِيَّةٌ مِّمَّا تَرَكَ آلُ مُوسَىٰ وَآلُ هَارُونَ تَحْمِلُهُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَةً لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lahum nabiyyuhum inna āyata mulkihi an yaʾtiyakumu t-tābūtu fīhi sakīnatun min rabbikum wa-baqiyyatun mimmā taraka ālu mūsā wa-ālu hārūna taḥmiluhu l-malāʾikatu inna fī dhālika la-āyatan lakum in kuntum muʾminīn
versets.trad: Their prophet said to them: "The sign of his kingship shall be that the Ark (tābūt) will come to you. In it is a presence (sakīna) from your Lord and relics left behind by the House of Moses and the House of Aaron, and it shall be borne by the angels. Truly in that is a sign for you, if you are believers."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from the Ark a striking comparison: the sakīna of Israel was laid in a chest that the enemies in turn buried, carried off, and gave back; but the sakīna of this community, God has laid within hearts—"It is He who sent down serenity into the hearts of the believers." And he ends: the heart of the believer is between two fingers of the All-Merciful, in the hand of the Real, under His pure sovereignty, secure from every grasp of creatures.

mots.def: tābūt: a sacred chest containing the relics.

mots.sens: a stilling presence from your Lord
mots.def: sakīna: serenity, peace descended from God.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا فَصَلَ طَالُوتُ بِالْجُنُودِ قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ مُبْتَلِيكُم بِنَهَرٍ فَمَن شَرِبَ مِنْهُ فَلَيْسَ مِنِّي وَمَن لَّمْ يَطْعَمْهُ فَإِنَّهُ مِنِّي إِلَّا مَنِ اغْتَرَفَ غُرْفَةً بِيَدِهِ ۚ فَشَرِبُوا مِنْهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّنْهُمْ ۚ فَلَمَّا جَاوَزَهُ هُوَ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ قَالُوا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا الْيَوْمَ بِجَالُوتَ وَجُنُودِهِ ۚ قَالَ الَّذِينَ يَظُنُّونَ أَنَّهُم مُّلَاقُو اللَّهِ كَم مِّن فِئَةٍ قَلِيلَةٍ غَلَبَتْ فِئَةً كَثِيرَةً بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ مَعَ الصَّابِرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā faṣala ṭālūtu bi-l-junūdi qāla inna llāha mubtalīkum bi-naharin fa-man shariba minhu fa-laysa minnī wa-man lam yaṭʿamhu fa-innahu minnī illā mani ghtarafa ghurfatan bi-yadihi fa-sharibū minhu illā qalīlan minhum fa-lammā jāwazahu huwa wa-lladhīna āmanū maʿahu qālū lā ṭāqata lanā l-yawma bi-jālūta wa-junūdihi qāla lladhīna yaẓunnūna annahum mulāqū llāhi kam min fiʾatin qalīlatin ghalabat fiʾatan kathīratan bi-idhni llāhi wa-llāhu maʿa ṣ-ṣābirīn
versets.trad: When Saul set out with the troops, he said: "God will surely test you by a river: whoever drinks of it is not of me, and whoever does not taste it is of me, save him who scoops up a single handful." Yet they drank of it, all but a few of them. When he and those who believed with him had crossed it, they said: "We have no power today against Goliath and his troops." But those who reckoned that they would meet God said: "How often has a small band overcome a numerous host, by the leave of God!" And God is with the steadfast.
themes: victory by the leave of God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the river a symbol of the unceasing trial: God has tried creatures through the company of creatures, through the lower world, and through the self; whoever takes from it only the strict handful needed is saved, whoever pours himself into it is swept away. And from "save a few" he draws a law of the Path: thus the elect, in every age, are few in number yet immense in rank. Before Goliath, some looked "with the eye of appearance" and human dread seized them; then God steadied their hearts by recalling His aid to His own.

mots.racine: ب ل و / ن ه ر
mots.def: ibtalā: to put to the test.
ightarafa: to take up in the hollow of the hand.

mots.racine: ف أ و / ق ل ل
mots.def: fiʾa: a group, a contingent.

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا بَرَزُوا لِجَالُوتَ وَجُنُودِهِ قَالُوا رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā barazū li-jālūta wa-junūdihi qālū rabbanā afrigh ʿalaynā ṣabran wa-thabbit aqdāmanā wa-nṣurnā ʿalā l-qawmi l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: When they came forth to face Goliath and his troops, they cried: "Our Lord! Pour out upon us patience, make our steps firm, and grant us victory over the disbelieving people!"
themes: fighting for God, not for self
versets.commentaire: The order of their prayer has a meaning, observes Qushayrī: they asked first for patience, then for steadfastness, then for victory—for patience is the right of the Real, and victory is their portion; they set God's right before their own share. And their intention was pure: they asked for victory not to avenge a loss they had suffered, but because the adversary was a disbeliever, an enemy of God. They stood through God and for God in everything: thus were they aided.

mots.racine: ف ر غ / ص ب ر
mots.def: afragha: to pour forth in abundance; ṣabr: endurance.

mots.racine: ث ب ت / ق د م
mots.def: thabbata: to make firm, stable.

versets.ar: فَهَزَمُوهُم بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ وَقَتَلَ دَاوُودُ جَالُوتَ وَآتَاهُ اللَّهُ الْمُلْكَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَعَلَّمَهُ مِمَّا يَشَاءُ ۗ وَلَوْلَا دَفْعُ اللَّهِ النَّاسَ بَعْضَهُم بِبَعْضٍ لَّفَسَدَتِ الْأَرْضُ وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ ذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-hazamūhum bi-idhni llāhi wa-qatala dāwūdu jālūta wa-ātāhu llāhu l-mulka wa-l-ḥikmata wa-ʿallamahu mimmā yashāʾu wa-lawlā dafʿu llāhi n-nāsa baʿḍahum bi-baʿḍin la-fasadati l-arḍu wa-lākinna llāha dhū faḍlin ʿalā l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: They put them to flight by the leave of God. David slew Goliath, and God gave him kingship and wisdom and taught him what He willed. Were it not that God repels some men by means of others, the earth would be corrupted; but God is the Dispenser of grace upon the worlds.
versets.commentaire: God, says Qushayrī, had made Saul feared for his stature, but He reserved the victory for the hand of David—small of frame, with no weapon but a sling. The triumph was his because the aid of God was with him: a lesson that power dwells not in the arm but in the divine election. And the balance of the world holds because God "occupies some by means of others," for "if all the strong were in accord, the weak would perish."

mots.racine: ه ز م / أ ذ ن
mots.sens: they routed them by the leave of God
mots.def: hazama: to defeat, to put to rout.

mots.sens: God's repelling of men (by one another)
mots.def: dafʿ: the act of holding back, of repelling.

versets.ar: تِلْكَ آيَاتُ اللَّهِ نَتْلُوهَا عَلَيْكَ بِالْحَقِّ ۚ وَإِنَّكَ لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ
versets.tr: tilka āyātu llāhi natlūhā ʿalayka bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-innaka la-mina l-mursalīn
versets.trad: These are the signs of God: We recite them to you in truth, for you are surely among the messengers.
versets.commentaire: These accounts of the past, says Qushayrī, addressing the Prophet, were neither within your knowledge nor within the reach of your ingenuity; you attained them only through a teaching come from God. The verse thus seals the sura of narratives: true knowledge is not conquered, it is given—pure communication of the Real to His messenger.

mots.def: āya: a sign, a verse.
mursal: one sent, a messenger.

versets.ar: تِلْكَ الرُّسُلُ فَضَّلْنَا بَعْضَهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ ۘ مِّنْهُم مَّن كَلَّمَ اللَّهُ ۖ وَرَفَعَ بَعْضَهُمْ دَرَجَاتٍ ۚ وَآتَيْنَا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ الْبَيِّنَاتِ وَأَيَّدْنَاهُ بِرُوحِ الْقُدُسِ ۗ وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ مَا اقْتَتَلَ الَّذِينَ مِن بَعْدِهِم مِّن بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَتْهُمُ الْبَيِّنَاتُ وَلَٰكِنِ اخْتَلَفُوا فَمِنْهُم مَّنْ آمَنَ وَمِنْهُم مَّن كَفَرَ ۚ وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ مَا اقْتَتَلُوا وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ يَفْعَلُ مَا يُرِيدُ
versets.tr: tilka r-rusulu faḍḍalnā baʿḍahum ʿalā baʿḍin minhum man kallama llāhu wa-rafaʿa baʿḍahum darajātin wa-ātaynā ʿīsā bna maryama l-bayyināti wa-ayyadnāhu bi-rūḥi l-qudusi wa-law shāʾa llāhu mā qtatala lladhīna min baʿdihim min baʿdi mā jāʾathumu l-bayyinātu wa-lākini khtalafū fa-minhum man āmana wa-minhum man kafara wa-law shāʾa llāhu mā qtatalū wa-lākinna llāha yafʿalu mā yurīd
versets.trad: Of these messengers, some We favored above others. Among them are those to whom God spoke, and some He raised by degrees. We gave Jesus, son of Mary, the clear proofs, and We strengthened him with the Holy Spirit. Had God so willed, those who came after them would not have fought one another after the clear proofs had reached them. But they fell into discord: some of them believed and some disbelieved. Had God so willed, they would not have fought one another; but God does what He intends.
versets.commentaire: The mission gathered them all, says Qushayrī, but they differ in the particulars of election: each has his own lights, and these lights have their dawning-places; one is higher in clarity, more abounding in elevation. And he sets aside any notion of earned wage: their excellences were no due won by their acts or their states; it is a decree of beauty that joined them, an outcome of grace that went before them. Men, for their part, are "governed by the eternal will": servitude is to draw tight the belt of service and to contemplate the portion already traced.

mots.def: faḍḍala: to prefer, to grant excellence.

mots.racine: أ ي د / ر و ح / ق د س
mots.sens: We strengthened him with the Holy Spirit
mots.def: ayyada: to fortify; rūḥ al-qudus: the Holy Spirit.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنفِقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقْنَاكُم مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِيَ يَوْمٌ لَّا بَيْعٌ فِيهِ وَلَا خُلَّةٌ وَلَا شَفَاعَةٌ ۗ وَالْكَافِرُونَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū anfiqū mimmā razaqnākum min qabli an yaʾtiya yawmun lā bayʿun fīhi wa-lā khullatun wa-lā shafāʿatun wa-l-kāfirūna humu ẓ-ẓālimūn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Spend in alms of that which We have provided you, before there comes a Day in which there is neither bargaining, nor friendship, nor intercession. And the disbelievers, they are the wrongdoers.
themes: alms before it is too late
versets.commentaire: Seize the occasion, Qushayrī exhorts: profit from the possibility still granted you, to advance the good before vigor fails and hope is spent. The gift of today prepares the only capital that will avail on the Day when neither commerce, nor friendship, nor intercession will have any hold.

mots.racine: ن ف ق / ر ز ق
mots.sens: spend of what We have provided you
mots.def: anfaqa: to spend in alms; razaqa: to provide.

mots.ar: لَّا بَيْعٌ فِيهِ وَلَا خُلَّةٌ وَلَا شَفَاعَةٌ
mots.tr: lā bayʿun fīhi wa-lā khullatun wa-lā shafāʿa
mots.racine: ب ي ع / خ ل ل / ش ف ع
mots.sens: neither bargaining, nor friendship, nor intercession
mots.def: khulla: intimate friendship; shafāʿa: intercession.

versets.ar: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ ۚ لَا تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ ۚ لَّهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۗ مَن ذَا الَّذِي يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَيْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِ إِلَّا بِمَا شَاءَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ ۖ وَلَا يَئُودُهُ حِفْظُهُمَا ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيمُ
versets.tr: allāhu lā ilāha illā huwa l-ḥayyu l-qayyūmu lā taʾkhudhuhu sinatun wa-lā nawmun lahu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi man dhā lladhī yashfaʿu ʿindahu illā bi-idhnihi yaʿlamu mā bayna aydīhim wa-mā khalfahum wa-lā yuḥīṭūna bi-shayʾin min ʿilmihi illā bi-mā shāʾa wasiʿa kursiyyuhu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa wa-lā yaʾūduhu ḥifẓuhumā wa-huwa l-ʿaliyyu l-ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: God! There is no god but He, the Living, the Self-Subsisting. Neither drowsiness nor sleep takes hold of Him. To Him belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth. Who is there that could intercede with Him save by His leave? He knows what lies before them and what lies behind them, while they grasp nothing of His knowledge save what He wills. His Throne extends over the heavens and the earth, and the keeping of them wearies Him not. He is the Sublime, the Magnificent.
versets.commentaire: Upon the Verse of the Throne (āyat al-kursī), Qushayrī rises to the highest. The Name "Allāh," he says, has no homonym: it is a name designating the possession of the attributes of majesty, not the derivation of words. And from "no god but He" he draws the station of fanāʾ: whoever is realized through this word no longer sees an atom of existence outside Him or through any other than Him; he hears only from God, contemplates only through God, is occupied only with God—effaced from all that is not God. Of "the Living, the Self-Subsisting" he says: He assumes the affairs of His servants and sustains every motion. And of "who could intercede save by His leave": whoever imagines that he draws near to Him by a merit, a work, a cause—conjecture is his homeland, ignorance his dwelling, and estrangement his end.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / ق و م
mots.def: al-Ḥayy: the Living; al-Qayyūm: He who subsists by Himself and makes all subsist.

mots.racine: س ن ه / ن و م
mots.sens: neither drowsiness nor sleep seizes Him
mots.def: sina: light slumber; nawm: sleep.

mots.racine: و س ع / ك ر س
mots.def: kursī: the Seat, symbol of all-encompassing sovereignty.

versets.ar: لَا إِكْرَاهَ فِي الدِّينِ ۖ قَد تَّبَيَّنَ الرُّشْدُ مِنَ الْغَيِّ ۚ فَمَن يَكْفُرْ بِالطَّاغُوتِ وَيُؤْمِن بِاللَّهِ فَقَدِ اسْتَمْسَكَ بِالْعُرْوَةِ الْوُثْقَىٰ لَا انفِصَامَ لَهَا ۗ وَاللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: lā ikrāha fī d-dīni qad tabayyana r-rushdu mina l-ghayyi fa-man yakfur bi-ṭ-ṭāghūti wa-yuʾmin bi-llāhi fa-qadi stamsaka bi-l-ʿurwati l-wuthqā lā nfiṣāma lahā wa-llāhu samīʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: There is no compulsion in religion! Right guidance has henceforth become distinct from error. Whoever rejects false deities (the ṭāghūt) and believes in God has grasped the firmest handhold, which has no fissure. And God is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
themes: rejecting what turns one from God
versets.commentaire: No compulsion, says Qushayrī, for the proofs are dazzling, the demonstrations manifest; guidance has parted from error as day from night. Then he interiorizes the "ṭāghūt": each person's ṭāghūt is whatever distracts him from his Lord; and faith in God is the life of the heart through God. As for the firmest handhold: to cling to it is to hold to the bounds of the command and the prohibition, to follow the way of the Chosen One—a hold "without fissure," by which the realized one is saved in both abodes.

mots.racine: ك ر ه / د ي ن
mots.def: ikrāh: coercion, compulsion.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / ط غ ي
mots.def: ṭāghūt: idol, tyrannical power; for Qushayrī, all that turns one from God.

mots.racine: ع ر و / و ث ق
mots.def: ʿurwa: handle, grip; wuthqā: the firmest.

versets.ar: اللَّهُ وَلِيُّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا يُخْرِجُهُم مِّنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّورِ ۖ وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَوْلِيَاؤُهُمُ الطَّاغُوتُ يُخْرِجُونَهُم مِّنَ النُّورِ إِلَى الظُّلُمَاتِ ۗ أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: allāhu waliyyu lladhīna āmanū yukhrijuhum mina ẓ-ẓulumāti ilā n-nūri wa-lladhīna kafarū awliyāʾuhumu ṭ-ṭāghūtu yukhrijūnahum mina n-nūri ilā ẓ-ẓulumāti ulāʾika aṣḥābu n-nāri hum fīhā khālidūn
versets.trad: God is the Protector of those who believe: He brings them out of the darknesses into the light. And those who disbelieve have the ṭāghūt for their patrons, who bring them out of the light into the darknesses. These are the companions of the Fire, wherein they shall abide forever.
versets.commentaire: God brings the believers out of the darknesses, says Qushayrī, by His eternal decree that preserved them from error and from innovations. Then he unfolds the verse into pearls: He brings them out of the darkness of their own contriving into the contemplation of His ordaining; out of the darkness in which they imagined they could reach Him by their movements and their rests; He lifts from them the shadow of their own selves and ushers them into the shadow of His solicitude; He delivers them from believing that salvation comes from themselves, by interposing Himself between them and reliance upon their works.

mots.sens: the Protector of those who believe
mots.def: walī: close friend, guarantor, protector.

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ن و ر
mots.sens: from the darknesses into the light
mots.def: ẓulumāt: darknesses (plural); nūr: light (singular).

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِي حَاجَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ فِي رَبِّهِ أَنْ آتَاهُ اللَّهُ الْمُلْكَ إِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ رَبِّيَ الَّذِي يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ قَالَ أَنَا أُحْيِي وَأُمِيتُ ۖ قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْتِي بِالشَّمْسِ مِنَ الْمَشْرِقِ فَأْتِ بِهَا مِنَ الْمَغْرِبِ فَبُهِتَ الَّذِي كَفَرَ ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā lladhī ḥājja ibrāhīma fī rabbihi an ātāhu llāhu l-mulka idh qāla ibrāhīmu rabbiya lladhī yuḥyī wa-yumītu qāla anā uḥyī wa-umītu qāla ibrāhīmu fa-inna llāha yaʾtī bi-sh-shamsi mina l-mashriqi fa-ʾti bihā mina l-maghribi fa-buhita lladhī kafara wa-llāhu lā yahdī l-qawma ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: Have you not considered the case of him to whom God had given kingship, and who disputed with Abraham about his Lord? When Abraham said: "My Lord is He who gives life and gives death," he retorted: "I give life and I give death!" Abraham said: "God brings the sun from the East; bring it then from the West!" Then he who disbelieved was confounded. And God does not guide the wrongdoing people.
versets.commentaire: God, says Qushayrī, hastened for His enemy the punishment of separation before that of the burning—and this chastisement is the gravest in reality, for whoever can see it. And he praises Abraham's pedagogy: he passed from one sound argument to another clearer still, not from any defect in the first, but because of the narrowness of the disbeliever's understanding. A lesson in discernment: before one whose eyes are sealed, to waste time in barren debate is meaningless—save in strictly necessary measure.

mots.sens: he disputed with Abraham about his Lord
mots.def: ḥājja: to argue, to contest.

mots.racine: ب ه ت / ك ف ر
mots.def: buhita: to be struck dumb, left without reply.

versets.ar: أَوْ كَالَّذِي مَرَّ عَلَىٰ قَرْيَةٍ وَهِيَ خَاوِيَةٌ عَلَىٰ عُرُوشِهَا قَالَ أَنَّىٰ يُحْيِي هَٰذِهِ اللَّهُ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا ۖ فَأَمَاتَهُ اللَّهُ مِائَةَ عَامٍ ثُمَّ بَعَثَهُ ۖ قَالَ كَمْ لَبِثْتَ ۖ قَالَ لَبِثْتُ يَوْمًا أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ ۖ قَالَ بَل لَّبِثْتَ مِائَةَ عَامٍ فَانظُرْ إِلَىٰ طَعَامِكَ وَشَرَابِكَ لَمْ يَتَسَنَّهْ ۖ وَانظُرْ إِلَىٰ حِمَارِكَ وَلِنَجْعَلَكَ آيَةً لِّلنَّاسِ ۖ وَانظُرْ إِلَى الْعِظَامِ كَيْفَ نُنشِزُهَا ثُمَّ نَكْسُوهَا لَحْمًا ۚ فَلَمَّا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ قَالَ أَعْلَمُ أَنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: aw ka-lladhī marra ʿalā qaryatin wa-hiya khāwiyatun ʿalā ʿurūshihā qāla annā yuḥyī hādhihi llāhu baʿda mawtihā fa-amātahu llāhu miʾata ʿāmin thumma baʿathahu qāla kam labithta qāla labithtu yawman aw baʿḍa yawmin qāla bal labithta miʾata ʿāmin fa-nẓur ilā ṭaʿāmika wa-sharābika lam yatasannah wa-nẓur ilā ḥimārika wa-li-najʿalaka āyatan li-n-nāsi wa-nẓur ilā l-ʿiẓāmi kayfa nunshizuhā thumma naksūhā laḥman fa-lammā tabayyana lahu qāla aʿlamu anna llāha ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: Or the case of him who passed by a town that had fallen down upon its roofs, deserted? He said: "How shall God bring this back to life after its death?" So God made him die for a hundred years, then raised him up and said: "How long have you tarried?" He answered: "I have tarried a day, or part of a day." God said: "Nay, you have tarried a hundred years. Look at your food and your drink: they have not spoiled with age; and look at your donkey—that We may make of you a sign for mankind; and look at the bones, how We set them up and then clothe them with flesh." When this was made plain to him, the man said: "I know that God has power over all things!"
themes: from certain knowledge to certain vision
versets.commentaire: This was no question of denial, Qushayrī insists, nor doubt of the power—for this account concerns the prophet ʿUzayr, and doubt is not permitted to prophets—but a question of wonder: by it he sought an increase of certainty. And God gave it to him in his very person. This, says Qushayrī, is the custom of the knowers: to seek from God the increase of certainty, passing from the knowledge of certainty (ʿilm al-yaqīn) to the eye of certainty (ʿayn al-yaqīn).

mots.racine: خ و ي / ع ر ش
mots.def: khāwiya: deserted, in ruins; ʿurūsh: roofs, rafters.

mots.sens: it has not spoiled (with the years)
mots.def: tasannaha: to grow stale with time.

mots.racine: ن ش ز / ك س و / ل ح م
mots.sens: We raise them up then clothe them with flesh
mots.def: anshaza: to raise, to set together; kasā: to clothe.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ رَبِّ أَرِنِي كَيْفَ تُحْيِ الْمَوْتَىٰ ۖ قَالَ أَوَلَمْ تُؤْمِن ۖ قَالَ بَلَىٰ وَلَٰكِن لِّيَطْمَئِنَّ قَلْبِي ۖ قَالَ فَخُذْ أَرْبَعَةً مِّنَ الطَّيْرِ فَصُرْهُنَّ إِلَيْكَ ثُمَّ اجْعَلْ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ جَبَلٍ مِّنْهُنَّ جُزْءًا ثُمَّ ادْعُهُنَّ يَأْتِينَكَ سَعْيًا ۚ وَاعْلَمْ أَنَّ اللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-idh qāla ibrāhīmu rabbi arinī kayfa tuḥyi l-mawtā qāla a-wa-lam tuʾmin qāla balā wa-lākin li-yaṭmaʾinna qalbī qāla fa-khudh arbaʿatan mina ṭ-ṭayri fa-ṣurhunna ilayka thumma jʿal ʿalā kulli jabalin minhunna juzʾan thumma dʿuhunna yaʾtīnaka saʿyan wa-ʿlam anna llāha ʿazīzun ḥakīm
versets.trad: And when Abraham said: "My Lord! Show me how You give life to the dead." He said: "Do you not believe?" He answered: "Yes, indeed, but that my heart may be set at rest!" He said: "Then take four birds; draw them to yourself so that they come back to you; then slay them and place a part of each upon one of the surrounding mountains; then call them: they will hasten to you. And know that God is Mighty, Wise."
themes: the lover seeks the word of the Beloved
the life of the heart through the slaughter of the soul
versets.commentaire: Abraham, says Qushayrī, wished to join the truth of certainty (ḥaqq al-yaqīn) to the vision of certainty he already possessed. And he attributes to the friend a subtler intention still: he drew forth the word of his Beloved—"do you not believe?"—for in that very word, "do you not believe?", there was a stilling for my heart; and the lover never ceases to seek, by every means, the address of the One he loves. Then, in the command of the four slaughtered birds, he reads the allegory of the way: the life of the heart comes only through the immolation of these things—that is, of the soul: he who does not slay his soul through spiritual combat does not bring his heart to life through God.

mots.racine: ط م ن / ق ل ب
mots.sens: that my heart may be set at rest
mots.def: iṭmaʾanna: to grow tranquil, to find repose.
ṣāra: to bend, to draw toward oneself.

versets.ar: مَّثَلُ الَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَالَهُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ كَمَثَلِ حَبَّةٍ أَنبَتَتْ سَبْعَ سَنَابِلَ فِي كُلِّ سُنبُلَةٍ مِّائَةُ حَبَّةٍ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يُضَاعِفُ لِمَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ وَاسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: mathalu lladhīna yunfiqūna amwālahum fī sabīli llāhi ka-mathali ḥabbatin anbatat sabʿa sanābila fī kulli sunbulatin miʾatu ḥabbatin wa-llāhu yuḍāʿifu li-man yashāʾu wa-llāhu wāsiʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: Those who spend their wealth in the way of God are like a grain that brings forth seven ears, in each ear a hundred grains. God multiplies [His gifts] for whom He wills. God is all-embracing, all-knowing.
themes: the spending of goods, the spending of souls
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two gifts. For one who spends his wealth, the reward is the Garden; but for one who spends his soul in the way of God, it is the Real Himself who stands in its stead. And he contrasts them: the spending of goods is done through almsgiving; the spending of states is done through unwavering fidelity to sincerity, through the rejection of every share and every portion for oneself. Thus the worshipper's grain multiplies seventyfold and beyond, but the gift of the "realized" one escapes all reckoning.

mots.racine: ح ب ب / ن ب ت / س ن ب ل
mots.sens: a grain that brought forth seven ears
mots.def: ḥabba: grain; sunbula: ear of corn.

mots.sens: He multiplies for whom He wills
mots.def: ḍāʿafa: to double, to multiply.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَالَهُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ لَا يُتْبِعُونَ مَا أَنفَقُوا مَنًّا وَلَا أَذًى ۙ لَّهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna yunfiqūna amwālahum fī sabīli llāhi thumma lā yutbiʿūna mā anfaqū mannan wa-lā adhan lahum ajruhum ʿinda rabbihim wa-lā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūn
versets.trad: Those who spend their wealth in the way of God and do not follow their bounty with reproach or injury, theirs is their reward with their Lord. No fear shall be upon them, nor shall they grieve.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī pierces to the root of "reproach" (mann): mann is to contemplate what you do; injury (adhan) is to recall your favor to the one you have obliged. The perfect gift effaces itself: they spend what they spend, then never contemplate their acts or their works; they do not pride themselves on their deed, but see the grace as being from God, who granted it to them.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / م ن ن
mots.sens: they do not follow [the gift] with reproach
mots.def: mann: reproach, the recalling of one's favor.
adhan: harm, vexation.

versets.ar: قَوْلٌ مَّعْرُوفٌ وَمَغْفِرَةٌ خَيْرٌ مِّن صَدَقَةٍ يَتْبَعُهَا أَذًى ۗ وَاللَّهُ غَنِيٌّ حَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: qawlun maʿrūfun wa-maghfiratun khayrun min ṣadaqatin yatbaʿuhā adhan wa-llāhu ghaniyyun ḥalīm
versets.trad: A kindly word and forgiveness are better than an alms followed by injury. And God is Self-Sufficient, Forbearing.
themes: the confession of one's poverty before God
versets.commentaire: A gentle word addressed to the poor, says Qushayrī, which turns the beggar away with an apology, is better and more perfect than the alms of one who delights in his own deed and attaches to it the weight of a reproach. Then he interiorizes: your confession before God of your incapacity and your fault, and the forgiveness God grants you upon that word—this is better than an alms mingled with reproach and accompanied by injury.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ع ر ف
mots.def: qawl maʿrūf: seemly, benevolent speech.

mots.racine: غ ن ي / ح ل م
mots.def: ghaniyy: He who is self-sufficient; ḥalīm: long-suffering.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تُبْطِلُوا صَدَقَاتِكُم بِالْمَنِّ وَالْأَذَىٰ كَالَّذِي يُنفِقُ مَالَهُ رِئَاءَ النَّاسِ وَلَا يُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ ۖ فَمَثَلُهُ كَمَثَلِ صَفْوَانٍ عَلَيْهِ تُرَابٌ فَأَصَابَهُ وَابِلٌ فَتَرَكَهُ صَلْدًا ۖ لَّا يَقْدِرُونَ عَلَىٰ شَيْءٍ مِّمَّا كَسَبُوا ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tubṭilū ṣadaqātikum bi-l-manni wa-l-adhā ka-lladhī yunfiqu mālahu riʾāʾa n-nāsi wa-lā yuʾminu bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri fa-mathaluhu ka-mathali ṣafwānin ʿalayhi turābun fa-aṣābahu wābilun fa-tarakahu ṣaldan lā yaqdirūna ʿalā shayʾin mimmā kasabū wa-llāhu lā yahdī l-qawma l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not render your alms void by reproach and injury, like one who spends his wealth to be seen by men and believes neither in God nor in the Last Day. He is like a smooth rock covered with fine soil, struck by a heavy downpour that leaves it bare. Such people gain nothing from what they have earned. And God does not guide the disbelieving people.
versets.commentaire: Bearable alone, says Qushayrī, is the recalling of a favor when it comes from God; but from a creature, no one has any favor to claim over another. For to bear the favors of men is the heaviest of trials, while to contemplate the favor of God is the greatest grace. And he warns against the pride of works: the most destitute of men is he who thinks himself rich and then discovers his bankruptcy; thus the most contemptible is he who imagines himself to be something, and to whom God reveals what he never suspected.

mots.racine: ب ط ل / ص د ق
mots.sens: do not render your alms void
mots.def: abṭala: to annul, to make void.
riyāʾ: the seeking of others' regard, hypocrisy.

mots.racine: ص ف و / ص ل د
mots.sens: a smooth rock... left bare and hard
mots.def: ṣafwān: smooth rock; ṣald: bare, stripped.

versets.ar: وَمَثَلُ الَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَالَهُمُ ابْتِغَاءَ مَرْضَاتِ اللَّهِ وَتَثْبِيتًا مِّنْ أَنفُسِهِمْ كَمَثَلِ جَنَّةٍ بِرَبْوَةٍ أَصَابَهَا وَابِلٌ فَآتَتْ أُكُلَهَا ضِعْفَيْنِ فَإِن لَّمْ يُصِبْهَا وَابِلٌ فَطَلٌّ ۗ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-mathalu lladhīna yunfiqūna amwālahumu btighāʾa marḍāti llāhi wa-tathbītan min anfusihim ka-mathali jannatin bi-rabwatin aṣābahā wābilun fa-ātat ukulahā ḍiʿfayni fa-in lam yuṣibhā wābilun fa-ṭallun wa-llāhu bi-mā taʿmalūna baṣīr
versets.trad: Those who spend their wealth seeking the good pleasure of God and to make their souls firm are like a garden upon a hill: should a heavy downpour reach it, its yield is doubled; and if no downpour reaches it, then a light dew suffices. And God sees clearly all that you do.
themes: spending for the good pleasure of God
versets.commentaire: These verses, says Qushayrī, set up in parables the contrast of two destinies: these see their effort accepted and their works bear fruit, their states rise up before God, and union as their end; those see their works annulled, their states ruined, and their hopes sealed in evil. The sincere one is like a seed whose root flourishes and whose branch rises; the dissembler, like a merchant whose stake is lost and whose cargo is stolen. "Are they equal? Do they even resemble one another?"

mots.racine: ب غ ي / ر ض و
mots.sens: seeking the good pleasure of God
mots.def: ibtighāʾ: seeking; marḍāt: satisfaction, good pleasure.

mots.racine: ث ب ت / ن ف س
mots.def: tathbīt: strengthening, anchoring.

versets.ar: أَيَوَدُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَن تَكُونَ لَهُ جَنَّةٌ مِّن نَّخِيلٍ وَأَعْنَابٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ لَهُ فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ الثَّمَرَاتِ وَأَصَابَهُ الْكِبَرُ وَلَهُ ذُرِّيَّةٌ ضُعَفَاءُ فَأَصَابَهَا إِعْصَارٌ فِيهِ نَارٌ فَاحْتَرَقَتْ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ لَكُمُ الْآيَاتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَفَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: a-yawaddu aḥadukum an takūna lahu jannatun min nakhīlin wa-aʿnābin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru lahu fīhā min kulli th-thamarāti wa-aṣābahu l-kibaru wa-lahu dhurriyyatun ḍuʿafāʾu fa-aṣābahā iʿṣārun fīhi nārun fa-ḥtaraqat kadhālika yubayyinu llāhu lakumu l-āyāti laʿallakum tatafakkarūn
versets.trad: Would any of you wish to possess a garden of date palms and vines, beneath which rivers flow, where he has all manner of fruits—then old age strikes him while his offspring are still feeble, and a fiery whirlwind falls upon his garden and burns it up? Thus does God make the signs clear to you, that you may reflect.
versets.commentaire: Pursuing the contrast, Qushayrī paints the fate of self-interested works: like one whose cargo is ruined, his trade plundered, his contrivance lost in the late hour, and whose trial repeats from every side and at every time. The promised garden burned at the very moment when strength declines and offspring remain weak: an image of one who staked everything on appearance and sees his labor swept away when he no longer has time to begin again. Hence the call to reflect before evening falls.

mots.racine: ع ص ر / ن و ر
mots.def: iʿṣār: whirlwind, tornado.
tafakkara: to ponder deeply.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنفِقُوا مِن طَيِّبَاتِ مَا كَسَبْتُمْ وَمِمَّا أَخْرَجْنَا لَكُم مِّنَ الْأَرْضِ ۖ وَلَا تَيَمَّمُوا الْخَبِيثَ مِنْهُ تُنفِقُونَ وَلَسْتُم بِآخِذِيهِ إِلَّا أَن تُغْمِضُوا فِيهِ ۚ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ غَنِيٌّ حَمِيدٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū anfiqū min ṭayyibāti mā kasabtum wa-mimmā akhrajnā lakum mina l-arḍi wa-lā tayammamū l-khabītha minhu tunfiqūna wa-lastum bi-ākhidhīhi illā an tughmiḍū fīhi wa-ʿlamū anna llāha ghaniyyun ḥamīd
versets.trad: O you who believe! Spend in alms of the good things you have earned and of what We have brought forth for you from the earth. Do not single out the worthless to give away, when you would not accept it yourselves save by shutting your eyes to it. And know that God is truly Self-Sufficient, Worthy of all praise.
versets.commentaire: Let each one, says Qushayrī, look upon what he spends for his own account and what he offers by his Lord's command: what falls to your share, keep it for yourself, but what is for your Lord, let it be the choicest of your wealth, the most precious and the most perfect. Then he marvels at the divine delicacy: see how He veils you, how He accepts from you, how He rewards you, how He praises your deed, how He ascribes it to you when all comes from Him by pure favor—He even calls it recompense, and He fills the world with your praise.

mots.racine: ط ي ب / ك س ب
mots.sens: of the good things you have earned
mots.def: ṭayyibāt: the pure and excellent things.

mots.racine: ي م م / خ ب ث
mots.sens: do not single out the vile
mots.def: tayammama: to head toward, to single out; khabīth: bad, impure.

versets.ar: الشَّيْطَانُ يَعِدُكُمُ الْفَقْرَ وَيَأْمُرُكُم بِالْفَحْشَاءِ ۖ وَاللَّهُ يَعِدُكُم مَّغْفِرَةً مِّنْهُ وَفَضْلًا ۗ وَاللَّهُ وَاسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: ash-shayṭānu yaʿidukumu l-faqra wa-yaʾmurukum bi-l-faḥshāʾi wa-llāhu yaʿidukum maghfiratan minhu wa-faḍlan wa-llāhu wāsiʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: Satan threatens you with poverty and commands you to indecency; but God promises you forgiveness from Himself and grace. And God is all-embracing, all-knowing.
themes: the dilation of the heart through trust
versets.commentaire: All lies in the contrast of the two promises, says Qushayrī: Satan promises poverty out of his own destitution, and God promises forgiveness out of His generosity. The Devil frightens you by bidding you hoard, by binding your heart to what you do not need, by veiling from your sight His all-sufficiency. But the "promised grace" is twofold: in the present, contentment and victory over the soul; and higher still, the opening of the door of gnosis, the spreading of the carpet of nearness, the welcome of the unveilings of intimacy.

mots.racine: و ع د / ف ق ر
mots.def: waʿada: to promise; faqr: destitution.
faḥshāʾ: infamy; for Qushayrī, the greed of the lower world.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ف ض ل
mots.def: maghfira: forgiveness; faḍl: favor.

versets.ar: يُؤْتِي الْحِكْمَةَ مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَمَن يُؤْتَ الْحِكْمَةَ فَقَدْ أُوتِيَ خَيْرًا كَثِيرًا ۗ وَمَا يَذَّكَّرُ إِلَّا أُولُو الْأَلْبَابِ
versets.tr: yuʾtī l-ḥikmata man yashāʾu wa-man yuʾta l-ḥikmata fa-qad ūtiya khayran kathīran wa-mā yadhdhakkaru illā ulū l-albāb
versets.trad: He gives wisdom to whom He wills. And whoever is given wisdom has been given a great good. Yet none takes heed save those endowed with understanding.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī multiplies the definitions of wisdom (ḥikma): that it be the inspiration of the Real that governs you, not the prompting of the soul; that you be ruled by the overpowering pulls of the Truth, not by the impulses of the devil. And again: wisdom is the rightness of things; it is that the whims of the human nature have no more hold upon you. And the highest: wisdom is the contemplation of the Real, and folly is the contemplation of what is other than Him.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ح ك م
mots.def: ḥikma: wisdom, right discernment.
lubb: the kernel of intelligence, the marrow.

versets.ar: وَمَا أَنفَقْتُم مِّن نَّفَقَةٍ أَوْ نَذَرْتُم مِّن نَّذْرٍ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُهُ ۗ وَمَا لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ أَنصَارٍ
versets.tr: wa-mā anfaqtum min nafaqatin aw nadhartum min nadhrin fa-inna llāha yaʿlamuhu wa-mā li-ẓ-ẓālimīna min anṣār
versets.trad: Whatever alms you spend, or whatever vow you make, God knows it. And for the wrongdoers there are no helpers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes three kinds of admonition, and so three ranks: some He warns by His chastisement, others He draws by His reward, and others still by His knowledge alone—and these are the elect. For nothing makes the servant fall from God's regard like betraying, in his heart, the covenant struck with Him: let the wayfarer guard himself, to the utmost, against making his soul stumble in this.

mots.racine: ن ف ق / ن ذ ر
mots.def: nafaqa: alms, expenditure; nadhr: vow.

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ن ص ر
mots.def: anṣār: helpers, defenders.

versets.ar: إِن تُبْدُوا الصَّدَقَاتِ فَنِعِمَّا هِيَ ۖ وَإِن تُخْفُوهَا وَتُؤْتُوهَا الْفُقَرَاءَ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۚ وَيُكَفِّرُ عَنكُم مِّن سَيِّئَاتِكُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: in tubdū ṣ-ṣadaqāti fa-niʿimmā hiya wa-in tukhfūhā wa-tuʾtūhā l-fuqarāʾa fa-huwa khayrun lakum wa-yukaffiru ʿankum min sayyiʾātikum wa-llāhu bi-mā taʿmalūna khabīr
versets.trad: If you give alms openly, it is well; but if you keep them secret and give them to the poor, that is better for you, and it blots out some of your evil deeds. And God is well aware of all that you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes the verse into the relation of love: if you display and proclaim your companionship with Us, you have done well and acted nobly; but if you guard our secret from the intrusion of intermediaries between Us and you, you have kept the conditions of tenderness and raised the pillar of union. The secrecy of the alms thus becomes the secrecy of the heart withdrawn from the gaze of third parties.

mots.racine: ب د و / ص د ق
mots.def: abdā: to show, to make public.
akhfā: to conceal, to hide.

versets.ar: لَّيْسَ عَلَيْكَ هُدَاهُمْ وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ يَهْدِي مَن يَشَاءُ ۗ وَمَا تُنفِقُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَلِأَنفُسِكُمْ ۚ وَمَا تُنفِقُونَ إِلَّا ابْتِغَاءَ وَجْهِ اللَّهِ ۚ وَمَا تُنفِقُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ يُوَفَّ إِلَيْكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تُظْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: laysa ʿalayka hudāhum wa-lākinna llāha yahdī man yashāʾu wa-mā tunfiqū min khayrin fa-li-anfusikum wa-mā tunfiqūna illā btighāʾa wajhi llāhi wa-mā tunfiqū min khayrin yuwaffa ilaykum wa-antum lā tuẓlamūn
versets.trad: Their guidance is not your charge, but God guides whom He wills. Whatever good you spend [in alms] is for yourselves, for you spend it only seeking the Face of God. And whatever good you spend will be repaid to you in full, and you shall not be wronged.
themes: spending for the Face of God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī places in the mouth of the Real a word addressed to the Prophet, and the whole honor of prophethood resounds in it: yours is the praised station, the bound standard, the eminent ranks, the lofty dwellings; you are the master of the first and the last. But their guidance is not your charge: direction belongs to Our prerogatives alone. O Muḥammad, you call them, but it is We who guide them.

mots.sens: their guidance is not your charge
mots.def: hudā: direction, guidance.

mots.racine: ب غ ي / و ج ه
mots.def: wajh Allāh: the Face of God, His pure good pleasure.

versets.ar: لِلْفُقَرَاءِ الَّذِينَ أُحْصِرُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ ضَرْبًا فِي الْأَرْضِ يَحْسَبُهُمُ الْجَاهِلُ أَغْنِيَاءَ مِنَ التَّعَفُّفِ تَعْرِفُهُم بِسِيمَاهُمْ لَا يَسْأَلُونَ النَّاسَ إِلْحَافًا ۗ وَمَا تُنفِقُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ بِهِ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: li-l-fuqarāʾi lladhīna uḥṣirū fī sabīli llāhi lā yastaṭīʿūna ḍarban fī l-arḍi yaḥsabuhumu l-jāhilu aghniyāʾa mina t-taʿaffufi taʿrifuhum bi-sīmāhum lā yasʾalūna n-nāsa ilḥāfan wa-mā tunfiqū min khayrin fa-inna llāha bihi ʿalīm
versets.trad: [Give] to the poor who are confined in the way of God and can no longer travel the land. The ignorant suppose them wealthy because of their reserve. You know them by their mark: they do not beg of people with importunity. And whatever good you spend, God knows it well.
themes: the mark seen by clear vision
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of these poor the lovers of God whom nothing distracts: the sovereignty of the Reality has closed every road to them—no east to go to, no west to encamp in; everywhere they see the pavilions of oneness pitched around them. The true poor one "stands with God through God," beyond the reach of alien eyes. And he glosses "you know them by their mark": this mark is not of those that appear to the eye, but a mark grasped by inner vision alone. As for "they do not beg with importunity," he reads in it a preservation of their secret, that none may look upon them with the eye of supplication.

mots.sens: confined in the way of God
mots.def: uḥṣira: to be held back, hindered.

mots.racine: ع ر ف / س و م
mots.sens: you know them by their mark
mots.def: sīmā: distinguishing sign, imprint.

mots.racine: س أ ل / ل ح ف
mots.sens: they do not beg of people with insistence
mots.def: ilḥāf: importunity, insistence.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَالَهُم بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ سِرًّا وَعَلَانِيَةً فَلَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna yunfiqūna amwālahum bi-l-layli wa-n-nahāri sirran wa-ʿalāniyatan fa-lahum ajruhum ʿinda rabbihim wa-lā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūn
versets.trad: Those who spend their wealth by night and by day, in secret and openly, shall have their reward with their Lord. No fear shall be upon them, nor shall they grieve.
themes: from the gift of goods to contemplation
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises generosity to perpetual contemplation: as long as they have any wealth, they do not weary for an hour of spending it, night and day; and when the wealth is exhausted, they do not cease for an instant to contemplate Him, night and day. The outward gift comes to an end, but the offering of the heart—the unbroken attention to God—is never interrupted.

mots.racine: س ر ر / ع ل ن
mots.def: sirr: secret; ʿalāniya: in the open.

mots.racine: أ ج ر / ر ب ب
mots.sens: theirs is their reward with their Lord
mots.def: ajr: reward.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبَا لَا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَقُومُ الَّذِي يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطَانُ مِنَ الْمَسِّ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا إِنَّمَا الْبَيْعُ مِثْلُ الرِّبَا ۗ وَأَحَلَّ اللَّهُ الْبَيْعَ وَحَرَّمَ الرِّبَا ۚ فَمَن جَاءَهُ مَوْعِظَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِ فَانتَهَىٰ فَلَهُ مَا سَلَفَ وَأَمْرُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ ۖ وَمَنْ عَادَ فَأُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna yaʾkulūna r-ribā lā yaqūmūna illā kamā yaqūmu lladhī yatakhabbaṭuhu sh-shayṭānu mina l-massi dhālika bi-annahum qālū innamā l-bayʿu mithlu r-ribā wa-aḥalla llāhu l-bayʿa wa-ḥarrama r-ribā fa-man jāʾahu mawʿiẓatun min rabbihi fa-ntahā fa-lahu mā salafa wa-amruhu ilā llāhi wa-man ʿāda fa-ulāʾika aṣḥābu n-nāri hum fīhā khālidūn
versets.trad: Those who devour usury shall not rise [on the Day of Judgment] save as one rises whom Satan has struck down by his touch. That is because they say: "Trade is just like usury." Yet God has permitted trade and has forbidden usury. Whoever, on receiving an admonition from his Lord, ceases [from usury] may keep what he has already gained, and his case rests with God. But those who return to it are the companions of the Fire, wherein they shall abide forever.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the verse in a spiritual key: whoever turns from the command and grants himself licenses according to what his daydream of interpretation whispers to him—there is no stability for him in the moment, no rising up at the end; he has lost his present without gaining his future. But whoever awakens to the warnings of admonition, bridles the mount of caprice, and does not loose the rein upon obstinacy—to him a reprieve is granted, so long as he does not fall back into the blamed state.

mots.racine: أ ك ل / ر ب و
mots.def: ribā: usurious interest, illicit increase.

mots.racine: خ ب ط / م س س
mots.def: takhabbaṭa: to make stagger; mass: touch, possession.

mots.racine: و ع ظ / ن ه ي
mots.sens: an admonition from his Lord, and he ceases
mots.def: mawʿiẓa: admonition; intahā: to desist.

versets.ar: يَمْحَقُ اللَّهُ الرِّبَا وَيُرْبِي الصَّدَقَاتِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ أَثِيمٍ
versets.tr: yamḥaqu llāhu r-ribā wa-yurbī ṣ-ṣadaqāti wa-llāhu lā yuḥibbu kulla kaffārin athīm
versets.trad: God blots out the gains of usury and makes alms grow. And He loves not any ungrateful sinner.
themes: the blessing of the permitted act
versets.commentaire: The spiritual law is clear to Qushayrī: what is done with the leave of God is joined to wealth and attended by blessings; what is done in obedience to caprice, upon that falls effacement, and its end is loss. Usury and alms are but the figures of two orientations of the heart: the one, turned toward the self, is consumed; the other, turned toward God, is multiplied.

mots.def: maḥaqa: to efface, to make the blessing vanish.

mots.racine: ر ب و / ص د ق
mots.def: arbā: to cause to increase, to make grow.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَأَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتَوُا الزَّكَاةَ لَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti wa-aqāmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātawu z-zakāta lahum ajruhum ʿinda rabbihim wa-lā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūn
versets.trad: Truly, those who believe and do righteous deeds, who establish the prayer and give the alms, shall have their reward with their Lord. No fear shall be upon them, nor shall they grieve.
themes: faith and works, prayer and alms
versets.commentaire: In a brief and warm word, Qushayrī sums up everything: those who were Ours—what they find from Us suffices them; We do not let the reward of one who has done well be lost. The believer's security rests not on his counted works but on the attachment of his very being to God.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح
mots.def: ṣāliḥāt: good and upright actions.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و / ز ك و
mots.sens: they established the prayer and gave the alms
mots.def: aqāma ṣ-ṣalāt: to establish the prayer; zakāt: the legal alms.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَذَرُوا مَا بَقِيَ مِنَ الرِّبَا إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū ttaqū llāha wa-dharū mā baqiya mina r-ribā in kuntum muʾminīn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Fear God, and renounce what remains of usury, if you are believers.
themes: the sufficiency of the divine promise
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from the command a maxim of trust: to be content with what the Lord has promised is better for the believer than to fasten his heart to what his soul covets. And he sets the two registers finely against each other: your desire belongs to the lures of the soul; what is promised to you belongs to what the Real has guaranteed. To renounce usury is to prefer the guarantee of God to the immediate gain.

mots.def: ittaqā: to guard oneself through piety.

mots.racine: و ذ ر / ب ق ي
mots.def: dhara: to abandon, to forsake.

versets.ar: فَإِن لَّمْ تَفْعَلُوا فَأْذَنُوا بِحَرْبٍ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ۖ وَإِن تُبْتُمْ فَلَكُمْ رُءُوسُ أَمْوَالِكُمْ لَا تَظْلِمُونَ وَلَا تُظْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-in lam tafʿalū fa-ʾdhanū bi-ḥarbin mina llāhi wa-rasūlihi wa-in tubtum fa-lakum ruʾūsu amwālikum lā taẓlimūna wa-lā tuẓlamūn
versets.trad: If you do not, then take notice of a war from God and His messenger. But if you repent, your capital is yours: you will neither wrong nor be wronged.
themes: the war of God against the obstinate
versets.commentaire: The warning is severe, and Qushayrī draws out its root: he who persists has with Us neither weight nor measure, neither rank nor regard. The "war of God" is first of all the forfeiture of all credit in the divine sight; repentance, for its part, restores to a man his rightful due—neither does he wrong, nor is he wronged.

mots.racine: أ ذ ن / ح ر ب
mots.def: adhina bi: to take note of; ḥarb: war.

mots.racine: ر أ س / م و ل
mots.def: raʾs al-māl: the principal, the capital.

versets.ar: وَإِن كَانَ ذُو عُسْرَةٍ فَنَظِرَةٌ إِلَىٰ مَيْسَرَةٍ ۚ وَأَن تَصَدَّقُوا خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-in kāna dhū ʿusratin fa-naẓiratun ilā maysaratin wa-an taṣaddaqū khayrun lakum in kuntum taʿlamūn
versets.trad: If your debtor is in straitened circumstances, then grant him respite until ease comes; but if you remit the debt as alms, that is better for you, if only you knew!
themes: the gracious remission of the debt
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises the creditor to the model of the merciful Lord: if the judge cannot keep the proven insolvent in custody, all the more does God not deal so with us—knowing our destitution, our incapacity, and the sincerity of our want before Him, He shows us mercy. For the utterly insolvent one, there remains no path but what his Master provides for him. To remit a debt is to imitate that clemency.

mots.def: ʿusra: hardship, poverty.

mots.racine: ن ظ ر / ي س ر
mots.def: naẓira: reprieve; maysara: ease.

versets.ar: وَاتَّقُوا يَوْمًا تُرْجَعُونَ فِيهِ إِلَى اللَّهِ ۖ ثُمَّ تُوَفَّىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا كَسَبَتْ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ttaqū yawman turjaʿūna fīhi ilā llāhi thumma tuwaffā kullu nafsin mā kasabat wa-hum lā yuẓlamūn
versets.trad: And fear a Day when you shall be returned to God; then every soul shall be repaid in full for what it has earned, and none shall be wronged.
themes: the return of hearts in every breath
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two returns: the return of bodies and souls, tomorrow, at death; and the return of the inmost secrets and the hearts, at every breath, by an immediate reckoning. And this latter is the more pressing: the present reckoning He exacts is more deserving of attention than the promise of the Resurrection. He notes too the nuance of address: to the common folk He says "fear a Day," but to the elect: "It is I whom you must fear."

mots.sens: therein you shall be returned to God
mots.def: rajaʿa: to return, to be brought back.

mots.racine: و ف ي / ن ف س / ك س ب
mots.sens: every soul is repaid in full for what it earned
mots.def: tuwaffā: to be rewarded in full.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا تَدَايَنتُم بِدَيْنٍ إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى فَاكْتُبُوهُ ۚ وَلْيَكْتُب بَّيْنَكُمْ كَاتِبٌ بِالْعَدْلِ ۚ وَلَا يَأْبَ كَاتِبٌ أَن يَكْتُبَ كَمَا عَلَّمَهُ اللَّهُ ۚ فَلْيَكْتُبْ وَلْيُمْلِلِ الَّذِي عَلَيْهِ الْحَقُّ وَلْيَتَّقِ اللَّهَ رَبَّهُ وَلَا يَبْخَسْ مِنْهُ شَيْئًا ۚ فَإِن كَانَ الَّذِي عَلَيْهِ الْحَقُّ سَفِيهًا أَوْ ضَعِيفًا أَوْ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُ أَن يُمِلَّ هُوَ فَلْيُمْلِلْ وَلِيُّهُ بِالْعَدْلِ ۚ وَاسْتَشْهِدُوا شَهِيدَيْنِ مِن رِّجَالِكُمْ ۖ فَإِن لَّمْ يَكُونَا رَجُلَيْنِ فَرَجُلٌ وَامْرَأَتَانِ مِمَّن تَرْضَوْنَ مِنَ الشُّهَدَاءِ أَن تَضِلَّ إِحْدَاهُمَا فَتُذَكِّرَ إِحْدَاهُمَا الْأُخْرَىٰ ۚ وَلَا يَأْبَ الشُّهَدَاءُ إِذَا مَا دُعُوا ۚ وَلَا تَسْأَمُوا أَن تَكْتُبُوهُ صَغِيرًا أَوْ كَبِيرًا إِلَىٰ أَجَلِهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ أَقْسَطُ عِندَ اللَّهِ وَأَقْوَمُ لِلشَّهَادَةِ وَأَدْنَىٰ أَلَّا تَرْتَابُوا ۖ إِلَّا أَن تَكُونَ تِجَارَةً حَاضِرَةً تُدِيرُونَهَا بَيْنَكُمْ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَلَّا تَكْتُبُوهَا ۗ وَأَشْهِدُوا إِذَا تَبَايَعْتُمْ ۚ وَلَا يُضَارَّ كَاتِبٌ وَلَا شَهِيدٌ ۚ وَإِن تَفْعَلُوا فَإِنَّهُ فُسُوقٌ بِكُمْ ۗ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ ۖ وَيُعَلِّمُكُمُ اللَّهُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū idhā tadāyantum bi-daynin ilā ajalin musamman fa-ktubūhu wa-l-yaktub baynakum kātibun bi-l-ʿadli wa-lā yaʾba kātibun an yaktuba kamā ʿallamahu llāhu fa-l-yaktub wa-l-yumlili lladhī ʿalayhi l-ḥaqqu wa-l-yattaqi llāha rabbahu wa-lā yabkhas minhu shayʾan fa-in kāna lladhī ʿalayhi l-ḥaqqu safīhan aw ḍaʿīfan aw lā yastaṭīʿu an yumilla huwa fa-l-yumlil waliyyuhu bi-l-ʿadli wa-stashhidū shahīdayni min rijālikum fa-in lam yakūnā rajulayni fa-rajulun wa-mraʾatāni mimman tarḍawna mina sh-shuhadāʾi an taḍilla iḥdāhumā fa-tudhakkira iḥdāhumā l-ukhrā wa-lā yaʾba sh-shuhadāʾu idhā mā duʿū wa-lā tasʾamū an taktubūhu ṣaghīran aw kabīran ilā ajalihi dhālikum aqsaṭu ʿinda llāhi wa-aqwamu li-sh-shahādati wa-adnā allā tartābū illā an takūna tijāratan ḥāḍiratan tudīrūnahā baynakum fa-laysa ʿalaykum junāḥun allā taktubūhā wa-ashhidū idhā tabāyaʿtum wa-lā yuḍārra kātibun wa-lā shahīdun wa-in tafʿalū fa-innahu fusūqun bikum wa-ttaqū llāha wa-yuʿallimukumu llāhu wa-llāhu bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīm
versets.trad: O you who believe! When you contract a debt for a fixed term, set it down in writing. Let a scribe write it down between you faithfully. Let no scribe refuse to write as God has taught him. Let him write, and let the debtor dictate, and let him fear God, his Lord, and not diminish anything of the debt. If the debtor is feeble-minded or weak, or unable to dictate himself, then let his guardian dictate in his stead, in all fairness. Call to witness two witnesses from among your men; and if there are not two men, then a man and two women, from among such as you approve as witnesses, so that if one of the two women should err, the other may remind her. Let the witnesses not refuse when they are called. And do not be averse to writing down the debt, whether small or great, together with its term. That is more just in the sight of God, more upright for testimony, and more apt to keep you free of doubt—unless it be a present transaction conducted on the spot between you, in which case there is no fault upon you if you do not write it down. Take witnesses whenever you trade. Let neither scribe nor witness be harmed. If you do, it is a transgression on your part. Fear God, and God will teach you. And God has knowledge of all things.
themes: setting the debt down in writing
versets.commentaire: In this longest verse of the Qurʾān, Qushayrī sees the tenderness of God ordering the lives of men: He has commanded creatures to hold to sincerity, has taught them how to deal with one another, and to take the precautions of writing and witnessing, that they may do one another no wrong—and this proceeds from His mercy, from His benevolence, that they may not fall into quarrel. And he adds: He who today institutes that which settles disputes is worthy of effacing tomorrow every trace of dispute among them.

mots.def: tadāyana: to extend mutual credit; dayn: debt.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ع د ل
mots.sens: set it down... a scribe with justice
mots.def: kātib: scribe; ʿadl: equity.
ʿallama: to instruct.

versets.ar: وَإِن كُنتُمْ عَلَىٰ سَفَرٍ وَلَمْ تَجِدُوا كَاتِبًا فَرِهَانٌ مَّقْبُوضَةٌ ۖ فَإِنْ أَمِنَ بَعْضُكُم بَعْضًا فَلْيُؤَدِّ الَّذِي اؤْتُمِنَ أَمَانَتَهُ وَلْيَتَّقِ اللَّهَ رَبَّهُ ۗ وَلَا تَكْتُمُوا الشَّهَادَةَ ۚ وَمَن يَكْتُمْهَا فَإِنَّهُ آثِمٌ قَلْبُهُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-in kuntum ʿalā safarin wa-lam tajidū kātiban fa-rihānun maqbūḍatun fa-in amina baʿḍukum baʿḍan fa-l-yuʾaddi lladhi ʾtumina amānatahu wa-l-yattaqi llāha rabbahu wa-lā taktumū sh-shahādata wa-man yaktumhā fa-innahu āthimun qalbuhu wa-llāhu bi-mā taʿmalūna ʿalīm
versets.trad: If you are on a journey and find no scribe, then let a pledge be taken in hand. But if one of you entrusts a deposit to another, let the one entrusted render up his trust, and let him fear God, his Lord. And do not conceal testimony: whoever conceals it, his heart is sinful. And God knows all that you do.
versets.commentaire: In the spirit of Qushayrī: the entrusted deposit calls for loyal restitution, and the "fear of God, his Lord," makes honesty not a calculation but an act of worship. The sin of concealed testimony is not of the tongue alone: it is the heart itself that takes it on—for true fidelity is that of the inner secret, and none conceals anything before Him who "knows all that you do."

mots.racine: ر ه ن / ق ب ض
mots.def: rihān: pledges; maqbūḍa: held in hand.

mots.racine: أ د ي / أ م ن
mots.sens: let him render up his trust
mots.def: addā: to render, to discharge; amāna: a trust given in confidence.

mots.racine: أ ث م / ق ل ب
mots.def: āthim: guilty; the sin reaches the very heart.

versets.ar: لِّلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۗ وَإِن تُبْدُوا مَا فِي أَنفُسِكُمْ أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ يُحَاسِبْكُم بِهِ اللَّهُ ۖ فَيَغْفِرُ لِمَن يَشَاءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَن يَشَاءُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: li-llāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-in tubdū mā fī anfusikum aw tukhfūhu yuḥāsibkum bihi llāhu fa-yaghfiru li-man yashāʾu wa-yuʿadhdhibu man yashāʾu wa-llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: To God belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth. Whether you make known what is in your souls or conceal it, God will call you to account for it. He forgives whom He wills and punishes whom He wills. And God has power over all things.
themes: the reckoning of the revealed and the hidden
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds in several readings the "what you make known or conceal": what you make known is worship; what you conceal is the inner will. What you conceal are the fleeting thoughts; what you make known, their expressions. And from this he draws the demand of vigilance: the allusion is to the permanence of watchfulness (murāqaba) and to the continual self-examination (muḥāsaba): let no thought distract you, let no breath of your time pass unobserved.

mots.racine: ب د و / خ ف ي
mots.sens: whether you make it known or conceal it
mots.def: abdā: to manifest; akhfā: to conceal.

mots.sens: God will call you to account for it
mots.def: ḥāsaba: to bring to a reckoning.

versets.ar: آمَنَ الرَّسُولُ بِمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِن رَّبِّهِ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ ۚ كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَمَلَائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّن رُّسُلِهِ ۚ وَقَالُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ۖ غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: āmana r-rasūlu bi-mā unzila ilayhi min rabbihi wa-l-muʾminūna kullun āmana bi-llāhi wa-malāʾikatihi wa-kutubihi wa-rusulihi lā nufarriqu bayna aḥadin min rusulihi wa-qālū samiʿnā wa-aṭaʿnā ghufrānaka rabbanā wa-ilayka l-maṣīr
versets.trad: The Messenger has believed in what was sent down to him from his Lord, and so have the believers. Each one believes in God, in His angels, in His Books, and in His messengers. We make no distinction between any of His messengers. And they have said: "We have heard and we have obeyed. Your forgiveness, our Lord! And to You is the final return!"
themes: faith by unveiling, not by proof
versets.commentaire: This verse, says Qushayrī, is the testimony that the Real Himself bears to His Prophet on behalf of his faith—and that is more perfect for him than if he himself bore witness to his own faith. Then he brings out the pearl that distinguishes the degrees: creatures have all believed by way of inference (istidlāl), but you, O Muḥammad, have believed by direct vision (ʿiyān); all have believed through the mediation of intermediaries, and Muḥammad believed without intermediary. One faith in name, two abysses: all have believed by deduction, and you have believed by union (wiṣāl).

mots.racine: أ م ن / ر س ل
mots.def: āmana: to believe, to give assent; rasūl: messenger.

mots.racine: س م ع / ط و ع
mots.def: samiʿa: to hear; aṭāʿa: to obey.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ب ب
mots.def: ghufrān: forgiveness implored.

versets.ar: لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا ۚ لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْهَا مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ ۗ رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَا إِن نَّسِينَا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَا إِصْرًا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ ۖ وَاعْفُ عَنَّا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا ۚ أَنتَ مَوْلَانَا فَانصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: lā yukallifu llāhu nafsan illā wusʿahā lahā mā kasabat wa-ʿalayhā mā ktasabat rabbanā lā tuʾākhidhnā in nasīnā aw akhṭaʾnā rabbanā wa-lā taḥmil ʿalaynā iṣran kamā ḥamaltahu ʿalā lladhīna min qablinā rabbanā wa-lā tuḥammilnā mā lā ṭāqata lanā bihi wa-ʿfu ʿannā wa-ghfir lanā wa-rḥamnā anta mawlānā fa-nṣurnā ʿalā l-qawmi l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: God charges no soul with more than it can bear. It shall have what it has earned, and against it shall be what it has incurred. Our Lord! Do not take us to task if we forget or fall into error. Our Lord! Do not lay upon us a burden such as You laid upon those before us. Our Lord! Do not charge us with what we have no strength to bear. Pardon us, forgive us, and have mercy upon us! You are our Master, so grant us victory over the disbelieving people!
themes: the burden measured to the soul
the gentleness reserved for this community
the seal of the sura: the aid implored
versets.commentaire: Upon this final verse of the sura, Qushayrī sings the privileges of the community of Muḥammad. By the perfection of His mercy, He has held them below their capacity, and by far: all this is gentleness and favor from Him. He compares: the past nations addressed God through an intermediary—"O Moses, call upon your Lord for us"—but to this community He said: "Call upon Me, and I will answer you." And again: the ancients, when they sinned, had to await a term for their repentance to be received; but to this community the Prophet said: "remorse is repentance." He seals it at last upon mercy: when they said "do not lay upon us a burden," God swallowed up their sins instead of swallowing their bodies as He did the ancients, changed their evil deeds into good ones, and rained mercy upon them, where upon the first peoples He had rained down stones.

mots.ar: لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا
mots.tr: lā yukallifu llāhu nafsan illā wusʿahā
mots.racine: ك ل ف / و س ع
mots.sens: God charges no soul beyond its capacity
mots.def: kallafa: to burden with an obligation; wusʿ: capacity.

mots.racine: ح م ل / أ ص ر
mots.sens: do not lay upon us a burden
mots.def: iṣr: a crushing load, a heavy burden.

mots.racine: و ل ي / ن ص ر
mots.sens: You are our Master, so aid us
mots.def: mawlā: master, protector; naṣara: to aid.
